inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1905.)
Tirukkōyilūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Trivikramaperumāl temple.

Vijayanagara, Narasiṁhadēva, Śaka 1393, Vikṟiti, Aṟpaśi 13, Monday, trayōdaśi, Aśvati (irregular)
= 1470 A.D.

The details of date viz., are irregular as the star Aśvati cannot occur on the 13th tithi in the month
of Aippaśi.

This inscription refers to the collapse of the mukhamaṇḍapa, outer wall (tirumadiḷ), and the
gōpura of the Tiruviḍaikaḻināyanār temple at Tirukkōvalūr, in the Vaḻudilampaṭṭu uśavaḍi for several
Years due to the disturbances caused by the Oḍḍiyas (i.e. Oḍḍiya-kalabai) and the repairs to them carried
out by Aṉṉamarasagaḷ, the agent of Sāḷuva Narasiṁhadēva. It further records that the lands that once
belonged to the temple were restored and made tax-free and the services to the god revived by
Aṉṉamarasar. Almost similar copiesof this inscription are found at Nerkunam (ARE. No. 213 of 1935),
Arakandanallur (ARE. No. 11 of 1935), Siddalingamadam (ARE. No. 262 of 1937) etc.

l.ta:
paṭṭu ucāvaṭi tirukovalūr ṉāyaṉāṟ tiruviṭaikaḻināyaṉāṟ koyil tiruviṭai
āṭṭa ciṉṉamkaḷ

pattu eṭṭukālamāka puntatalaiyāy muya maṇṭa(ṭa)paṅkaḷ tirumatiḷ tirukkopuram
viḻuntu pokaiyil (naṭa) narasi
l.note:
This gap may be restored as
tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka

l.ta:
pala iṭa[t*]tilum naṭantuvarum cirmaikaḷum inta koyilukku naṭantuvarum rekai
coṭi viṉiyokamum piṟakka nilavu uṭpaṭa

[ra]paṭiyum tiruv[ā*]rātanamum tiruppaṇiyun tirunāḷu maṟṟu muṇṭāna
vaipokaṅkaḷum naṭatti [nara]si

l.note: These imprecatory verses are full of mistakes.
l.gr:
dānā sbrēyōtupālanam || dānāsvargamavāpnōti
pālanātaśvutam padam || svadattām pradattam vāyōharētivasandarām ṣaṣṭhi

l.ta: kaṅkai karaiyil kārāmpacuvai koṇṇa pāva[t*]tile poka kaṭavan [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1905.)
Tirukkōyilūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prakāra of the Trivikrama-perumāl temple.

This records the deed of exchange for several pieces of land (details given) by Taṇḍēsvaran of
Tiruvīraṭṭānamuḍaiya Nayinār temple at Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a district of Jananātha-
vaḻanādu alias Milāḍu, as kshētra parivattanai, to the deity Sēnāpati-Ālvān of Tiruviḍaikaḻi Emberumāṉ
temple at Tirukkōvalūr. Taṇḍēśvaraṉ also spelt as Chaṇḍēśvaraṉ and Sēnāpati Āḻvāṉ are evidently the
representative deities of the Śaivaite and Vaishnavaite pantheons. In enumerating the boundaries of the
land the record refers to Kaṇḍaraṅkuśan vāykkāl as also to two inscriptions “Nūṟṟukkala variśai-paṟṟu
kalveṭṭu” and “Kīḻvaḷai kalveṭṭu.”

l.ta:
vattikaḷ īḻamumaturaiyum pāṇṭiyan muṭittalaiyum
kacciyum karuvūruṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkacoḻa

ṉātavaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟukkaik kūṟṟattu tirukkovalūr uṭaiyār tiruviraṭṭāṉa
muṭaiyaṉāyaṉār koyil taṇṭecuraṉen ittāṉattāroṭum meva meṟppaṭiyūr
tiruviṭaikaḻi emperumān koyil [śrī]-

vaṭapāṟkellai tiruviṭaikaḻi emperumān tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam a-

marāṭa vallavan tirunantavaṉattukkum palavāyutavallavan tiruntavaṉattukkum
[ca]ṟṟuk kuṭātān vāykkālukkut teṟku kiḻpāṟkellai tirumañcaṉa oḻukkaikku
meṟkut teṉpāṟkellai [ci]ṟṟeri vāykkālāna

kaṇṭaraṅkucan vāykkālukku vaṭa[kku]m innānkellaik[ku*] uṭpaṭṭa

varicaippaṟṟu kalveṭṭuppaṭi ciṟupāṭikāval niṅkum mel vaḷai[ta]ṭi

kalavaricaippaṟṟut taṭipalaviṉāl kuḻi

kalavaricaippaṟṟu tiruvāykkeḻvippaṭi peṇṇai peruñceṟuttaṭi onṟu kuḻi

m āka palavaricai peṟṟa kuḻi rūpāy

tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam palavāyutavallavan tirunantavaṉattukkuk kiḻakkum
vaṭapāṟkellai peṇṇai[ā]ṟṟukku teṟkkuk kiḻpāṟkellai naraciṅka-

nokkiṉa maramum kiḻnokkiṉa kiṇaṟum uḷpaṭa viṭṭukuṭuttu koḷvatāṉa

koḷvatāṉa nilamāvatu tirupp[ā*]ṭalicuramuṭaiya nā[yaṉā*]r tevatāṉa tarattukkuk

vaṭakku kiḻpāṟkellaicu ••••• perumpaḷḷi

ccan[ta*]ttu[k*]ku meṟkum vaṭapāṟkellai tiruvi[ṭai*]kaḻi emperumān
tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam ciṟupulattukkum caṟṟukuṭātān vāykkālukku teṟkkum

tum iṅkuviṭṭu tiruvitik[kuk*] kiḻakku tirukkapālicu[ra*]muṭaiya nāyanār

pāṟkellai ciṟṟeri vāykkālukku kiḻakkum tenpāṟkellai ivvāy-

munṟināl kuḻi
ṟum itan meṟkkut taṭi onṟu kuḻi

melpāṟkellai kukan kuḻikaraikku kiḻakkum kiḻpāṟkellai toppu

rūpāy

kku nilantaṉam vāciēṟṟi tiruviṭaikaḻi emperumān tiruviṭaiyāṭṭattil

kku kiḻa[kku*]m tenpāṟkellai ciṟupulattuku vaṭa[kku*]m kiḻpāṟkellai
tirumātuṟai uṭaiyār tevatānattukku meṟkum vaṭapāṟkellai peṇṇai

parivattaṉaittiṭṭuk kuṭutteṉ tiruviṭaikaḻi emperumān koyilil cicenāpati
āḻvānukku uṭaiyār tiruviraṭṭāṉamuṭaiya nāyanār

•••rāḻampar koyil caṇṭecuranen ippaṭi cattirātittavarai cellakkaṭavatu ji
ācciñai[yeva] itu panmā

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records the gift of 96 sheep, by Karumāṇikkaṉdēvi, a servant-woman (peṇḍaṭṭi) of the king,
for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭāṉamuḍaiyār at Tirukkōvalūr in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam,
a sub-division of Jananātha-vaḻanādu alias Milāḍu a district of JayaṅgoṇḍaChōḻamaṇḍalam. Also
records the gift of a tarā-viḷakku, measuring 2 chāṉ, by the same donor.

edition.l: ḻadēvaṟki yāṇṭu [ 3 āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa] coḻama-

l.ta: ḷey iṭṭa [eṟumpukkāṇi] iruccāṇ uḷḷa tarā viḷakku-

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

Incomplete. Stops with mentioning the god Tiruvīraṭṭāṉamuḍaiya-Maha[deva*] of Tirukkōvalūr
in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a sub-division of Jananātha-vaḻanādu alias Milāḍu, a district of Jayaṅgoṇḍa-
chōḻamaṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

Damaged and incomplete. Begins with the praśasti commencing with “Tirumaṉṉivaḷara” etc.
Stops after mentioning Tirukkōvalūr in (Kuṟukkai)-kūṟṟam, a district of Jananātha-vaḻanādu alias Milāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records the gift of 192 sheep, by Milāḍuḍaiyān Rāman Mummuḍisōlan alias Rājendrasōla-
Milāḍuḍaiyān, for burning two perpetual lamps to the deity Tiruvīrattānamuḍaiya-mahādēva, at
Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a district of Jananātha-valanāḍu alias Milāḍu. Also records the gift
of 2 lamps each weighing 872 palams by the weighing scale veḷḷikkōl.

l.ta: nāṭun tuṭarvaṉavelippaṭarvaṉa vāciyuñ cuḷḷiccūḻmatiṭ koḷḷip pākkai-
yum naṇṇaṟ karumuraṇ maṇṇaikkaṭakkamum porukaṭal īḻattarai cartamuṭiyum
āṅkavar teviyar oṅkeḻil muṭiyum muṉṉavar pakkal teṉṉavaṉ vaitta
eṟipaṭaik keraḷaṉ muṟaimaiyiṟ cūṭuṅkulataṉamākiya palar pu-
nti maṟṟivaraṇ karuti iruttiya cempoṟṟiruttaku muṭiyum payaṅko-
m piṭiyil iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai ilakkamum navanetikulap perumalaikaḷu
āvatu malāṭāṉa jaṉanātavaḷanāṭṭu kuṟukkaik kūṟṟattut tiruk-
ṉāṉa rājentiracoḻa milāṭuṭaiyān vaitta tiruno[n]tāviḷakku iraṇṭuk-

ku vaitta cāvāmuvāp perāṭu nūṟṟu toṇṇūṟṟu iraṇṭu
ittirunontāviḷakku

niṉṟeriya ivvūr veḷḷikkolāl karuvuṭpaṭa viḷakku oṉṟu niṟai
eṇṇūṟṟu-

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.
[Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 104.]

The alphabet of the inscription closely resembles that of No. 8 engraved below this, which is one
of Rājēndrasōla I. It records the gift of 90 sheep and a lamp stand (īḻa-viḷakku) for burning a lamp in the
temple of Tiruvīraṭṭātanattu-Āṇḍār at Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a sub-division of Milāḍu by
Puliśayyaṉ Sāmi-abbai alias Malādamādēviyār, the wife of Vikramasōḻa-Milāḍuḍaiyār and the daughter
of the Pāṇḍiya King.

l.ta:
āvatu milāṭṭuk kuṟukkaik
kūṟṟattu tirukkovalūr tiruvi-

raṭṭāṉattu āṇṭārkku vikkiramacoḻa milāṭuṭaiyār teviyār pāṇṭiyaṉār
makaḷā[r*] puḷicayyaṉ cāmiappai āṉa malāṭamāte-

[vi]yār vaitta na[n*]tāviḷakku oṉṟiṉukku vaitta cāvāmuvāpper āṭu
[t]oṇṇū[ṟu*] īḻaviḷakku oṉṟu ivai

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records the gift of 96 sheep, by Maṭṭai Suṟṟiyaṉ alias Rājarājavānakōn a manṟāḍi, of
Vaḍaiyil-nāḍu, a division of Palūr-kūṟṟam, for burning a perpetual lamp to the god
Tiruvīraṭṭānamuḍaiyār at Tirukkovalur, in Kuṟukṭkai-kūṟṟam a district of Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu alias
Milāḍu, a sub-division of Jayanggoṇḍa-chōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
vaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟukkai kūṟṟattu tirukkovalūrt tiruviraṭṭā[na]muṭaiyāṟku
innāṭṭup palūrk kūṟṟattu [vi]ṭaiyil nāṭṭu nāṭṭuma-

nṟāṭi maṭṭaicūṟṟiyaṉāṉa rājarāja vāṇakon vaitta nuntāviḷakku oṉṟu[nu]kku
vaitta (noviḷa[k*] koṉṟiṉukku vaitta) cā

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This inscription begins with the praśasti commencing with ‘Tirumagaḷpōla’ etc. It records the
gift of 96 sheep, by a Kāḷi-Mataṅgan, brother of Kāḷi-Āchchan of Uḍuppur, in Vēsālippāḍi, for burning
a perpetual lamp during the Tiruppaḷḷikkaṭṭil and ardhayāma services in the temple of
Tiruvīraṭṭānamuḍaiyār at Tirukkōvalūr in Kuṟukkai-kuṟṟam, in Jananātha-valanāḍu alias Milāḍu.

l.ta: ṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamum iraṭṭapāṭi eḻaraiilakkamum [tiṇ]ṭiṟal veṉṟi

tu tirukkovalūrt tiruviraṭṭāna muṭ[aiyār tiru]paḷḷikkaṭṭil [tiruvattacāma]
ttukku vecālippāṭi

uṭuppūruṭaiya kāḷiyāc cantaṉ [ta]maiyaṉ [kāḷi]mataṅka[n eriya] vaitta ṉuntā
viḷakkoṉṟi nu-
kku āṭu toṇṇūṟṟāṟu cāvāmuvāp perāṭu ca

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This inscription begins with the prasasti commencing with ‘Tirumaṉṉivalara’ etc. It records the
gift of a vessel (taṭṭam), weighing 29(3/4) kaḻanjñu and 4 manñjāḍi by the kuḍinaikal, by Irāman
Maṟavaḍiyān alias Muḍikoṇḍasōla Milāḍuḍaiyān, for water offerings to the god Tiruvīraṭṭaṉam-uḍaiya-
mahādēva at Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, in Janaṉātha-vaḷanāḍu alais Milāḍu.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉivaḷara irunilamaṭantaiyun poṟceyap pāvaiyuñ
cirttaṉic celviyu

viyarāka iṉpuṟaneṭutiyalūḻiyuḷ

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This unifinished inscription begins with the prasasti commencing with Tirumaṉṉi-vaḷara etc. It
records the sale deed of 4+1 ma of tax-free land (boundaries specified) given by the members of the
Sabhā of Tirukkōvalūr, a brahmadēya village in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a district of Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu alias
Malāḍu to Kuravaśeri Madhāvas-sakhyāditthan, a member of the Āḷuṅganattār of Kaḍariyūṟṟu alias
Kalimalla-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, in Pāṉūr-kūṟṟam. Mādhavas-sakhyāditthan gave away the land for the
sacred bath, with 108 kalaśas, of god Mahādēva of Tiruvīraṭṭāṉam and for other related expenses on the
occasion of Uttaramayaṉa.

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: East wall of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This inscription begins with the praśasti commencing with Tirumagaḻ maruviyaseṅgōl vēndan
etc. Records the gift of 4 mā of land (boundaries specified) to the temple, by purchase from the ūrār of
Puduppēṟūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a district of Jananātha-vaḷaṇaḍu by Nārayanan Aranggaṉ alias
Sembiyan Chedi-vishaya Muvendavelan, a Kaṇiyar of Tirukkōvalūr, at the request of Kausikan Mallan
Amudan, a śivabrāhmaṇan of the temple, for conducting a festival during the month of Mārgaḻi, ending
with a recital of Tiruvembāvai on the Tiruvādirai day.

l.ta:
ṉ muṉnoṉ ceṉaipinnatuvāka
munnetiren ṟiraṭṭapāṭi eḻaraiilakkamuṅ koṇṭu kollāpurattu

virttu tannāṇai celutti etirppeṟā teṇṭicai nikaḻap paṟaiyatu kaṟaṅkap
perāṟṟaṅkaraik koppattu va

keṭṭaruḷi

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records the gift of 100 sheep by Irāman Mummuḍisōlar, for burning a perpetual lamp on
Uttaramayanana-Saṅkrānti to the deity Tiruvīraṭṭāṇam-Uḍaiya Mahādēva at Tirukkōvalūr in Kuṟukkai-
kūṟṟam, a district of Jananāthavaḷanāḍu alias Milāḍu, for the merit of his mother Rājaśēkharaṉ Umai-
Naṅgaiyār alias Milāḍa-mādēviyār, daughter of Munṇṉaiyavaraiyar and wife of Paṭṭālakaṉ Irāman
alias Arumoḻideva-Milāḍudaiyār. Also records the gift of lamp, weighing 210 palam, by the weighing
scale velḷḻikkōl of Tirukkōvalūr and a white metal vessel weighing 40 palams by the same kōl for offering
water to the god.

l.ta: milā[ṭā]ṉa jaṉanāta vaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟukkaik kūṟṟattut tirukkovalūr tiruvīra-
ḷakku oṉṟukku vaiytta cāvāmuvāp perāṭu nūṟum ittiruna

ṟpatiṉ palattāl vai[tta pi]ttaḷaik karakakkāl oṉṟum ivai

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa a in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This incomplete inscription stops with citing the prasasti commencing with Tirumaṉṉi-vaḷara
etc. of Rājēndra Chōḻa I

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉivaḷara irunilamaṭantaiyum poṟceyappāvaiyum cirttaṉic
celviyum taṉperu

viyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭutuyilūḻiyu ḷiṭaituṟai nāṭun
tuṭarva[ṉa*] velip paṭarvaṉavāciyum cu[ḷ*]ḷic cūḻmatiṭ koḷḷi-

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records the gift of 15 Kaḻañju of gold by Mallaṉ-Aṇṇāvaṉ, a resident of Vayiramēgha-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Uḍaikkāḍu-nāḍu for burning a perpetual lamp to the deity Tiruvīraṭṭānatt-āḷvar of
Tirukkōvalūr. The gift money was left in the hands of the nagarattār of Tirukkōvalūr, who agreed to
maintain the lamp with one uḻakku of ghee daily.

l.ta: ṉ patiṉaiṅkaḻañcu ippoṉ koṇṭu nicatam uḻakku ney

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records a renewal of a gift of land by Vaidumba-mahārāja Sri Vikramāditya ruling over
Malāḍu, Vaṇakōppāḍi, Siṅgapura-nāḍu and Veṇkuṉra-kkōṭṭam, which was made previously by
Sembiyan Milāḍudaiyān alias Siddhavaḍavan Kollai tulaṅgōṉ who died at Madurai (Madurait-tuñjina) for
several food offerings to the deity Tiruvīraṭṭā[ṉam-uḍaiyār] at Tirukkōvalūr in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam and also
for expenses of the devotees during festival days.

l.ta:
ñcaiyuṅ ko ••••• [kaṉ*]ṉara tevaṟku ••••• ṭṭuk kuṟukkaik
kūṟṟattut tirukkovalūrt tiruviraṭṭā

ṟṟukku [pā]tiyum •••nilam iṟaṅkik kiṭantamaiyil immalāṭum
vāṇakoppāṭiyum ciṅkapuranāṭum veṇkuṉṟakkoṭṭamum

mi iṟaṅkik kiṭantamai
keṭṭu ārā[y]ntu iṟaṅkal nikki veḷipaṭuttu muṉ

ṉa maturait tuñciṉa cempiyaṉ milāṭuṭaiyāṉākiya cittavaṭavaṉ kollai
tulaṅkoṉ ceyta [ni]vaṉtapaṭiye kuṭuttāṉ itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This begins with the praśasti commencing with Tirumagaḻpōla etc. and records the gift of 96
sheep by Ayyāṟan Irāman alias Kavaṟa-śeṭṭi, of Kuṟukkaipāḍi, in Vembākkuṟṟam, a district of
Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu for burning a perpetual lamp to the deity Vīrasthānam-uḍaiya-mahādēva at
Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a district of Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu alias Malāḍu, a subdivision of
Jayaṅgoṇḍasōla-maṇḍalam alias Toṇḍai-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
ḷūrccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅkapāṭiyu nuḷampapāṭiyun
taṭikaipā-

ṭiyuṅ kuṭamalai nāṭuṅ kollamuṅ kaliṅkamum eṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamum
iraṭ-

vaḷar ūḻiyuḷellā yā-

kecaripa

tirukkovalūr śrīvira-

kuṟukkaipāṭiyuṭaiya ayyā-
ṟaṉ irāmaṉnāṉa kavaṟa ceṭṭi vaitta na

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

Incomplete. Stops after citing the praśasti commencing with Pūmaruviya tiśai mugattōṉ etc., of
a king whose name is lost. cf. SII. Vol. VII = No. 890.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya ticaimukattoṉ paṭaitta perumpuvi viḷaṅka temaruviya
pacuntuḷavattiru neṭu[mā] livaneṉna

[va]raiyāḻi neṭumpuṟattuḷorāḻi taṉi naṭātti uṭaiya kulattavatarittuk
karuṅkaliyiṉniruḷotuṅkak katir veṇku[ṭ]ai nilāveṟippa varuṅkatirin
veyil

virikkum maṇimakuṭam puṉaintaruḷi maṇkaḷippavum maṉaṅkaḷippavu malar maṭantaiyan
mutal maṅkaiyar kaṇkaḷippavun taṇṇaḷip peruṅkaruṇaiyāl mutaṟkāval pūṇ-

ṭu cempon viraciṅṅācaṉattup puvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya
koirājakesari

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 18-A of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This inscription records the gift of 32 cows for a perpetual lamp for god Tiruvīraṭṭaṉam-uḍaiyār
by Pālūr Vāṇarāyaṉ Vēmbaṉ Sōḻaṉ alias Rājādhirāja-nāḍuḍaiyān. The donor appears to have belonged to
Uḍaikkāḍu-nāḍu on the northern bank of Peṇṇai, in Vāṇagappāḍi alias Madirāntaka-vaḷanāḍu. The
record is engraved in characters of about the 11th century.

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

The king may be identified with Kulōttuṅga I (1070-1120 A.D.) on grounds of palaeography. This
records the gift of 7 kaḻanñju of gold by Āḍavallān and Sūtta •••valan (?), devaradiyar (temple servants)
of the deity Tiruvīraṭṭānamuḍaiya-mahādēva at Tirukkōvalūr for the provision of food offerings and
ornaments such as tirukkāppu, tiruttāli for the god and goddess in the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭānam-uḍaiya-
mahādēva on the fifth day of chittirai festival at Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a district of
Jananāthavaḷaāḍu, alias Milāḍu. The gift money was left in the hands of the Śivabrāhmaṇa members of
the Tiruvuṇṇāḻigai sabhā of the temple, who agreed to maintain the services with kalam of paddy by the
measure Tiruvīraṭṭāṉa-marakkāl.

l.ta:
āvatu milāṭāna jananāta vaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟukkaik kūṟṟattu
tirukkovalūr tiruviraṭṭānamuṭaiya

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: Narth wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This is dated in the 4th Year of the reign of Parakēśarivarmaṉ alias Rājēndra Sōḻadēva who may be
identified with Rājēndra I (of acc. 1012 A.D.) on grounds of palaeography and on account of the title
Uttamasōḻa-Milāḍuḍaiyar of the donor, being taken after the King's title Uttamasōḻa.

It records the gift of 1(1/2) vēli and 3 mā of land by purchase from the sabhā of Tirukkōvalūr by
Pirāntakaṉ Yādava Bhīmaṉ alias Uttamasōḻa-Milāḍudaiyar of Bhārggavagōtra at the instance of Āviyūr-
uḍaiya Araiyaṉ Malaiyar-Ādittaṉ alias Chēdi-nāḍu vēlāṉ, for daily food offering (akkār-aḍalai) at noon
to god Tiruvīraṭṭāṉam-uḍaiya mahādēva at Tirukkōvalūr. The donor gave also 280 sheep to provide with
milk and ghee for the said food offering. The land and the sheep were entrusted to the members of the
tiruvuṇṇāḻigai-sabhai of the temple. The record mentons Taittiriya-vaḷai as the name of a plot of land.

l.ta:
ṟṟuk kuṭutta nilamāvitu i[v*]vūr kiḻvāyp pulattu kovalūrk kuṟai nilam
orumāvum atti eḻumā-

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēsari Rajendra, Kulōthuṅga I Year 2 = 1072 A.D.

inscription.p:
This contains the praśasti ‘Bhūmiyum Tiruvum’. This records the gift of 16 sheep by
Madhurāntaka nāḍāḷvān of Nerkuppai, in Vilaiyūr-nāḍu on the north bank of Peṇṇai, for burning half a
lamp in the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭāṉam-uḍaiyār at Tirukkōvalūr, in Ku[ṟukkai-kūṟṟam], a district of
Jananātha vaḷanāḍu, for the merit of his paternal uncle (siṟṟappaṉ) Upakārimallaṉ who is stated to have
died as a result of some accident (pramādam), at the hands of Parukkaluḍaiyān Piritiveṇkāḍaṉ. The
members of the tiruvuṇṇāḻigai-sabhai, agreed to maintain the gift.

l.ta: pūmiyum tiruvum tāme puṇarntu vikkiramattāl cakkara naṭātti vi-
vaṟku yāṇṭu iraṇṭāvatu jananāta vaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟu •••••

ruk kovalūr tiruviraṭṭāna uṭaiyār koyililey peṇṇai vaṭakarai vilaiyūr
nāṭṭu neṟkuppai irukkum nelkuppai uṭaiyānāna ko • • •

ka nāṭāḻvānen eṉ ciṟṟappaṉ upakāri mallaṉ parukkaluṭaiyān
pirativeṇkāṭan kaiyiley piramā[ta*]ttāl paṭṭamaiyi •••••

cārtti vaitta viḷakku arai araikkum vaitta cāvāmuvā naṟpacu patināṟu ivai
kaikkoṇṭu nima

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: North wall of the maṇḍapa (right of entrance) in front of the central shrine in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records the gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold, by Vīranaraṇiyār, the mistress (Bhōgiyār) of Sri
Vikramādityaṉ, for a perpetual lamp to the deity Tiruvīraṭṭānattāḷvār of Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-
kūṟṟam, a district of Malāḍu. The gold was left in the hands of the Nagarattār of Tirukkōvalūr, who
agreed to maintain the gift, with one uḻakku of oil daily.

l.ta: raiyaṉ viranāraṇiyār vacca viḷakku onṟukku pon pa[ti]

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: North wall of the gōpura in front of the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Vijayarāya Mahārāya, Saka 1368, Kshaya, Tulā śu. 10, Aviṭṭam, Friday = 1446
A.D., September 30.

inscription.p:
It states that when the members of the Idaṅgai and Valaṅgai classes were oppressed severely by
the pradhānās who demanded kāṇikkai tax every Year they migrated from their homes leading to
cessation of worship and other calamities and that Aṇṇappa-uḍaiyār got a royal order to the effect that the
officials should not collect iṉavari and idaṅgaivari from the community. It is further stated that since the
order was engraved only in some places, a fresh order from Nagarasa uḍaiyār was secured. There is a
copy of this inscription at Tiruveṇṇainallūr. (See ARE. No. 476 of 1921.)

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: Slab detached from the temple and placed near the gōpura in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records the gift of 40 kaḻañju of gold by the Ūrkkaṟ-semmai, left in the hands of the
members of the Śabhai of Attaḷam-poḷḷil-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a division of Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam. The
members of the Śabha agreed to measure out 40 kalam of paddy (at the rate of 1 kalam per kaḻañju) to the
treasury (bhaṇḍāra) of Tiruvīraṭṭānattāḷvār temple at Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a district of
Malāḍu. The purpose of the gift is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1905.)
Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p:
Slab detached from the temple and placed near the gōpura
Of the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

Incomplete. Stops after mentioning the deity Tiruvīraṭṭānattāḷvar, at Tirukkovalur, in Kuṟukkai-
kūṟṟam, a district of Malāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1905.)
Aragaṇḍanallūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the Aḻagiya-Poṉṉi-Ammaṉ temple.

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Mallikārjuna, Śaka 1378, Dhātu, Kārttigai 25, Hasta = 1456 A.D.
November, 22, Monday.

inscription.p:
This records the sale of the village of Perichchāḷinallūr on the north bank of Peṇṇai as
tirunāmattukkāṇi to god Opporuvarum-illāda-nayiṉār of Tiruvaṟaiyaṇinallūr in Uḻai(ḍai)kkāḍu-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Koṟukkai-kūṟṟam in Jananātha vaḷanāḍu alias Malāḍu, for 200 paṇam by Āviyūr
Manaṟppaṅgiḻār Gāṅgaiyar and some other persons. The village is stated to be their kāṇi-āṭchi.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalīcuraṉ aṟiya irāyavipāṭaṉ mūvarāyar
kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭu kuṭātāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1905.)
Tiruppālappandal, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākāra of the Madhyasthānēśvara temple.

p.b: Vijayanagara, Harihara II Śaka 1300 Kālayukti, Karkaṭaka śu. 13, Thursday, Mūla = 1378, July 8.

inscription.p:
This records the sale-deed of land called puḷiyāṇḍai tiurthai (boundaries specified) for 100
Virachampan kuḷigai, by the authorities of the temple of Tirunāgiśvaramuḍaiya nāyaṉār at
Tiruppālaippandal, in Kīḻ[ko]ṇḍa nāḍu of Vānagōppāḍi, on the southern bank of Peṇṇai, to
Tiruvambalapperumāl, a madhyastha of Vaḍapēṟu, a southern hamlet of Rājarāja-chaturvēdimaṅgalam,
a division of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. The land is stated to have been formerly purchased from
Kulaśēkharadēva alias Māḷuva-chakravarti.

There are two endorsemnts dated in the cyclic Years Yuva, Mārgaḻi 5 and Pramādi, Pūraṭṭādi 15,
recording further transactions pertaining to this land made by Cheyya Tirumēni of Vaḍapēṟai, son (?) of
Tiruvambala-pperumāl of Vaḍapēṟu and in this connection the sum of 80 vīrachampan kuḻigai is
mentioned. The dates mentioned above correrspond respectively to 1395 A.D., December 2 and 1399
A.D., September 13.

l.ta: mel ariyappa uṭaiyāṟku cellā niṉṟa kālayu

yum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa mulattunāḷ peṇṇait teṉkarai
vāṇakoppāṭik kiḻ[ko]ṇṭa nāṭṭu

muṉṉāḷ kulacekaratevarāṉa māḷuvaccakkaravatti

pakkal koṇṭuṭaiya puḷiyāṇṭai tiruttai cūḻnta nilattukku ellaiyāvatu
kiḻpāṟkellai muṭiyaṉūr ellaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai

uṭaiyār ental kaḻaṉikku vaṭakkum pokiṟa ōṭai uṭpaṭa vaṭakkum itaṉmeṟku
teṉpāṟkelai vārap paṟṟukku oṭaiyuṭpaṭa vaṭakkum itaṉ me-

ṟku teṉpāṟkellai paṭṭukkaṭṭi ental kaḻaṉikku vaṭakku oṭaiyuṭpaṭavum
itaṉmeṟku paṭṭukkaṭṭi ental vaṭakoṭiyil pāṟaikku vaṭakku itaṉmeṟku

intap pāṟaic cevvaikku vaṭakkum melpāṟkkellaik kuṭiyellaiyāṉa
paccāṟuṭaiyāṉ ental kaḻaṉikku kiḻakkum itaṉ vaṭapāṟkellait taṭi-

yellaikku teṟkum itaṉ kiḻakku muṭiyaṉūrellaikkut teṟku[mi]
ṉāṉkellaikkuḷpaṭṭa nilattukkuḷḷut taṭikkeṟa nirukku eruvaiyāṉa nila-

ttile tammuṭaiya perāle entalum veṭṭi pereri nirukku e[ru]vaiyāṉa
iṭaṅkaḷile vāykkālum veṭṭi koṇṭu kā-

ca

viracampaṉ kuḷikai

pārvaiyāka orukuḻippāṭip paṟṟikkoṇṭu viṟṟu vilaippiṟamāṇam

titittavaraiyuñ cella naṭattuk koḷḷavum ippaṭikku ivai immaṇṭa-

l.ta:
muṭiyaṉūr mutaliyār nāyiṉār muṭiyaṉūr

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1905.)
Tiruppālappandal, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: South wall of the first prākārā of the Madhyasthanēśvara temple.

p.b: Pāndya, Sundarapāndya Year 14 = c. 14th century A.D.

inscription.p:
This records the gift of 85 paṇam, realized from the sale of (the right of) Vaṇṇārakkāṇi, to
Periyanāṭṭu-Vaṇṇāṉ (washerman) of Aṟamana, by auction, by the Śrī Māhēśvara of the temple of
Tirunāgīśvaram-uḍaiyār, the local authorities, the inhabitants and Kaikkōlas is of Tiruppālaippandal.
The gift amount was utilized for the construction (tiruppaṇi) of the Magadai-nāṭṭān-tirumaṇḍapa in the
temple.

l.ta: yil ivaṉukku viṟṟu tirupaṇikku iṭṭa paṇam

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1905.)
Tiruppālappandal, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: Rock named Vavvākuṭṭai-malai near the village.

It is damaged. It seems to record a gift of land made tax-free. Maṅgalam uḍaiyāṉ Toṇḍaiyāḻvān
figures as a signatory.

p.i: Line 6 to 14 are very much damaged.

l.ta:
ku meṟkum āka ivvellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nancai puncaikku ūrviṉiyokam
āḷamañci [meṟ]

ce •••kaḻittu tamakkum tamvarkkattārukkum cantirātittavaraiyum celvatāka
veṭṭik ku-

ṭuttom ippaṭikkut tāṉattārkaḷaṉaivom• akara[m]maṅkala muṭaiyān
toṇṭaiyāḷvāṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1905.)
Tiṇḍivaṉam, Tindivanam Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: Gōpura of the Tinṭriṇśvara temple, left of entrance.

p.b: Vijayanagara, Śrīranga I, Saka 1505, Subhānu, Vriśchika śu. 15 = 1583 November 20.

inscription.p:
The king Srirangayadēvamahārāya, son of Tirumalaidēva-mahārāya is described to have
extracted tribute from Ceylon and to have reduced the troublesome Kaḷḷar and Maṟavar in Koṅgu and
Malaināḍu and also to have bounteously given away elephants and pearls.

This records the gift of the proceeds (magamai) from the tolls levied on articles like paddy, sesamum,
cotton (loose and spun), jaggery, cocoanuts etc., at stipulated rates per pack-load or head-load as the
case may be, which are brought for sale in the (weekly) market fair on every Wednesday at Kiḍaṅgil.
The gift was made by Śiṉṉa-Vīrappanāyakarayyaṉ, son of Pāppu-nāyakkarayyan of Vēlūr, who was the
karttar of Kiḍaṇgil-paṟṟu and also by the heads of the merchant community called Āyirattu toḷḷāyira-
mudaligal, the Anbattāru dēśam and patineṇbūmi of Tiṇḍivaṉam, Pēṭṭai etc., for the tiruppaṇi (repairs)
to the temple of Tiṇḍīśvaramuḍaiya Nāyiṉār, at Tiṇḍivaṉam, in Kiḍaṅgil-Paṟṟu, a sub-division of
Paḍaivīḍu-rājya.

l.ta:
muvarāyakaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭa nāṭukoṭātāṉ tulukkataḷa
vipāṭaṉ tulukka-

mokaran tavittāṉ emmaṇṭalamum yiḻamu tiraikoṇṭa rācātirācaṉ
rācaparamecuraṉ pūṟuvate-

kṣaṇa paccima uttiracatucamuttirātipatiyum koṅku malaināṭu aṅkuḷḷa kaḷḷar
maṟavar kuṟumpaṟuttu veḻamum

muttum vārivaḻaṅkum śrīvirapiṟatāpaṉ śrītirumalaitevamakārāyar kumārar
śrīraṅkayatevamakārāyaṉ piṟutivi rā-

ṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu pañ[ca]miyum comav[ā*]ramum rohaṇi nakṣattaramum peṟṟa
nāḷ cantirakiri rācciyam mutalākiya pa-

ṭaiviṭṭu rācciyattil kiṭaṅkil paṟṟi[l*] tiṇṭivaṉam tiṇṭicuramu^1^ṭaiyārkku
iṉṉā-

ṭṭukku kaṟtarākiya velū[r*] pāppu nāyakkarayyaṉ kumārar ciṉṉavirappa
nāyakkarayyaṉum āyirattu toḷ[ḷā*]

yira mutalikaḷil talaivarānavarkaḷum tiṇṭivaṉam peṭṭai mutalākiya aṉpattāṟu
tecamum patiṉeṉpūmiyuḷḷa palarum

tiṇṭicuramuṭaiya nayiṉār koyil tiruppaṇikku makamai kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭapaṭi [|]
keṭaṅkil putaṉ kiḻamaic can[t*]aiyil vaitta ka-

ṭai oṉṟukku kācu araiyu[m*] nellup potikku kācu oṉṟum eḷḷup potikku
kācuraṇṭum pañcup potikku kācu

raṇṭum itil mārppu talaiccumaikku kācu oṉṟum vellap potikku kācuraṇṭum
teṅkāyp potikku kācu oṉ-

ṟum paḻap potikkum kācu oṉṟum pākku miḷaku potikkum kācuraṇṭum uppu
potikku kācu araiyum karuvā-

ṭṭup potikkuk kācuraṇṭum piṭa[vai]māṟumulaikku paccavaṭam añcukku kācu
oṉṟum [pa]ñcuttārukku

oṉṟukku paṇam oṉṟum [kappal]ccarakkukku paṇam pattukku kācu raṇṭum intap
paṭi makamai kaṭṭa[ḷai]yi-

ṭṭom inta makamaiyil utaiyam koṇṭu tiṇṭicuramuṭaiya nayiṉār koyil
tiruppaṇi naṭattak kaṭavatāka

vum inta taṟmam ācantirāṟkamāka paripālaṉam paṇṇi naṭatti[na]perkaḷ
tevapatam coṟkapatam peṟakkaṭa-

varākavum itukku yātāmoruvar akitam paṇṇiṉāl keṅkai karaiyile
ka[vi]laip pacukkaḷaiyum tan-
ṉiṭa mātāpitāvaiyum taṉkaiyālekoṉṟa pāvattile poka kaṭavarākavum §

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1905.)
Tiṇḍivaṉam, Tindivanam Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: Gōpura of the Tinṭrinīśvara temple, left of entrance.

p.b: Vijayanagara, Venkata II, Saka 1525 Panguni 2, śu 7, Monday, Mrigasirsha = 1603, March 9.

inscription.p:
This records the order of Bommu-nāyakkar, the agent of Nāgama-nāyakkarayyan, the Śrīkāryam
officer of the Tiṇḍīśvra temple, the sthānamāhēśvaras (trustees), Palapaṭṭaḍai Śēḍar, the Śeṭṭis, peasants
and other elders to the Kaikkōlamudalis residing at the maḍavilāgam of the temple to the effect that they
should pay as vechcha-ilāḍam, kāṇikkai, and kaṭṭāyam, (1/2) paṇam per loom, per month and also do
service in the temple.

l.ta: kku mel cellāṉiṉṟa copakiṟuti varuṣampaṅkuṉi mātam

kāriyañ cey[yu][m*] nākamaṉāyakkarayyaṉ kāriyattukku kaṟttarāṉa
pommu-
l maṭa[vi]ḷākam kaikoḷa mutalikaḷukku kaṭṭaḷai iṭṭapaṭi ṉāḷḷatu mutal ve-

ccavilāṭam kāṇikkai kaṭṭāyam uḷpaṭa mātam oṉṟukku taṟi oṉṟukku
araippaṇam
viḻukkāṭu koyilukku iṟuttu koyil ūḻiyamum ceytu koṇṭu cukattile
itukku yātāmoruvar atikam paṇṇiṉa perkaḷ keṅkai karaiyile kāram

pacuvaik koṉṟa toṣattile poka kaṭavarākavum ituvum taṅkaḷ tāyār
toppaṉār
taṉṉuṭa kaiyālek koṉṟa toṣattile pokak kaṭavarākavum §

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1905.)
Tiṇḍivaṉam, Tindivanam Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the maṇḍapa of the Lakshminarasiṁḥa Perumāl temple, right side

It seems to record the gift of kuḻi of land as tirumēni-kaval, by the trustees and Mallappaṉ, son
of Pradhāni Tipparasan, for the God Narasiṅgamūrti.

l.ta:
kāmaṇṭalicura metiṉi[mi]curakaṇṭa kaṭṭā[ri] cāḷuva ariya
irāya vipāṭaṉ pū

śrīmatu tiṇṭivaṉam perumāḷ naraciṅkamuṟti koyil tāṉattārum piṟatāṉit
tirupparacar kumārar mallapparum meṟpaṭi •••••

kiṭaṅkil ēri etuvāyp piṟavaṭaiyil vāḻavantāṉ piṟavaṭaiyil tirumeṉi
kāvalukkuviṭṭa ku

k koṇṭu tirumeṉi kāvaluṇṭāṉatu kāttu uttaram collak kaṭaivarākavum
tiṇṭicuramuṭaiya ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1905.)
Tiṇḍivaṉam, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the maṇḍapa of the Lakshminarasimha Perumāḷ Temple, left side

p.b:
Vijayanagar, Sadāśivarāya, Śaka 1464, Kīlaka, Dhanus, Apara 14, Friday = 1548,
December 28, Friday.

inscription.p:
Records the gift of a village for the merit of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja Chiṉṉa-
Timmaiyadēva Mahārāja by Meypōkayarāya-nāyakkar. The village Veṇmaṇayāṟṟūr was within Oḻugaṟai,
a māgaṇi-sīrmai of the latter.

l.ta: ttirātipati śrīcatācivaṭevamakārāyar piṟitivirācciyam paṇṇiyaruḷā niṉṟa cakā-
ṉmel cellāniṉṟa kilaka varuṣam taṉu nāyaṟṟu amarapa-

veṇmaṇa yāṟṟūr tiṇṭivaṉam nāraciṅkamūrttikku tārāpūṟuvamāka kuṭuttom
atukku
ntiviḷakku aṉpatum rāttiri naivvettiya veḷaiyile pattu kaccāyam

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1905.)
Tiṇḍivaṉam, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa of the Lakshminarasimha Perumāl Temple.

p.b:
Śaka 1439, Īśvara, Makara, Paurṇami, Punarpūsha, Monday, the Sankrānti day = 1518,
January 25, Monday.

inscription.p:
Mentions a gift of land, measuring 300 kuḻi, in his sīrmai by Perugu Appalaiyaṉ of Śrīvatsa
gōtra to the deity Lakshminarasimhamūrtti, for the provisions of curd rice, vegetable offerings, and
sandal past praying for the merit of the nāyaka chief Tirumalai Nāyaka, who was the patron of the donor.
Related to the next inscription (35 of 1905).

l.ta:
tavākkiya piramāṇikarāy tiru[mura]nāyakar tirumalai nāyakkar eṉakku muṉṉāḷ
comakkireṇa puṇṇiya kālattile kiṭaṅkilēri kiḻakku ṉa[t*]ta nilattil
vatikku mēṟkkum • • •

kuḻikku teṉkiḻakku piḷḷaiyārmāniyattukku vaṭakkum meṭṭukku teṟku inta
cīrmaikku viṭṭa kuḻi

yotaṉamum varuṣattukku iraṇṭu paṇam cantaṇakkāppum kaṟiamutu
erikarumpukkumāka oru paṇamum āka tirumalaināyakkaṟku taṟmamāka oru canti
naṭattikka[kaṭa]-

nāraciṅkamūṟtikku utakam
paṇṇicilālihitam paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ eṉakku nittiyamum nānāḻi a-

pāramparaiyāka naṭattakkaṭavarākavum
niṉṟa aṟunāḻi aricipiṟacātamum tāṉattār

taṅkaḷ niṟuvākattile pakuntukoḷḷakkaṭavarākavum ippaṭikku utakampaṇṇi
taṟmacātaṉam paṇṇikkuṭuttōm inta taṟmattukku

yātoruvar akitam niṉaittāl keṅkaikaraiyil kārām pacuvaikkoṉṟa
pāvattilepokak kaṭavarākavum oru tiruviḷakkum nittam iṭakka-

ṭavarākavum ippaṭikku taṟmacātanam kuṭutteṉ nāraciṅkammuṟtikku
appalayaneṉ

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 35 of 1905.)
Tiṇḍivanam, Tiṇḍivanam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa of the Lakshminarasimha Perumāl Temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of 300 kuḻi of land irrigated by the tank Kiḍaṅkil-ēri, by Tirumalai-nāyaka, to Appili of
Śrīvatsa gōtra, son of Muttarasar on the auspicious day of lunar eclipse. The land was in Tiṇḍivanam-
sīrmai which was the donor's māgaṇi-sīrmai

l.gr:
śubhamastu svastiśrīmadrājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara pūrvva dakṣiṇa paścima
samudrādhiśvara bhujabala parākrama sampanna

l.ta:
nammuṭaiya mākāṇic cīmaiyāṉa
tiṇṭivaṉac cīrmai ki[ṭa]ṅkilil Eri kīḻE nañcai nilatti-
inta nañcai nilattilē kuḻi muṉṉūṟum inta

kamākak kuṭuttOm āṉapaṭiyālE inta nañcainilam ku[ḻi]
muṉṉū[ṟu*]

vittukkoḷḷak kaṭavarākavum itukku arinīr koṭinīr
niyamaṟṟu matikāri^1^ peṟakkaṭavarākavum inta

ṇṇ[ā]tapaṭikku inta nilattil nālu mūlaiyum kallu veṭṭi
nāṭṭikkuṭuttōm inta

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the slab set up in a field near the village at Perumukkal.

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Vēngaṭapathirāyar Śaka 1513, Virodhi, Makara, Purva 10, Monday,
Uttirāḍam (irregular) = 1589 A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of some garden land with well and tank by the watchman (kāval) Vēṅkaṭappa-nāyaka for the
merit of Timmakkāḷ of the ‘palace’ (araimaṉaiyār) at the order of Bommu-nāyaka, son of Nagama-
nāyaka. Koṇḍama-nāyaka, son of Krishṇappa-nāyaka is also mentioned. Trees like mango, areca,
coconut, lemon, berry and other crops were permitted to be cultivated in the garden.

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the rock to the right of the way up to the hill in Perumukkal.
inscription.p:
Records a list of gifts of cows, and sheep for perpetual and sandhi lamps in the 16th, 43rd and
45th Years of the king, respectively by Rājēntirachōḻa-brammarāyar, Kiḍāraṅkoṇḍa-chōḻa-Milāḍarāyan
and Araichūr-uḍaiyān Vēḷān Tiruvaraṅkamuḍaiyān, and Nuḷampuḻān Āṉai-choṭṭaiyāḻvān Chēvūr-nāṭṭu-
vēḷān.

l.ta: pukaḻmātu viḷaṅkaccayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭicūṭi minavarnilaikeṭa villavar kulai [tara ēnaimannavar]

ṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟirun taruḷiya kovirācakecari parmarāna tiripuvanac
cakkaravattikaḷ siri-

kulottuṅka coḻatevarkku iyāṇṭu patināṟāvatil oymānāṭāna
vicaiyarāce-

rāyar viṭṭa tirununtāviḷakku onṟināṟ cāvāmūvāpacu muppatum
ittevaṟku yāṇṭu

nāṟpattumunṟāvatu coḻamaṇṭalattu paṇṭāḻacceri uṭaiyān veḷān
tirucciṟṟa-

mpala muṭaiyāṉāna kiṭāraṅkoṇṭa coḻamilāṭarāyan it
tiruvānmi

māhā tevaṟku viṭṭa tirununtāviḷak konṟināṟ cāvāmuvāp pacu muppatum
perumpan[tū]r nā-

ṭṭu araicūruṭaiyān veḷān tiruvaraṅkamuṭaiyān ittevaṟku viṭṭa tirununtā
viḷakku
onṟināṟ cāvāmuvāp pacu muppatum śrīkulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu

anapanāyakan viṭṭa cantiviḷakku onṟināṟ cāvāmuvāpperāṭu panniraṇṭum
śrīkulottuṅka-

coṭṭaiyāḻvānāna ce-

rammuṭaiya mātevaṟku viṭṭa
cantiviḷakku onṟināṟ cāvāmuvā

p perāṭu panniraṇṭum iccanti viḷakkiraṇṭum tirununtā viḷakku munṟum ivai
kaikkoṇṭu cantirātit-

tirucciṟṟampalapaṭṭan

ampalaṅkoyilkoṇṭānum āttiraiyan pañcaneti aṟivāḷapaṭṭanum
āttiraiyan ulakan kaṇavatipaṭṭanum

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On a slab to the left of the same way up to the hill in Perumukkal
inscription.p:
In Kannaḍa language. Records that the steps were made by Liṅgappa-nāyaka, son of Chikka-
Kōnēri-nāyaka of Paḍavīḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Mukhyāchalēśvara temple on the hill
inscription.p:
Records a gift of 32 cows and a bull, for a lamp to the deity Tiruvānmīśvaram-uḍaiyār on the top
of the hill at Perumukkil by Kūttu-kantāḷ, daughter of Nachchi, belonging to the parigraham of Kūṟṟūr
in Tiruviḍaikaḻi-nāḍu. Perumukkil alias Gaṅkaikoṇḍachōḻa-nallūr was included in Ōymā-nāḍu alias
Vijaiyarājēndra-vaḷanāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: kaṭal cūḻnta pārmātaruñ cirmātarum vantu vāḻka nāṟ-
kaṭalcūḻ puviyeḻum pāṟkaṭal poṟ pukaḻparappi āti-

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Mukhyāchalēśvara temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Records the construction of the temple, along with the maḍaviḷāgam and flower garden, by
Perumaṅgalam-uḍaiyāṉ alias Periyāṉ Tiruvaṉ for the merit of Araiyaṉ Kākkunāyakaṉalias Kaṉakarāyaṉ
of Kōṉūr in Poyyiṟ-kūṟṟam in Rājādhirāja-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Mukhyāchalēśvara temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Gift of land measuring 500 kuḻi as measured by 12-foot rod, to the temple of Tiruvānmikai-
īśvaram-uḍaiyār at Perumukkil alias Gaṅgaikoṇḍachōḻa-nallūr, as endowment for lamp service
(tiruviḷakkuppuṟam) by one Tiraiyan Mādēvaḍikaḷ Gaṅgaikoṇḍāṉ alias Rājādhirāja-vaḷavataraiyaṉ of
Cheyyūr alias Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-nallūr in Puṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Chembūr-kōṭṭam. The land was purchased by
the donor from the ūrār of Perumukkil in public auction.

l.ta:
[ṭu] perumukkilāṉa kaṅkaikoṇṭāṉallūr ūrom eṅkaḷur nirnilattil
naṭuviṟ-
tūmpiṉiṉṟun teṟku nokkippoṉa vāykkālukku meṟkut taṭiyiraṇṭu nikki
itaṉ melvarampukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai ērippaṭṭi vaṭavāykkā-
lukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai attimeṭṭu peruvarampu[k]ku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟ-

kellai atticceṟu vaṭavarampukku teṟkum innāṟperellaiyuḷḷum uṭpaṭṭa
ni-

ṉṟu kūṟa immoḻi keṭṭu etirmoḻi kuṭutton immaṇṭalattuc cempūrk
koṭ-
ṭattu puṟaiyūr nāṭṭucceyyūrāna jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻanallūrt tiraiyanmāte-
ḷ muṟcollappaṭṭa nirnilam viṟṟuttarak koṇṭu perumukkilāna kaṅkai
viḷakkup puṟamākak koṇṭu viṭuvenānkoḷ[v]eṉ muṟkūṟiya perumukki-
lāna kaṅkaikoṇṭānallūr ūrom emmil icaintavilaipporuḷ a[ṉ*]

ṟāṭu naṟkācu aiñcu ikkācu aiñcu āvaṇakkaḷiye kiḻikaiccelak koṇṭu
piṟkūṟiya

l.note: Few letters lost in the end.

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Mukhyāchalēśvara temple on the hill.
inscription.p: In Tamil verse praising one Timmayappōlayaṉ.

l.ta: temālaimārpiṉaṉ teṣi kulātipaṉ cirmaruvum kāmar tarunikaṟ kāmapaṉ mun-
vanta kārvaṇṇamāl vāmā nakulaṉ kecakatta vāraṇaṉ vaṇmai mukkil

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Mukhyachalaesvara temple on the hill.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Maravarman Vikrama Pāṇḍya Year 7 = 1290 A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of a salt-pan (aḷam) called Tirumalaichchokkaṉ, by the ūrār of Marakkāṉam in Ōymā-nāḍu
alias Vijaiyarājentira-vaḷanāḍu as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple of Vālmīkīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār on
the hill at Perumukkil. Originally this salt-pan was a kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadānam of the temple and it was lying
waste from the days of Kōpperuñjinga until now. Nearly twenty members of the ūrār signed the
document.

l.ta: ṉāṭāṉa vicaiyarācentira vaḷanāṭṭu marakkāṉattu ūravarom puttūrāṉa
kiḻakkut tirumalaiccokkaṉ eṉṟu per kūvappaṭṭa aḷam
yaṉār kuṭiniṅkāt tevatāṉam neṭunāḷppaṭa aṉupavittup pontu
pallavaṉ kopperuñciṅkar nāḷ tuṭaṅki varampuṅ karaiyum aḻintupāḻ
kiṭakkaiyil ivvāṇṭaip paṅkūṉi māta mutal kuṭiniṅkāt tevatāṉaṅkaḻit-
tu iṉṉāyaṉārkkut tirunāmattuk kāṇi ākat tantom marakkāṉattu ūravarōm

ivai aṇukkappallavaraiyaṉ eḻuttu cempiyaṉ pallavaraiyaṉ eḻuttu
teṉṉavatarai-

yaṉ eḻuttu vāḷuvarāyaṉ eḻuttu maḻavarāyaṉ eḻuttu tevarkaṇṭaṉ eḻuttu
kurukularāyaṉ eḻu-

ttu cempiyataraiyaṉ eḻuttu miṉavaṉ muventaveḷāṉ eḻuttu ceḻiyataraiyaṉ
eḻuttu vicai-

yarāyaṉ eḻuttu tiṇṭivaṉamuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu vāṉavaṉ viḻupparaiyaṉ eḻuttu
tevāṇṭai

eḻuttu vikkiramacoḻappaṭṭaṉa ṉāṭṭu veḷāṉ eḻuttu kulottuṅka
coḻappaṭṭiṉa ṉāṭ-

ṭu veḷāṉ eḻuttu kuṉṟameṭuttāṉ eḻuttu tiruvaṇṇāmalaiyuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu
kurukularā-

yaṉ ātipuruṣaṉ eḻuttu irācamāṇikkap pallavaraiyaṉ eḻuttu aruḷmoḻip
pallavaraiyaṉ eḻuttu

paṭṭiṉaṉāṭṭu veḷāṉ eḻuttu teṉṉavarāyaṉ eḻuttu irārāppaṭṭiṉa ṉāṭṭu
veḷāṉ

eḻuttu villavaṉ viḻupparaiyaṉ eḻuttu tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu
akaḷaṅkappal-

lavaraiyaṉ eḻuttu civakkoḻuntaṉ irācentira coḻa viḻupparaiyaṉ eḻuttu
araiyaṉ

tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu arumpākkiḻāṉ eḻuttu nallātaṉ
tiruvaṇṇāmalaiyuṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Mukhyachalaesvara temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Gift of 32 sheep for a lamp to the hill temple at Perumukkil alias Gaṅgaikoṇḍachōḻa-nallūr in
Ōymā-nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, by Ammai-appa-kōn of Vākūr Uppaḍukuḍi in Naḍuvi-nāḍu.

l.ta: puviyeḻum pāṟkaṭal poṟ pukaḻparappi āti ukamā[m] menna-
c cotimaṇimuṭi kavittaruḷi teṉṉavarum cer(ā)larum ciṅkaḷarum
mutalāya mannavarkaḷ tiṟaikuṇantu ce(y)vippa vāḻivāḻi ceṅkol
runtaruḷiya śrīirājā[ti]rāja tevarkku yāṇṭu añcāvatu jaya-
ḷa nāṭṭu perumukkilāna kaṅkai koṇṭānallūr tirumalaimel
paṭukuṭi kuttuvān patāḷi komaṇi āna ammaiappakon vaitta
ṭum iṣapa erutonṟum kaikkoṇṭu cantirātittavarai erikka-

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Mukhyachalaesvara temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Gift of land by Araichūr-uḍaiyāṉ Uyyavandāṉ •••(name broken) from Nittavinōda-vaḷanāḍu in
Chōḻa-maṇḍalam, for applying sandal paste to the deity on festival days. The donor purchased the land
from the ūrār of Perumukkil in Perumukkil-nāḍu in Ōymā-nāḍu alias Vijayarājēndra-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇara puvimātu vaḷara n[ā][m]ātu viḷaṅkac cayamātu virumpa
[ta]ṉṉirumalaraṭi maṉṉavar cūṭac ceṅkol ceṉṟu ticaitoṟum vaḷarppa
veṅkaliniṅki me[y]yaṟaṉ taḻaippakkaliṅkamiriyak kaṭalmalain(ā)ṭātti
valaṅkoḷḷāḻi va-

vaḷanāṭṭup perumukkil nāṭṭup paḻamukkil ūrom
coḻa[maṇṭala]ttu nittavinota vaḷanāṭṭu a[rai]cūruṭaiyāṉ uyyavantāṉ
aḻakiya •••viṟṟukkuṭutta nirnilamāvatu kiḻpāṟkellai •••perumā ceṟuvukkum
teṉpāṟkellaivāykkālukkum

vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai niṇ[ṟā]npaṭṭikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
kaṟkuḷavarampukkut teṟkum iṉnāṟpāṟkellai uḷḷum uṭpaṭanilam
paṉ[ṉi]raṭikkolāl kuḻi aiñnūṟum tirumalaimel tiruvāṉmi

ccāntu cāttukaikku aṟṟaināḷaikku veṇṭuṅkuṟaivaṟuppukku nāṅkaḷ
ivaḻakiyate[va*]nukku viṟṟukkuṭuttu emmil icainta vilaipporuḷkai
aṟakkoṇṭu vilaikkaṟaviṟṟukkuṭuttom iṉṉilattukku talainir kaṭainir
pāccap peṟuvatākavum cilvari coṟu māṭṭuveṭṭi muṭṭaiyāḷ iṟai

muṟaimaik kāṭṭakkaṭavomallātomāka sammatittu inta aḻakiya tevanukku
viṟṟu[kuṭu*]ttom paḻamukkil ūrom ūrkkaṇakku kocikaṉ caṭaikoṭan
malaikuniya niṉṟāṉ eḻuttu itu

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Mukhyachalaesvara temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Gift of 32 cows for a lamp by a temple woman (dēvaraḍiyāḷ) Sivan-pākaṅkoṇḍāḷ alias Vīrapattira-
naṅgai to the temple at Perumukkil alias Edirilichōḻa-nallūr in Ōymā-nāḍu. The donor was the daughter
of Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-māṇikkam and grand daughter of Āṇḍakai.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya [tiru]mātum puvimātum jayamātum nāmaruviyakalaimātum
pukaḻmātum nayantu pulkaarumaṟai nanneṟiyaṉaittun taḻaippa varumuṟaimayil
ma[ṇi]muṭicūṭit tiṅkaḷveṇkuṭait ticaikaḷi-

ṟeṭṭuntaṅkutani kūṭantāneṉa viḷaṅkak karuṅkalippaṭṭiyaic ceṅkol tuṟappa
porukatirāḻi pulivaḷantuṭantu miṉavarp pallavar [mu]taliyapāttivar pa[ṇi]ya
eṇṇaruṅkaṟpum maṇmicainaṭātti eṇṇaruṅkaṟpum

vaḷanāṭṭup perumukkalāṉa etirilicoḻanallūr
uṭaiyār tirumalaimel āḷuṭaiyanāyaṉār tiruvānmi

muṭaiyāṟku
ikkoyil tevaraṭiyāḷ āṇṭakai makaḷ kulottuṅkacoḻa māṇikkattin
makaḷ

civanpāka[ṅ]koṇṭāḷāṉa virapattiranaṅkai innāyaṉāṟku vaitta tirununtā
viḷakku onṟu onṟukkun nāṉviṭṭa pacu

iṣapam onṟu ivaḷ
virapattiranaṅkai vaitta tirununtā viḷakkāṟ pacuk kaikkoṇṭu
cantirātittavarai celuttakka-

ṭavomānom ikkoyil kā[ṇi]uṭaiya civappirāmaṇarom itu
santirātittavarai

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1905.)
Perumukkal, Tiṇḍivaṉam Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Mukhyachalaesvara temple on the hill.
inscription.p:
Sanskrit and Grantha. Records the gift of a pot by a chief who belonged to the Vrishabha family.
Year is mentioned in Kadhapayathi system.

l.gr:
svastiśrī [||*] śakābtēmnidhinityākhyē dēvō vraṣabha vamśajaḥ [||*] dēvasya
mukhyaśailasyabhēdamam makuṭamarppayat ||§

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1905.)
Daḷavānūr, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rājēndra Year 10 + = c. 11th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
On the south wall of the rock-cut cave at Dalavanur. right of entrance.
Fragment. No details.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1905.)
Daḷavānūr, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the pillar at the entrance into the rock-cut cave at Dalavanur.
inscription.p:
Seems to record gift of gold by one Puraichar Mōḍaṉ of Mūlaṅgūr, which was entrusted to a
priest living in Veṇpēḍu.
(Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XII, No. 52).

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1905.)
Daḷavānūr, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.
On one of the pillars inside the same rock-cut cave at Dahavanur.
inscription.p:
Tamil verse recording the excavation of the cave called Śatrumallēśvarālayam dedicated to Śiva
by the king who was wearing the garland of Toṇḍai flowers. (This seems to be a later Tamil rendering of
the following Sanskrit inscription of Mahēndravarman I). Composed by Brahmamaṅgalavaṉ Chellan
Śivadāsan a local resident.
(Published in EpigraphiaIndica, vol. XII, pp. 225-26.)

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1905.)
Daḷavānūr, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.
On one of the pillars inside the same rock-cut cave at Dahavanur.

p.b: Palava Narēndra alias Satrumalla = c. 800 A.D.

inscription.p:
Sanskrit in Grantha letters. Records the excavation of the cave called Śatrumallēśvarālya by King
Narēndra Śatrumalla.
[Published in Epigraphia Indica Vol. XII, page 225-226.]

edition.p: Two late inscriptions below this record the names of a God and Goddess.

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1905.)
Daḷavānūr, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Īsvara temple in the same village.
inscription.p:
This is an order from Sāḷuva Maṅgudēva-mahārājaṉ sent to the ūravar of Talaivānallūr saying
that made tax-free some tirunāmattukāṇi land of the temple as directed by (his superior) Aṇṇar Goppaṇar.
Lines 5 to 7 give the names of some 12 hamlets which may be having the tax exempted land.

l.ta: cāḷuva maṅkutevamahārājaṉ Olaitalaivānallūr ūravar kaṇṭu taṅkaḷūr uṭai

ramuṭaiya nāyaṉār tirunāmattukāṇi nañcey puñceyum uṭaiyār
jayaciṅkacōḷi

kāṇi kuṭinīṅkāt tevatānamum tirunāmattukāṇi tiruviḷakkuppuṟa iṟaiyiliyum
niṅkalāka

koppaṇar niruppaṭiye nāmum kuṭuttom ippaṭikku cantirātittavarai cella
kalveṭṭikoḷvatu § talavānallūr piṭākai kallālippaṭṭu

uḷḷiyampākkam aṇilāṭiyinampaṭṭum maruvūr nillālam nāvalental
neṟkuppai marampaṭṭu tiruvaṉpaṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1905.)
Daḷavānūr, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Īsvara temple in the same village.
inscription.p:
The king issued an order through his officer Cheyyagaṅgadēvar making some land in
Talaivānallūr as tax-free tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple of Cheyyakichuramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār. A number
of taxes exempted is mentioned.

l.gr:
ḷakula kāḷāndhakāra divākara cōḷa vamśavāri rāśipandhabānala laṅkāpati
kāḷakūṭa kadanakāḷa kaṇṭhakkarikāḷabhakaṇdhirava kṣēmāsura vidāraṇa
narasimhakāmvamśa

śvānara jayantamaṅgalapurādhiśvara vīragaṇḍagōpāla
bhujaṅgavihaṅgarāja kāñcipura viracitasāmrājyābhiṣēka gaṇapa-

ṭapākala bahuvidhavairiduggabhājana praṇatarāja sthāpanācārya
tribhuvana rājādhirāja paramēśvara śrīvikramapāṇḍyadēva svastiśrī
l.ta:
timaṅkalattu vaṭapiṭākai talaivāynallūril namakku naṉṟākac
ceyyakaṅkatevar Olaippaṭināyaṉār

ceyyacikicuram muṭaiyanāyaṉārkku tevatāṉam kaṭṭaicceṟu rūpāy
pāṇaṉkuṇṭil rūpāy

nilaṉtiruttu taṭi iraṇṭiṉāṉāṉāl rūpāy
kiḻaikkaṭṭaicceṟu taṭi nāliṉāl rūpāy

iṉṉāyaṉār eṟiaruḷiṉa āṇṭu mutal
tirunāmattukkāṇi iṟaiyiliyākak kārumpacāṉamum navirai

edition.l:
cittiraikkaṇṭaṉ payir karumpu kamuku kaḻunir vāḻaikoḻuntu mañcaḷiñci
uḷḷiṭṭa veṇṭum payir ceytu amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi tiruppaṇi taṭṭuppaṭāmal
celuttavum iṉṉilattukku kaṭamai iṉavari kācukaṭamai veṭṭi nilaiyāḷ
paṇikkottu ōlai eḻuttu viṉiyokamvācal viṉivokam aviṣekak
kāṇikkai pañcupīlikūṟṟilakkai kaṭaikkūṭṭilakkai taṇṭuvāṇ
peṟuuṭaippo • • • (unfinished).

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1905.)
Daḷavānūr, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Īsvara temple in the same village.
inscription.p:
These Sanskrit verses, referring to some gifts by Vikrama-pāṇḍya, seem to be related to the
above inscription.

l.gr:
svastiśrī§ śrī man vikramapāṇḍya dēva sacivvaśśurōdhiśśrēyasām gōgrāma
sarōjani dinakarō gōbhūhiraṇya pradaḥ§

•••••••••• hidēva ḷatiyasōyamsavanāmāsaraḥ pātamkṣētramapipradāyavidhivat
śambhōḥ pratiṣṭhām vyadhāt

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1905.)
Daḷavānūr, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa of the central shrine in the Īsvara temple in the same village.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to record a gift, by Prince Vijayarāyar, of land as dēvadāna to the temple
at Talaivānallūr, which was a hamlet of the brahamadēyam-Taṉiyūr Rājarāja-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Paṉaiyūr-nāḍu. The gift was meant to be an endowment for supply of flowers to the temple.

l.ta: lattu vaṭapiṭākai talaivāṉallūril jayaci • • muṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku •••ttāmur viṇ
•••paṟṟil tevatānamāka viṭṭa nilam ivvūr nañcai nilattil comaṉ taraca •••••
iṉṉāyināṟku tiru •••tirumuttu akilāṇṭa nāyakiyārkku tiru • n •••malaiyum paṇi •••
•••iṟaiyili āka vēṇṭum payir ceytu cantirātittavaraiyum tirumālaippuṟamāka
va •••eḻuttu ivai irāyappaṉ eḻuttu ivai veṇāvuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu

edition.p: The name of the goddess Ñāṉapūṅkodai is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1905.)
Maṇḍagapaṭṭu, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the pillar at the right of entrance into the maṇḍapa of the rock-cut cave at Maṇḍagapaṭṭu.

p.b: Pallava, Mahēndra I = c. 6th Century A.D.

inscription.p: Sanskrit in Grantha. Records the excavation of the cave temple by the king Vicitracitta.
[Published in Epigraphia Indica Vol. XVII page 12.]

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1905.)
Gingee, Gingee Taluk, South Arcot District.
On a slab built into the threshold of the main gate of the fort at Gingee.
inscription.p: Persian, Records that the fort was captured by Saadat-ul-lah khan.

l.pers: Lut6fi Haidar buwad bar ū afzūn ||
Fatḥ Kard ū ḥisāri Jinjī na

l.note: The numerical value of the letters of this hemistich gives the Year 1125 A.H.

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1905.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the west gōpura of the Sundarēśvara temple at Madura.
inscription.p: In Tamil verse. It is in praise of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1905.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On four pillars in the second story of the east gōpura of the Sundaraēśvara temple at Madura.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Jatāvarmaṉ alias Tribhuvaṉachakravartin Kulasēkharadēva I Year 2 = 1192 A.D.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. After the eulogy portion, only the palace at Madurai in Māḍakkuḷakkīḻ is
mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1905.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On four pillars in the second story of the east gōpura of the Sundaraēśvara temple at Madura

p.b: Pāṇḍya. Māṟavarman Sundara-pāṇḍya I, Year 10 = 1226 A.D.

inscription.p:
Full of gaps. Seems to mention gift of a village called Irāmaṉēri as tax-free dēvadāna by the king.
Some existing tax-free maḍappuṟam (endowment for maṭha) was excluded from the present gift.

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1905.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the pillars in the second story of the east gōpura of the Sundaraēśvara temple at Madura.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. After the eulogy portion, only the Brahmadēyam Cōlāntakac-caturvētimaṅgalam in
Pākanūr-kūam is mentioned.
A

edition.l: yate[vaṟku]yāṇṭu 15-

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1905.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a pillar in the second story of the east gōpura of the Sundaraēśvara temple at Madura.

p.b:
Pāṇḍya. J. Kulaśēkhara, Year 2. Tulā month, Apara 6, Thursday, Mrigaśīrsham = 1238,
September 30.

inscription.p:
Records that the king remitted taxes on some land that his senior officer Ayyan Maḻavarāyar gave
to a maṭha called Tiruñāṉasambandaṉ at Madurai in Māḍakkuḷakkīḻ which was under the management of
Taṟpurshaśivar a disciple of the Āchāriyar of Dakshiṇagōḷaki-maḍam of Chelva-Tiruvārūr. The land was
lying in Chōḷāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Pāgaṉūr-kūṟṟam. The sabhā of this village also agreed to
exempt the land from the payment of their dues, sabhā-vinoyōgam.

edition.l: mayāji vaṭuka[j]āta[ vēda] bha-
kkivai [ śiṟā] paḷḷi-

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock to the left of the entrance into the central shrine of the rock-cut temple of Narasinga-Perumal

p.b: Chōḻa, Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēsarivarmaṉ, Parānthaka I, Year 33 = 940 A.D.

inscription.p:
In Vatteluttu characters. Records the digging of a tank called Kaliyanēri after the donor Arunidi-
Kaliyaṉ, an officer of the king. Refers to Kīḻ-Iraniyamuṭṭam and the village of Narasiṅgamaṅgalam
included in it; also to the temple of Narasiṅga Perumānaḍigaḷ on the Tiruvānaimalai hill.
(Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. III, No. 106.)

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock to the left of the entrance into the central shrine of the rock-cut temple of Narasinga-Perumal

p.b: Chōḻapāṇḍya. J. Sundara-chōḻapāṇḍya, Year 21 = c. 1050 A.D.

inscription.p:
It is said that 25 sheep given by somebody for a lamp in the temple of Narasiṅgadēvar on
Tiruvāṉaimalai hill in Mēl-veṇ-iraṇṇiyaṉchōlai in Kīḻ-iraṇiya-muṭṭam was taken by the temple veṭṭikkuḍi
Nakkaṉ Cheṇpakaṉ for rearing them and supplying ghee to the temple.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 168.)

l.ta: ṉ colai tiruvāṉaimalaiymel eḻuntaruḷiya śrīnaraciṅkatevarkku vaicca vi-
ḷakkukku vacca āḷākku ne[y*]kku vaicca āṭu
tāṉeṉ nakkaṉ ceṇpatteṉ nāṉum eṉ vaṟkattārum muṭṭāmalaṭṭuvatāṉe-

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the rock to the right of the entrance into the central shrine of the rock-cut temple of Narasinga-
Perumal.

p.b: Chōḻapāṇḍya, J. Sundara-chōḻapāṇḍya, Year 21 = c. 1050 A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of a perpetual lamp to the deity Narasimha-āḷvār at Tiruvāṉaimalai in Kīḻ-iraṇiya-muṭṭam in
Rājēntrachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Rājarāja-pāṇḍi-nāḍu by Tattaṉ Chēnti, a brāhamaṇa woman of
Iṟaiyāṉaraichūr.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 169.]

l.ta: coḻa vaḷanāṭṭu kiḻ iraṇiyamuṭṭattu tiruāṉaimalai eḻuntaruḷi
ha āḷvārkku iṟaiyāṉ araicūr cēntaṉ paṭṭaṉēṉ

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the mandapa of the cave near the rock-cut temple of Narasinga-Perumal.
In Vatteluttu charecters. Says that it (the Jain sculpture) was remade by one Parivirājakar.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 98.]

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock with sculptures overhanging a natural cave in Āṉaimalai.
inscription.p: Fragment. The image was made by one Eṉāti-naṭi for the merit of Aṇiyaṉ.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 99.]

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock with sculptures overhanging a natural cave in Āṉaimalai.
inscription.p: Says that it is under the protection of the tiṇaikkaḷattār (tax officers).
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 100.]

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock with sculptures overhanging a natural cave in Āṉaimalai.
inscription.p: Says that it is under the protection of the karaṇattār (accountants) of Poṟkōḍu.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 101.]

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock with sculptures overhanging a natural cave in Āṉaimalai.
inscription.p:
Says that these images were made by Accananti and under the protection of the sabhā of
Narasiṅgamaṅgalam.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 102.]

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock with sculptures overhanging a natural cave in Āṉaimalai.
inscription.p: Mentions that the Yaksha image was made an individual of Peruvembāṟṟūr in Teṉ-kaḷavaḻi-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 103.]

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock with sculptures overhanging a natural cave in Āṉaimalai.
inscription.p: The image was made by an individual of Veṇpurai in Veṇpurai-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 104.]

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock with sculptures overhanging a natural cave in Āṉaimalai.
inscription.p: Says that an individual of Mallattirukkai made the image.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 105.]

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1905.)
Āṉaimalai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a rock with sculptures overhanging a natural cave in Āṉaimalai.
inscription.p:
Says that the image was made by an individual of Veṇpaikkuḍi-Vēṭṭañchēri in Veṇpaikkuḍi-nāḍu
and put under the care of the puravuvari-tiṇaikkaḷattār.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 106.]

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1905.)
Tirumōgūr, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa of the Kālamēgha-Perumāḷ temple at Tirumōgūr.
inscription.p:
Purports to be the order of the main deity of the temple, mentioning the grant of tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam
villages by Tammappa-nāyakkaṉ, son of Vasavaṇa-nāyakkan, a devotee of the temple. The gift villages
were located in Nālukōṭṭai-chīrmai within Madurai-maṇḍalam. Assignment of the income from the gift
villages to various services in the temple is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1905.)
Tirumōgūr, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa of the Kālamēgha-Perumāḷ temple at Tirumōgūr.
inscription.p: Gift of land by Basavana-Nayaka Timmappa-Nayaka.

l.gr:
ētat svarāsvarāthiśa mauli mālāpibhūṣitam [|*] śrīmanmōhūranāthasya bhratyala
gigasya śāsanam [||*] svastiśrīśakāptam1473

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1905.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On a pillar set up within the Muhammandan masjid at Gōripāḷayam (Madurai).

p.b:
Madurai Nayaka, Vīrappa-nāyaka (grand son of Viśvanātha-nāyaka), Śaka 1495, Bhava = 1574
A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that the king reconfirmed the sale of some six villages made in the past by some Pāṇḍya
king to the Mosque called Dilli-oru-kōl-sulutān Paḷḷivāsal at Gōrippāḷayam on the north bank of the
Vaigai River in Madurai-vaḷanāḍu. He did this on the authority of some old document. The six villages
were Gōrikuḍi, Chokkikuḷam, Allikuḷam, Kaṉṉānēmpal, Chiṟrutūr, and Tiruppālai.
[Royal flag, insignia (suruṭṭi), kettle, bow and arrow are engraved at the top of the inscription.]

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1905.)
Sōḻavandāṉ, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Janakanārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ temple at Sōḻavandāṉ.
inscription.p:
The trustees of the Śanaka-nārāyaṇa-perumāḷ temple received some money endowment from one
Ayyāvayyaṅgār for feeding in the Rāmānuja-kūḍam in the temple. The money was to be invested in some
temple land and the income from the land was to be used for the feeding.

l.ta:
m perumāḷ tiruviḷaiyāṭṭa mūṅkilaṭikku ōṭai nilamāvatu colai kuṟu[vā]kku
vaṭakku pāṟtta kiḻakku pārtta

yiruppukku meṟku mūṅkilaṭikku meṟku yinta nāṅku yellaikkuḷppaṭṭa nilam
tamakku upakṣaya

yiṉālē yinnilam nāṅkellaiyu muḷpattāme kaiyyāṇṭu koṇṭu
ipperumāḷ koyilile

vittu koḷḷak kaṭavarākavu[m*]
yippaṭik kalveṭṭik kuṭuttom

l.note: Few letters are lost at the end.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1905.)
Sōḻavandāṉ, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Janakanārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ temple at Sōḻavandāṉ.
inscription.p:
Fragment. Seems to be an order of the king to the sabhā of Chōḻāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Pāgaṉūr-kūṟṟam. (This may be related to the following inscription).

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1905.)
Sōḻavandāṉ, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Janakanārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ temple at Sōḻavandāṉ.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavaman Sundara-pāṇḍya II, Year 2 = 1240 A.D.

inscription.p:
This is the king's order remitting the mākkalam tax towards viniyōgam by the sabhā of
Chōḻakulāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam during both the perumpū and kuṟuvai cropping seasons on the
occasion of his coronation anniversary. This was made on representation by the king's senior officer
Ayyaṉ Maḻavarāyar.

l.ta: ceyappuyattiruppa vētanāviṉ veḷḷitaḻ tāmaraik kātalmātu kaviṉpe-
ṟat tiḷaippa veṇṭiraiyuṭutta maṇṭiṇik kiṭakait tirunilamaṭantaiyuri-
maiyiṟ kaḷippac camaiya nītiyun tarumamun taḻaippa yimaiyavar viḻāk ko-
ṭi yiṭantoṟumeṭuppak karuṅkalik kaṉalkeṭak kaṭavuḷ vetiyararun to-
ḻil veḻvic ceṅkaṉal vaḷaṟpak curutiyun tamiḻun tolvaḷaṅ kulavap po-

rutiṟalāḻi pūtaliñcū[ḻa]var ro[ru]kai yirucevi mummata [nāṟkoṭṭairā]vata
mu[ta]-
l ceyiṟtir koṟṟat teṇṭicaiyāṉai yetta me[ṟ]k kaṇṭa nāṭemateṉa-
k kayal kaḷikūrak kocalan tuḷuvaṅ kutiraṅ kuccaram pocala makatam poppaḷam
puṇṭaraṅ kaliṅkamiḻaṅ kaṭāraṅ keḷaṭateliṅkañ coṉakañ ciṉa mutalā viti
muṟai tikaḻa vevveṟu vakutta mutunilak kiḻamaiyiṉ muṭipuṉai ventar toru-
taṉināyaka ṉeṉṟulaketta tirumuṭi cūṭic ceṅkoloccik ko[ṟṟa]ttāḷ
kkuḷirk kuṭai niḻaṟ[ka]ṟṟaik kava[ri kāvalar vica miṭaikatir navamaṇi vi]-
ra simhāsaṉattuṭaṉ muṭicūṭiyuyarku tiruviṉ paṅkaiyamalaṟ karaṅkuvi-
ttup pāttipar maṅkaiyar tiraṇṭetir vaṇaṅkuñ ceṉṉiyiṟ cuṭaroḷi mau-
lik cuṭarmaṇi meliṭac civantaṇai malar ciṟṟaṭik kamalamatukaraṅ kamalameṉṟu-
tu mutal tavirap peṟaveṇumeṉṟu aiyyaṉ maḻavarāyar namakkuc coṉṉa-
mpilum veṭṭuvittuk koḷka ivai kulacekara[ut]taramantiri eḻuttu ||§ yā-

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1905.)
Sōḻavandāṉ, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Janakanārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ temple at Sōḻavandāṉ.

p.b: Pāṇḍya. Māṟavaman Sundara-pāṇḍya II, Year 2 = 1240 A.D.

inscription.p:
This is the king's order remitting the mākkalam tax towards viniyōgam by the sabhā of
Chōḻakulāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam during both the perumpū and kuṟuvai cropping seasons on the
occasion of his coronation anniversary. This was made on representation by the king's senior officer
Ayyaṉ Maḻavarāyar.

l.ta:

[tira]

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1905.)
Sōḻavandāṉ, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Janakanārāyaṇa-Perumāḷ temple at Sōḻavandāṉ.

p.b: Pāṇḍya. J. Kulaśēkhara II, Year 2 = 1240 A.D.

inscription.p:
This is a royal order issued to the sabhā of Chōḷāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam reducing the
mākkalam tax (both in cash and grain) that had been in vogue in the village until the 22nd Year (of the
previous king). Here also, as in the above inscription, Ayyaṉ Maḻavarāyar figures as the officer
requesting the tax reduction.

l.ta:
kalaimaṭantaiyum jayamaṭantaiyu nalañciṟappak koḷānta ciṉappuliyuṅ
koṭuñcilaiyuṅ kulaintoḷi-

ppa vāḷānta poṟkirimel varikkayalkaḷ viḷaiyaṭa iruṅkaṭal valaiyattiṉitaṟam
perukka karuṅkali kaṭin-

camayamumaḻakuṭan tikaḻa eḻuvakai pāṭalumiyalu-

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1905.)
Tiruttarakōsamangai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa of the Maṅgalēśvara temple at Tirutaraksśamaṅgai.

p.b: Śaka 1393, Khara, Kārttikai, Purva 11, Aśvati = 1471 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records the performance of abhishēkam to the deity Maṅgai-perumāḷ-nayiṉār of the temple by on
Naṇichala-uḍaiyāṉ Pūvāṇḍa-chēdirāyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1905.)
Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa of the Maṅgalēśvara temple at Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai.

p.b:
Tañjāvūr Nāyaka. Achchutappa-nāyaka, son of Sevvappa-nāyaka. Śaka 1505,
Chittirabhānu, Vaigāsi, Purva 5, Monday = 1582 March.

inscription.p:
Gift of a village, Chāttaṉūr near Tirukkaḷāchchēri in Chōḻa-maṇḍalam, by the king to Nayiṉār-
maṅgaipperumāḷ temple at Tiruvuttarakōsamaṅgai in Chembi-nāḍu, for renovation and worship.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1905.)
Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa of the Maṅgalēśvara temple at Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai.

p.b: Śaka 1425, Dundubhi, Puraṭṭāti, 13, Purva 11, Monday, Tiruvōṇam = 1502 September 12.

inscription.p:
Sale of some dēvadāna land of the temple by the trustees (Śrī-rudra Śrī-māhēśvarar) of the
temple to one Vaḷattuāḻvikkum-perumāḷAḻagiya-tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyār of Veṉṟumuḍichūḍiya-
sundarapāṇḍiyapuram in Pāvūr-nāḍu for a sum of 300 neṉmēṇikalipaṇam stipulating that the purchaser
should give half the produce of the land for conducting Śivarātri-pūja, etc in the temple.

l.ta:
piṭipāṭu paṇṇik kuṭuttapaṭi iṉāyaṉārkkum nācciyār
vāruṟupūṇmu[lai]

ṟṟāṉiṉpamikat tecattār poṟṟuñ civarāttiri maṅkaiyīcaṟ curam yicaintu §

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1905.)
Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa of the Maṅgalēśvara temple at Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai.

p.b:
Pāṇḍya, M. Vīra-pāṇḍya, Year 2+12, Śaka 1355 Dhanu, Purva 7, Wednesday, Hasta = 1433
A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that the king gifted all taxes from the village of Puṟkuḷam in Chembi-nāḍu to a
brāhmaṇa of Maruṅgūr alias Paṇḍitachōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Muttūṟṟu-kūṟṟam. It is specified that
the gift village also included the untouchable slaves (tīṇḍā-aḍimai) of the outer chēri.

edition.l: vaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrīvirapāṇṭiya tevaṟkuyāṇṭu 2 vatu etir[ 12 vatu] mi-

kkum iyaṟkaikuṭikālukkum teṟkum āka ipperu nāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa [pa]-
ṟṟu maṉai[ti]ṭal ūruṇi uṭaippukuḷam kuḷapparappu naṉcey puṉcey meṉokkiya

karaiyile

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1905.)
Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the east wall of the kitchen of the Maṅgalēśvara temple at Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai.

p.b:
Madurai Nāyaka, Muttuvīrappa-nāyaka, Śaka 1530, Kīlaka, Āni, Pūrva 7, Monday, star
Rōhiṇi (irregular) = 1608 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that one Kallūr-kiḻavar Tiruvirunda-perumāḷ donated the taxes, kāval-achchu and
ettuvaḻi, from his sīrmai to conduct a festival in the temple for the merit of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1905.)
Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the east wall of the kitchen of the Maṅgalēśvara temple at Tiruttarakōsamaṅgai.

p.b: Pāṇḍya M. Kaliyugarāmaṉ Tirunelvēlipperumāḷ Vīra-pāṇḍya, Year 2+25 = 1323 A.D.

inscription.p:
The king gifted more than ten villages in Kīḻ-chembi-nāḍu as tax-free dēvadāna to the temple of
Maṅgaipperumāḷ temple at Tiruvuttarakōsamaṅgai.

l.ta: nayiṉār koyilil śrīrutti[ra] śrīmākeccuraṟku iṉṉayiṉāṟku paḻaiya-
ūrkaḷ iṉṉāḷ mutalukku tevatāṉamāka kaiyyāṇṭu ko-
ḷḷumpaṭi tāpittuk kuṭukkaiyil immaruyāti kalveṭṭuk ko-

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1905.)
Rāmēsvaram, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the dhvajasthambha of the Parvatavardhanl-Amman temple at Ramesvaram.
inscription.p:
Sanskrit in Nāgari script and also in Grantha script. Mentions the setting up of the
dhvajasthambha.

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1905.)
Rāmēsvaram, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the balipitha close to the dhvajasthambha of the Rāmanāthasvāmi temple at Rāmēśvaram.
inscription.p:
Simhala script and language. Contains a praśasti of the king narrating his expeditions and
charities. He is called Kalinga Chakravarti and is said to have renovated the temple called
Niśśamkēśvara (named after him). The follwing text and translation are reproduced from EI, Vol.
XXXVI, pp. 23-32.

l.simtr:
Śrī siṁha-vikrama a7ti Tri-Siṁhaleśvara Kāliṅga-chakravartti-[svāmīn-vahanse rā]jaya pat
tān paṭan avu[rudu]ven Laṁkāva sisārā gam niyam-gam rājadhāni ādivū no-ek prasiddha-
sthāna hā jala-durgga paṁka-durgga [vana-durgga] Samanoḷa ādi-vū giri-durgga at-
aṁbulu-pakak se balā vadārā •••••••••• [bi]so-varun-vahan-

patā pas-tulābhārayak ba7gin dī dukpatu[n su]vapat koṭa7 suvapatun srimat kota7 tun ra7[jayehi
no]ek buddhālaya namvā tun-nakā samaiga koṭa7 lo-[sasun] samurddha(mriddha)
koṭa7 •••••••••• rajadaru-kenakun karavū-

ye pe[n]e da7 yi vadārā ananta-vastu vi[yadam karavā] mehekaruvan ānanda karavāPihiṭi-
rajayaṭa piyumak se vū Ruvan[va7li-maha]-dahagab-vaha[nse] karavā ehi sisāra boho
[saṅgun pi]rivan ganvā mahā-vihāra ya7 yi nam tabbā ge[na7] •••••

tun=aka ba7gin aya gannā niyāsye]n vyava[stha koṭa7] •••••

param=āna [ndaa] laḷā [ya]m [kena]kun karana pereḷiyak tabā to[si]n a7laḷisiti [prajāvan] •••

hira [-sanda-pamuṇu vēyan] mīyan ādin visin sopadras[va] vū talpata7 liyā diyehi ha7ndi hiri
se no koṭa7 un-un-ge [vamś-ānugata va7 [e] kaḷavun [nama] da dun raja-daruvan nama da
boho kalak pa[vatnā niyāyen tāmbra(mra)-śāsana pavat karavā] •••••

nam yut devālayalk namvā •••••

vālayehi aturuvā maṇḍapaya sarahā ama •••••

•••••Puvagu-divayina Miṇinak-divayina Kappa-divayina Kāra-divayina ādi-vū no-ek
divayin balā vadāra ma •••••

•••••va[dan supip]-piyum se karavā nra(nri)tya-gita-dāna-vinoda nam vīra-siṁ
[hāsanaye]hi •••••

translation.p:
(Line 1) Hail! The Sovereign Lord Kalinga-chakravarti, who possesses the prowess of a lion and
who is the Lord of the Three Simhalas, in one Year from his accession to the sovereignty toured
round Lamkā and inspected thoroughly, as if they were an āmalaka fruit on (the palm of his) hand,
many well-known places such as villages, market-towns, seats of royalty as well as places difficult
of access on account of water, places difficult of access on account of marshes, places difficult of
access on account of forests and places difficult of access on account of mountains such as
Samanola (Sumana-kūta, i.e. Adam's Peak) •••••

(Lines 1-2) He gave annually five tulābhāras, the five personages including •••••••••• Their Majesties
the Queens and His Highness the Heir-apparent (maha-pānṇan) having ascended the scale pans; thus
he made the poor affluent and the affluent illustrious. He caused to be erected shrines of the Buddha
in the Three Kingdoms he unified the sects (of the Sangha)^4^ and thus he made the State and the
Church prosper •••••

(Lines 2-3)••••••••••• Having uttered 'Do you see how a certain king has caused it to be restored' he
caused vast treasures to be spent, made the workmen to rejoice and effected the restoration of the
great dāga7ba, the sacred Ruvanva7li, which is like unto a lotus for the Pihiṭi kingdom. Around that
da7ga7ba he made the members of the Saṅgha take up residence in pirivanas, and gave the name of
Mahāvihāra (to that monastery) ••••••••••

(Line 4). He enacted a law that the (grain)-tax on an amuṇa (sowing extent) of the best grade (of
fields) should be (not in excess of) one amuṇa and three pa7lās and six akas as maḍaran (levy), on an
amuna of middle grade (of fields), one amuṇa and two pa7lās and four akas as maḍaran, and on an
amuṇa of the lowest grade (of fields) the tax to be one amuṇa and two pa7las and three akas as
maḍaran ••••••••••

(Line 5). He caused supreme joy (to the people) by their reflection = King Suḷu-Kalingu, Veli and
Kaḷava of widespread fame are indeed kings of the lineage of this (king); and, far from there being
any sort of disturbance caused by any one, the subjects being stirred with emotion •••••

(Line 6) ••••• He introduced the practice of granting charters written on copper-plates so that
heritable lands, granted to last as long as the sun and the moon endure, may not become like lines
drawn on water, on account of the grant being written on palm-leaves which are in danger from
termites and rats, but ensured that, associated with the lineages of those (concerned) the names of
those who have done deeds (to merit such grants), and the names of the kings who granted such
gifts do last for a long time •••••

(Line 7)••••••Having eradicated thorns in the villages as well as in the forests, and having placed
the country in tranquility, he being desirous of single combats, proceeded to Jambudvipa (India) on
two occasions, and tarried there for the full duration of three months ••••• his viruda of
Apratimalla •••••

(Line 8) ••••• ‘Who indeed are those over the whole of the earth that can stand in front of Kālinga-
chakravarti? What remains to be done by us therefore is to accept the tribute sent (to us) with an oath
of paying tribute (in the future)’ •••••

(Line 9)••••••He gave incalculable treasure to repair a dilapidated shrine (devālaya) in the •••••
country, and erected here the shrine named Niśśamkeśvara •••••

(Line 10). Having raised it to the revenue of ••••• he acquired a gift-village, and assigned it to the
alms-hall (satra); again, having spread hundreds of thousands of suru ••••• on the vāla, and
having decorated the pavilion •••••

(Line 11) ••••• He was also pleased to inspect numerous islands including Puvagu Island, Miṇinak
Island, Kappa Island and Kāra Island.

(Line 12) ••••• Having made the faces of ••••• appear like full-blown lotuses ••••• on this throne
of heroes named Nṟitya-gīta-dāna-vinōda (literally one who rejoices in dance, singing and gifts).

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1905.)
Mēlkiḍāram, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the deserted Isvara temple at Mēlkiḍāram.
inscription.p:
Related to the following inscription. Records that ūrār of Tiruvāppanūr sold a village called
Dēvaṉ alias Dēsappugaḻ-nallūr, freed from all taxes, to the temple of Tiruvanantīśvaram-uḍaiya-
nāyanār.

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1905.)
Mēlkiḍāram, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the deserted Isvara temple at Mēlkiḍāram.

p.b: Pāṇḍya Sundara-pāṇḍya, Year 5 = c. 13th-14th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This seems to be an extension of the previous inscription and refers to the same sale of the village
by the ūrār of Tiruvāppanūr.

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1905.)
Mēlkiḍāram, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the deserted Isvara temple at Mēlkiḍāram.
inscription.p: Refers a sale deed. Incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1905.)
Mēlkiḍāram, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the deserted Isvara temple at Mēlkiḍāram.

p.b: Pāṇḍya [Māṟavarman Sundara Pāṇḍya I] = c. 1216-38 A.D.

inscription.p:
Much damaged. Related to the above two inscriptions. This mentions the acknowledgement of
receipt of the sale price by the ūrār.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1905.)
Mēlkiḍāram, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the deserted Isvara temple at Mēlkiḍāram.

p.b: Pāṇḍya. Māṟavarman Sundara-pāṇḍya II, Year 4+2 = 1244 A.D.

inscription.p: Built in. Records that the king made tax-free on the dēvadāna village of Cheḻuvaṉūr.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1905.)
Kōyil-Māriyūr, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Pushpavanesvara temple at Kōyil-Māriyūr.

p.b: Pāṇḍya. Māṟavarman Sundara-pāṇḍya II, Year 4+2 = 1244 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records the remission of taxes on several pieces of land belonging to the temple of
Tirupūventīśvam-uḍaiyār. The lands were lying in Churavaranēri and another village in Ēḻūr-chembi-
nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1905.)
Kōyil-Māriyūr, Mudukulattur Taluk, Ramnad District.
On the east wall of the Pavalaniravalli Amman shrine in the Pushpavanēśvara temple at Kōyil-Māriyūr.
inscription.p:
Portions mutilated. Records a decision of the trustees of the Pūvēntīsuramuḍaiya-tambirāṉār at
[•••pūr]kāl-paṭṭiṉam in Cintāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu regarding the performance of some service in the temple.

ttāvarka[ḷ*] iruntu ēḻām tiru[nāḷ] tiruccāntu cāttuki-
