inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1905.)
Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the Erichcha-Udaiyar shrine in the Ericbchā-Uḍaiyār temple at Ambasamudram.
inscription.p: Gift of sheep for a half lamp in the Tiruppōttuḍaiyadēvar temple. In Vatteluttu charecters.

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1905.)
Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the Erichcha-Udaiyar shrine in the Ericbchā-Uḍaiyār temple at Ambāsamudram.
inscription.p:
In Vatteluttu charecters. Gift of lamp by Rājarājaṉ, son of Tēyamperumāṉ alias Vīranārāyaṇa-
pallavaraiyaṉ. Also a viyāpāri called Āchchar Uṟayūr alias Chōḻiya-araya-māyilaṭṭi is mentioned but
hisrelation to the former is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1905.)
Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the Erichcha-Udaiyar shrine in the Ericbchā-Uḍaiyār temple at Ambāsamudram.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land for the expenses of god Thiruppōttuḍaiya Mahādēvar at Rājarāja-
chadurvētimaṅgalam a bhramadēya in Muḷḷi-nāḍu.

The inscription is several pieces, This inscription is dated in the 20th Year of Jaṭāvarmaṉ
Śrīvallabha and registers a gift of land for the expenses of the temple of god Thiruppōttuḍaiya-Mahādēvar
at Rājarāja-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya in Muḷḷinadu. the previous tenants of this land were
removed and it was named as Nālāyira-viḷāgam and made tax-free from the day Jupitar enter Kumbha.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV No. 245.]

l.ta: ḷil iṉitiruppa iru nilamum perumai e-

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1905.)
Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the south wall of the Erichcha-Udaiyar shrine in the Erichchā-Uḍaiyār temple at Ambāsamudram.

p.b:
Pāṇḍya, Vīra-pāṇḍya, ‘who took the head of the Chōḻa’, Year 12+1, Mithuna month, Solar
eclipse = 959 June 9.

inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu charecters. Records that the king donated ten vēli of land at Vaḻudiyūr in Muḷḷi-nāḍu
as kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadāna to the temple Tiruppōttuḍaiya-Bhaṭārar in the same nāḍu. The officer Chōḷāntaka-
brahmārāyar supervised the apportioning of the produce of the land for various services in the temple.
[Published in South Indian Inscriptions, vol. XIV, No. 95 & Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XXV, p. 35ff.]

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1905.)
Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the south wall of the Erichchā-Uḍāiyar shrine in the Ericbchā-Uḍaiyāar temple at Ambāsamudram.

p.b: Chōḻa-Pāṇḍya, Chaḍaiyavarman Sundara-chōḻapāṇḍya, Year 18 = c. 1050 A.D.

inscription.p:
Fragments. Only the names of the temples of Tiruppōttuḍaiyār and Tiruchchālaittuṟai
Śrīmūlastānamuḍaiyār are to be seen.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 162.]

l.ta: ṭi ñāṭṭu muṭikoṇṭa coḻavaḷa nāṭṭu muḷḷiñāṭṭu
kācu iraṇṭum āka kācu pattu ikkācu
yār koyil caṅkaranārāyaṇappiccanakamuṭaiyāḷ appi nampāṉa taṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 103 of 1905.)
Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the east wall of the Erichchā-Uḍaiyār shrine in the Erichchā-Uḍaiyāar temple at Ambāsamudram.

p.b: Pāṇḍya Māṟavarman Vikrama-pāṇḍya, Year 2 = c. 13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
In several pieces. Gift of 60 sheep to the temple of Tiruppōttuḍaiya-mahādēvar in Rājarāja-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Muḷḷi-nāḍu by a local resident.

The other pieces seem to belong to some inscription(s) of the 11th century. One refers to a
poliyūṭṭu gift of paddy by one Māḍalan Iḷayān Cheṭṭi of Vaḻakūr in Tiruvaḻundūr-nāḍu of Chōḻa-
maṇḍalam for feeding pilgrims during Aśvati festival. Piece IV refers to a similar gift by a merchant of
Kaḷakkuḍi-nāḍu to feed in Chōḻapāṇḍiyaṉ maḍam.

l.ta: t tirumiḻalaic caṅkaranārāyaṇaṉ vaitta tirununtā viḷa-

edition.p: The slabs below this inscription are out of order and fragments. 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 104 of 1905.)
Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the slab built into the floor of the first circuit of the Erichchā-Uḍaiyār temple at Ambāsamudram.

p.b: No king. Year 39 = c. 10th century A.D.

inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu. Gift of 150 sheep for three lamps in the temple of Tiruppōttuḍaiya-bhaṭārar in
Iḷaṅgōkkuḍi, a brahmadēya in Muḷḷi-nāḍu by three ladies, Taviriṇi Sātaṇan Antari of Kulattūr in Muḷḷi-
nāḍu, Iraiyāṉ Iraisiṅgam of Māṇilai-Veṇmāṉiḍam in Teṉ-Kaḷavaḻi-nāḍu and her younger sister Iraiyāṉ
Nōṇpay.

l.ta: kuḷattūr taviṇiri cātaṇaṉ antari vaicca tiruviḷakku oṉṟu teṉkaḷavaḻi nāṭṭu

māṇilai veṇmāṉiṭam iraiyāṉ iraiciṅkam vaicca viḷakku oṉṟu ivaḷ
taṅkai

iraiyāṉoṇpay vaicca viḷakku oṉṟu [ākaviḷakku] mūṉṟu immūṉṟu
viḷakkiṉukku
niyati muḻakkuneyyaṭṭi erivatāka vaiytta cāvāmūvā perāṭu nūṟṟaimpatu

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 105 of 1905.)
Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the slab built into the floor of the first circuit of the Ericbchā-Uḍaiyār temple at Ambāsamudram.
inscription.p: (THE SLAB IS NOW IN THE MADRAS MUSEUM.)

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu. While camping at Araichūr on the banks of the Peṇṇai river in Toṇḍai-nāḍu, the
king made an endowment of 200 kāsu entrusted with the sabhā of Iḷaṅgōykkuḍi in Muḷḷi-nāḍu for food
offerings in the temple of Tiruppōttuḍaiya-bhaṭārar in the village.
[Published in EI, vol. IX, p. 84 and SII, vol. XIV, No. 13.]

This record dated in the 4+12th Year of the reign of Varaguna Maharaja registers the gift of 290
kasu to the sabha of Ilangoykkudi in Mullinadu for worship and offerings to the God Bhatara in the
Srikoyil of Tiruppottudaiyar. The gift was made when Varaguna was stationed at Araisur on the bank of
the Pennai river in tondainadu.

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Gōpālasvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōyil.

p.b: Chōḻa-Pāṇḍya. Jaṭavarman alias Uḍaiyar Sundara-Chōḻa Pāṇḍya, Year 14 = 1035 A.D.

inscription.p:
Registers the sale of the village Mānābharana-chadurvēdhimañgalam, a brahmadēeya in Muḷḷi-
Nāḍu, which was the property of two brahmanas of Nigrilichōḻa-chaturvedhimangalam named Govindan
Madava-bhattan and Govindan Thiruvikiraman to the temple of Rajendracola-vinnagar-paramasvamikal
at Rajendra-chaturvedhimangalam. It is stated that the brahmanas had originally, obtained the village as
the gurus, probably, of the ruling dynasty.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 148.]

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Gōpālaśvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōil.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Jatāvarman alias Sundara-Chōḻa-Pāṇḍya dēva, Year 4 = 1025 A.D.

inscription.p:
States that assembly having met in the hall, ambalam, made some decision about the lands and
houses granted to the gardeners, drummers, potters, garland-makers, torch-bearers and dēvaraḍiyārs of
the temple of Sri Rājēndrachōḻa viṇṇagar-Āḻvār at Rājarāja-chadurvēdhimañgalam, a brahmadēya in
Muḷḷi-Nāḍu, a sub-division of Muḍikoṇḍachōola-vaḷanāḍu, a district of Rājarāja-Pāṇḍināḍu. The record
is incomplete.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 132.]

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Gōpālaśvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōil.
inscription.p:
Fragment Seems to register a grant of kāṇikaḍaṉ from a devadana village Imānabharana
chadurvēdimangalam. The seat in the palace hall (kūḍam) from where the order was proclaimed was called
RājendraChōḻa-Kārāṉai-viḷupparaiyan. Officers tirumantiravōlai, (Tirumuga)viḍai, paṭṭōlai and
puravuvari are mentioned.
[Published in SII. Vol. XIV, No. 133.]

caruppētimaṅkalam kāṇi

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Gōpālaśvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōil.

p.b: Chōḻa Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman alias Sundara-Chōḻa-Pāṇḍya, Year 13 = 1034 A.D.

inscription.p:
Registers a sale of land measuring 14+vēli, consisting several pieces, by the sabha of Rājarāja-
chadurvēdhimaṅgalam to the temple of Rājēndra Chōḻa-viṇṇagar-Paramaswāmikaḻ.
[Published in SII. Vol. XIV, No. 144 & Epigraphia Indica Vol. XI, page 292.]

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 110 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north, west and south walls of the central shrine in the Gōpālaśvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōil.
inscription.p:
This is related to the above inscription. The king ordered that the land sold by the sabhā be converted
from the original brahmadēya status to veḷḷānvagai status under the new name Chōḻapāṇḍiya-nallūr and made
it into a tax-free dēvadāna of the temple and get it registered so in the government revenue accounts. The king
was in his palace at Rājēndrachōḻapuram when he issued the order. Many taxes remitted on the granted village
is mentioned. Several officers involved in the execution of the grant are mentioned.
[Published in SII. Vol. XIV, No. 145.]

l.ta: varkku ivvūrc cavaiyār kācu koṇṭu vilaikku viṟṟa nilam

yil mutalākki coḻapāṇṭiyanallūr

uṭaiyār śrīcuntaracoḻapāṇṭiyatevar rāje

coḻapurattuk koyiliṉ

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Gōpālaśvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōil.
inscription.p:
Gift of land. Mentions that the Chēra king Rājarājadēvar made an additional grant of ten vēli of land
as kuḍinīngā-kārāṇmai to the temple of Rājēndrachōḻa-viṇṇagar, as the amount of kāṇikkaḍan(land tax) from
the dēvadāna village of Māṉābharaṇa-chaturvēdimangalam granted earlier was insufficient for the temple
expenses. The land was distributed among some six persons for cultivation. The latter had to supply a quantity
of 2000 kalam of paddy regularly on that land.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 154.]

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Gōpālaśvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōil.
inscription.p:
This registers an order of the Chōḻa king while he was staying in his palace at Kāñchipuram making
the veḷḷānvagai village of Piñchanūr, with its kāṇikkaḍan of 3340 kalam, as tax-free dēvadāna of the temple of
Rājēndrachōḻa-viṇṇagar founded by the Chēra king Rājasimhan at Rājarāja-chaturvēdimangalam in Muḷḷi-nāḍu
from the fifteenth regnal year of his son (the Chōḻa-pāṇḍya viceroy at Madurai) Sundarachōḻa-pāṇḍiyan. The
gift village was in Kuṟumaḍai-nāḍu in Muḍikoṇḍachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu of Rājarāja-pāṇḍi-nāḍu.
[Published in Rajendravinnagar by Thirumalai.]

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Gōpālaśvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōil.
inscription.p: Fragment. Refers to some gift of Rājarājadēvar, who seems to be the Chēra king.

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1905.)
Maṉṉārkōyil, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Gōpālaśvamin temple at Maṉṉārkōil.
inscription.p:
Lines broken. Refers to the gifts of cows, buffaloes, etc. donated by the Chēra king
Rājasiṅgadēvar and by a local Veḷḷāḷa.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 177.)

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1905.)
Tiruvālīśvaram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple at Tiruvālīśvaram.
inscription.p:
Contains two sections. The first mentions a gift of cows for a perpetual lamp by the sabhā of
Rājarāja-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Muḷḷi-nāḍu. The second one mentions the decision of the same sabhā
that no tax should be collected from the cultivators of the flower garden called Rājēndrachōḻaṉ attached to
the temple of Tiruvāliśvaram.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 177.]

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1905.)
Tiruvālīśvaram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple at Tiruvālīśvaram.
inscription.p:
Vaṭṭeḻuttu. Gift of land by Ambalavaṉ Niṟāḍi of Piḍavūr in Piḍavūr-nāḍu in Chōḻa-nāḍu for the
expenses of various services and for wages to the temple establishment in the temple of Tiruvālīśvaram
on the bank of the riverKaḍiñai in the northern hamlet (piḍāgai) of Rājarāja-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Muḷḷi-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. The donor purchased the land from the sabhā of the latter village.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1905.)
Tiruvālīśvaram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple at Tiruvālīśvaram.
inscription.p:
Vatteluttu. Records a decision of the sabhā of Rājarāja-chaturvēdimaṅgalam not to collect the
taxes maṉṟuvari and taṇḍukuṟṟam on the cultivators of the dēvadāna land of the Tiruvālīśvaram temple
who live in the new settlement called Niṟāḍinallūr. They have to pay some dues after the fourth year of
their living in the new settlement.

ivvūrkkaṭiñaikkarai tiruvālī

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1905.)
Tiruvālīśvaram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple at Tiruvālīśvaram.
inscription.p:
Vaṭṭeluttu. Relates to above two inscriptions (116 and 117 of 1905.) The sabhā assigned part of
the land given by the donor mentioned in 116 of 1905 above as hereditary kārāṇmai to Piḍavūr-uḍaiyāṉ
Ambalavaṉ Āṟaikkāvaṉ. The samvatsara-vāriyar committee was asked to execute the decision.

l.ta:
vatsaravāriyar kaṇṭu coṉāṭṭu
vaṭakaraip piṭavūr nāṭṭup piṭavūruṭaiyāṉ amapalava-

ṉ niṟāṭi nampakkal nammūr vaṭapaṭākai kaṭiñaikku meṟkkum
veññirppaṉoḻikku vaṭakkum tiruvāli

nokkik kaṭiñaikkēy yiḻinta ikkāṇi araikkāṇik kotta mūṉṟukku
itaṉukkut teṟkku mivvicaitta perunāṉkel-

niṟāṭikuṭutta nilaṉ eḻu-
māvarai araikkāṇi muntirikaiyu[m*] nikki niṉṟa nilaṉ arai-

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1905.)
Tiruvālīśvaram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vālīśvara temple at Tiruvālīśvaram.
inscription.p:
Vaṭṭeluttu. Related to the above three inscriptions. This records the sale of a vēli of land by the
mahāsabhā of Rājarāja-chaturvēdimaṅgalam to Ambalavaṉ Niṟāḍi of Piḍavūr.

l.ta: kkuṭutta pūmiyāvatu nam vaṭapaṭākai veññīrkkaraip poḻikku vaṭakku

vatikkuk kiḻakkut teṟkaṭaiyak kāṇi
araikkāṇik kottattuk kiḻakku nōkki kaṭi ••••• [yo]

itaṉukkut teṟkuṅ kiḻellai kaṭiñaikku mēkkum ivvicai[nta] perunāṉ
kellaiyiṉuṭkiṭanta ni[la] ••••• vvūr nilamaḷavu kolāl vaṭaiñcili aṭaiya vēli
nilam śrīkoyil vaḷākamuṅ kalluṅ kaḻiccu vi[lai]kkaṟa viṟṟup poruḷa •••••

yom piṭavūruṭaiyāṉ ampalavaṉiṟāṭikku innilam velikkum
emmilicainta vi ••••• vvilaiyaṟak koṇṭu ituvey vilaiyolaiyum
po[ruṭ]celavolaiyumākavum vilaimāvaṟuti poruṭ celavolai itu [vE]ṟu
kāṭṭak kaṭavaṉallā •••

ampalavaṉiṟā[ṭi*]kku innilattukkuk kākka[vi]pperuṅkālil niṉṟu
nirpāyuṅkālkaḷāl munnāḷi[lor ni]ṉṟu nirpāyappeṟuvatākavum

paṭāraṉ cātta ṉeḻuttu ippa •••••

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1905.)
Tiruvālīśvaram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On a pillar set up near the balipītha the Vālīśvara temple at Tiruvālīśvaram.
inscription.p:
This contains a long eulogy of the military body called Mūṉṟukai-mahāsēṉai (‘the great army
having the three sections’). From the details it may be understood that it refers to the Chōḻa army. Among
the martial achievements of the body are mentioned their victories in several battles like Mātōṭṭam (in Sri
Lanka), Banavāsi, Uchchanti, Ettakkiri, Bādāmi against the Chēras, the Chāḷukyas, etc. The main
purpose of the inscription is to record that the precincts and the entire establishment (including the
Śivabrāhmaṇas, the carpenters, smiths, etc) of the Tiruvālīśvaram temple are under the protection of
the army people. Anyone who dies fighting for the protection of the temple was assured of honour and
protection to their kith and kin for ever.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1905.)
Tiruppuḍaimarudūr, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the east wall of the Chanḍēśvara shrine in the Pūtarjunēśvara temple at Tiruppuḍaimarudūr.
inscription.p:
Fragment. Sanskrit in Grantha script. Seems to mention a stone structure. The name Vīra-pāṇḍya
is legible in line 4.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1905.)
Tiruppuḍaimarudūr, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the east wall of the Chanḍēśvara shrine in the Pūtarjunēśvara temple at Tiruppuḍaimarudūr.
inscription.p:
Vaṭṭeluttu. This may be related to the above inscription and refers to a chēvakaṉ (soldier)
belonging to the Vīra-pāṇḍya-vēḻakam in Maṉayamādēvipuram in Pāṇḍimāttāṇḍa-vaḷanāḍu.
(Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 68.)

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1905.)
Tiruppuḍaimarudūr, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the east and north walls of the Chanḍēśvara shrine in the Pūtarjunēśvara temple at Tiruppuḍaimarudūr.
inscription.p:
Vaṭṭeḻuttu. Records a sale of some pieces of land by some brāhamaṇas of Iyalāṭṭimaṅgalam, a
brahmadēya in Muḷḷi-nāḍu to Piḍavūr-uḍaiyāṉ Ambalavaṉ Niṟāḍi of Piḍavūr-nāḍu in Chōḻa-nāḍu.

l.ta: ttu iyalāṭṭi maṅkala(m)muṭaiya vaṭavāyakattu nanta ca[ṉ]ma-
ykkālālun nirpāñcu nellu viḷaiyun nilattukku kiḻel

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1905.)
Tiruppuḍaimarudūr, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
On the west wall of the Chanḍēśvara shrine in the Pūtarjunēśvara temple at Tiruppuḍaimarudūr.
inscription.p: Fragment. Just Muḷḷi-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu is readable.

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1905.)
Tirukkuṟuṅguḍi, Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the nambi temple at Tirukkurungguḍi.

p.b: Pāṇḍya Māṟavarman Sundara-pāṇḍya I, Year 12 = 1228 A.D.

inscription.p:
This is a resolution of the mahāsabhā of Rājarāja-chaturvēdimaṅgalam to exempt, from taxes,
the land shares being enjoyed by a Nambi in their village. Perhaps Nambi was a priest in the Vaishṇava
temple of Tirukkuṟuṅguḍi and therefore the resolution was communicated to the Śrīvaishṇavas of that
temple. The mahāsabhā undertook to add the remitted taxes to the taxes towards the kaḍaikkar-jīvitam (?)
in their village. The remitted taxes were kaḍamai, antarāyam, uḻutāṉkuḍi, vari and vārapalam.

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1905.)
Tirukkuṟuṅguḍi, Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On the bell hanging in frontof the central shrine in the Nambi temple at Tirukkurungguḍi.
inscription.p: Sanskrit in Grantha. Gift of the bell. [same as in the following inscription]
[Published in Indian Antiquary, II, p. 360.]

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1905.)
Tirukkuṟuṅguḍi, Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On the bell hanging in front of the central shrine in the Nambi temple at Tirukkurungguḍi.
inscription.p:
This is the Tamil version of the previous inscription. The king, who is called as Ādittan of
Chiṟaivāy in Cheyatuṅga-nāḍu, gifted the bell.

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1905.)
Tirukkuṟuṅguḍi, Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On a stone set up in the northern side of the inner circuit of the Nambi temple at Tirukkuruṅguḍi.
inscription.p:
Vaṭṭeḻttu. The mahāsabhā of Vaikundavaḷanāḍu, a brahmadēya in Nāṭṭāṟṟuppōkku sold land
freed from taxes, to one Pañchavan Brahmādhairājan alias Chēndan Chāttan of Nellittōḻ in Malaināḍu,
who donated it for food offerings in the temple of Kuṟuṅguḍi.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIV, No. 19.]

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1905.)
Kaḷakkāḍu. Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the Perumal temple at Kaḷakkāḍu.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. The cyclic year and the Śaka year do not tally. Records that Rāmappa-nāyakkar, the
executive agent (kāriyattukku karttar) of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja Timmarāja Viṭṭaladēva gave
something (details lost) to the temple of Paramasvāmi at Chōḻakulavallipuram alias Kaḷakkāḍu in
Pāchchāṟṟuppōkku in Vāṉava-nāḍu.

l.ta:
maṉ mahāmaṇṭaliccura kaṇṭa kaṭṭāri[c] cāḷuva
ariyarāya vipāṭa pāḻcai-

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1905.)
Kaḷakkāḍu. Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On the north wall of the Satyavagisvara temple at Kaḷakkāḍu.

p.b: Travncore king (name not given), Kollam 676 = 1500 A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of land as dēvadāna to midday service in Puravarīchchuram temple in Kaḷakkāḍu
alias Chōḻakulavallipuram in Vāṉava-nāḍu. The king made the gift when he was in his camp at Vaḍachēri.

l.ta:
aruḷicceyal vāṉavaṉ ṉāṭṭu paccāṟṟuppokkuk kaḷakkāṭāṉa
coḻakulavallipurāttu nayiṉār puravericcuvaram uṭaiya nayiṉāṟku
ucciccantipūcai uṭaṉ kūṭṭi amutupaṭi kuṟuṇi

araimā muntirikaiyiṉ kiḻa-

raiyē muṉṟu mā mukkāṇi mutal taram itil araikkāṇīyiṉ

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1905.)
Kaḷakkāḍu. Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On the north wall of the Satyavagisvara temple at Kaḷakkāḍu.

p.b: Travncore king (name not given), Kollam 688 = 1512 A.D.

inscription.p:
From his camp at Vēḷākuṟichchi the king assigned the services of some kuḍi as the fifteenth
member in the temple at Kaḷakkāḍu. The document was written by Mārttāṇḍan Mārttāṇḍaṉ.

l.ta:
aruḷicceyal peṇṭukaḷil aṅkayakkaṇ nācciyāṟku
vāṉavaṉṉāṭṭu paccāṟṟup pokku kaḷakkāṭāṉa coḻakulavallipurattu nayiṉār
puraveriyicccuvaram uṭai-

yanayiṉār koyilukku taimātam patiṉañcāntiyati mutalukku patiṉañcāṅkuṭi āka
a[ka]k kuṭikku [kuṭu]tta vaṇṇam turamum ceyutu cuvarantiriyaṅkaḷum coṟum
paṟṟik koṇṭu ācantiratāravaṟ cantatippi-

ṟavēcamē ittaravu piṭipāṭāka kallilum cempilum veṭṭi kaiyyāṇṭu
koḷḷumpaṭi aṟunūṟṟu eṇpattu eṭṭām āṇṭu taimātam
patiṉañcānteyti veḷākuṟicciyil putiya viṭṭil

nām viṭāy irukka kaṟpitta aḷavukku itu taravākum ivai mārttāṇṭaṉ
māttāṇṭaṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1905.)
Kaḷakkāḍu. Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Gomati-Amman temple at Kaḷakkāḍu.
inscription.p:
The king gave permission to Iḷaiyāṉ Māttāṇḍakkuṭṭi to manage a maṭha called Vīra-māttāṇḍaṉ
and look after the distribution of free meals [nīrākāram] there. Rāmaṉ Rāmaṉ wrote the document
[Kollam] 708.

l.ta:
aruḷicceyal eṉṟum iḷaiāṉ māttāṇṭak kuṭṭi
vEḷāvuṭaiāṉukku kaḷakkāṭṭu nayiṉār po-

ycollāmeyyanayiṉār kōyilil pūcainaṭantu potum vakaiyil nācciāṟku

ippaṭikku irāmaṉ irāmaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1905.)
Kōdasēri Nāṅgunēri Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
On a stone set up near the village of Kōdasēri.
inscription.p:
Records some deed of charity given to Narasiṅgagōvinda-baṭṭar of Vāttullāsa gōtra by the trustees
of the Mīnāṭchi Sundarēchurar temple along with the ūravar, the royal officer Kamparayyaṉār, and
others assigning some land in Kōdachēri. The donee had to give a part of the produce of the land to the
temple for worship.

l.ta: l talai putukaraikku teṟku kiḷ el-

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1905.)
Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On a rock in Matribhutesvara temple at Tiruchirāppaḷḷi.
inscription.p:
For the merit of Vīrappa-nāyakkar, one Kallūr-kiḻavar Tevvantināta-piḷḷai Kurukirundaperumāḷ
made gift of the village called Pallapuram for Āvaṇi festival in the temple of Chevvandīśvarasvāmi.

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1905.)
Karuvūr, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the Moṭṭai-gōpuram of the Pasupatīśvara temple at Karuvūr, right of entrance.
inscription.p:
The Tiruvāyppāṭināṭṭār of Veṅgāla-nāṭu in Cōḻakēraḷa-maṇḍalam resolved to contribute to the
Tiruvānilai-mahādēva temple at Karuvūr alias Muḍivaḻaṅguchōḻapuram from each of their household in
the nāḍu one kiḍāy, one uḻakku of rice and one piḍi of ghee every year starting from the current Aippasi
month. The local brāhmaṇas, the tirunnīlakaṇḍamuḍaiyār, eḻilkōṭṭisamaiyattār and the vīrabhattirar
were requested to protect the charity. Stones out of order.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravarttikaḷ īḻamum maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉmuṭittalaiyuṅ
karuvūrum koṇṭaruḷiṉa śrīkulo-

ttuṅkacoḻa tevarkku yāṇṭu irupattaiñcāvatu coḻakeraḷa maṇṭalattu
veṅkālaṉāṭṭu karuvūrākiya muṭivaḻaṅkucoḻa-

cempiluñ cilaiyilum amaiya veṭṭikkuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu karuvūrcuṟṟiya
veṅkālaṉā-

aṟppicaimāta mutal āṭṭāṇṭutoṟum kuṭiyāl

ūrpirāmaṇarum tirunilakaṇṭamuṭaiyārkaḷum eḻil koṭṭi-

piṟamaṇṭalattil niṉṟuvantiṉa uḷavākil nāyaṉār śrīpaṇṭāra-

kuṭuppārkaḷākavum ittaṉmam cantirātittaval celvitāka kal-

veṭṭik kuṭuttom ivveṅkālaṉā[ṭ]ṭu tiruvā[y*]ppāṭi nāṭṭom
tiruvāṉilai

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1905.)
Karuvūr, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the Moṭṭai-gōpuram of the Pasupatīśvara temple at Karuvūr, right of entrance.

p.b: Kongu Chōḻa (?) Year 3 = c. 13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
This is a communication from the king to Śivabrāhamaṇas, āṉdār, dēvaraḍiyār, Kaikkōḷa,
Veḷḷāḷa, and uvachchar that they can enjoy the following privileges = to use double conches and the
bērikai drum for both joyous and sorrowful occasions, to build two-storied houses, to use lime paste for
the walls and put up double doors, to wear garlands of lotus flowers and not to pay the forty achchu they
had been paying to the king. The officers who signed the document were Nandipanman, Vayirādarāyaṉ,
Toṇḍaimāṉ, and Kāliṅgarāyaṉ.

l.ta:
m uvacca[r]kkum ōlai kuṭutta paricāvatu ivarkaḷ naṉmai tiṉmaikaḷukku
iraṭṭai caṅku

ūtavum perikai koṭṭavum taṅkaḷ viṭukaḷ iraṇṭu nilai eṭuttuc cāntu
iṭṭukkoḷ-

ḷavum iraṭṭai katavu iṭṭukkoḷḷavum ceṅkaḻunirpūvāl vācikaitoḷmālai
iṭavum i-
varkaḷ nam carakkukku taruvatāṉaaccu nāṟpatuṅ koṇṭu ivarkaḷ makkaḷ c[ey]tu
koḷvārkaḷ āka nam ōlai piṭipāṭāka kuṭuttom

ṉ eḻut[tu i]vai[yirāt]tarāyaṉ eḻuttu toṇṭai[māṉ eḻuttu]
kāliṅkarā[ya]ṉ eḻu[ttu] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1905.)
Karuvūr, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the Moṭṭai-gōpuram of the Pasupatīśvara temple at Karuvūr, right of entrance.

p.b: Local chief Mīnāṭsi nāyakkar, Pārttiva = c. 17th-18th century. A.D.

inscription.p:
One Mīnāṭchi-nāyakkar gave endowment of land for performing worship on all the 360 days of
the year in the Pasupatīśvara temple at Kerpāpuri (i.e. Karur).

l.ta: yil ceṭṭipāḷaiyattil keṟpāpuric cettirattil pacupaticuracu[vā]mi a-

edition.p: Engraved in the beginning with a trident.

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1905.)
Karuvūr, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the Moṭṭai-gōpuram of the Pasupatīśvara temple at Karuvūr, right of entrance.
inscription.p:
Records that Sōmaiya-daṇḍanāyakkar, Koppāḷa-daṇḍanāyakkar, and Siruppaya-
daṇḍanaāyakkar (perhaps Hoysaḷa military captains) jointly made a gift of 50 poṉ to the Tiruvānilai-
nāyaṉār temple at Karuvūr for worship, etc. At the end of the inscription some illegible words (perhaps
signatures) in Kannaḍa script are found.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1905.)
Karuvūr, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the Moṭṭai-gōpuram of the Pasupatīśvara temple at Karuvūr, left of entrance.
inscription.p:
The Śivabrāhmaṇas of the Tiruvānilai-uḍaiya-nāyanār temple at Karuvūr in Veṅgāla-nāḍu
in Vīrachōḻa-maṇḍalam received an amount of 10 Śriyakki-paḻañchalākai achchu from the
Puravuvari officer Āḷavandār Avināsi for food offerings in the temple. It is said that the offerings had
been disrupted so far due to some calamity (kalaham).

l.ta:
kopparakecari paṟmarāṉa tiripuvaṉacakkaravattikaḷ śrīvikki[ra*]ma
coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu

ṟāvatu viraco[ḻa*]maṇṭalattu veṅkalanāṭṭu karuvūṟ
tiruvāṉilaiyāḷuṭaiya nā •••uṭaiya civappirāmaṇarom perumāḷ puravariyāril
āḷavantāṉ aviṉāci •••m nācci •••koyil kuṭaṅ • •

kalakattil cemantappiṉamaiyil ivarai ārā[y*]ntu eḻuntaruḷivittu
ko[ṇ*]ṭuvara tāṉattār kuṭutta paṇam nūṟum inṉāyaṉār paṇṭārattil kuṭutta

putukkācāl paṇam eṇpatum āka paṇa[m] nūṟṟeṇpatum ivar pakkale

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1905.)
Karuvūr, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the Moṭṭai-gōpuram of the Pasupatīśvara temple at Karuvūr, left of entrance.
inscription.p:
Some decision made by the Eṟaraṭṭiyār of Pattippūṇḍi who was the headman (maṇiyam) to fix the
rate on betel gardens in Karuvūr at 15 paṇam only. (A second fragment refers to income from the tax vēli-
poṉ collected for the renovation work).

edition.p: A fragment in 13th century characters fixed nearby.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1905.)
Karuvūr, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the Moṭṭai-gōpuram of the Pasupatīśvara temple at Karuvūr, left of entrance.
inscription.p:
Records a king's order communicated to the temple trustees of Karayūr Tirupāṇḍikkoḍumuḍi in
Koṅgu alias Chōḻakēraḷa-maṇḍalam saying that he made the village of Iḷanagar in Taṭṭaiyūr-nāḍu in the
same maṇḍalam as dēvadāna-iṟaiyili to the temple as requested by his officer Chōḻakōṉ.

tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ īḻamum maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ
koṇṭaruḷi vīrāa

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1905.)
Karuvūr, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On a pillar to the right of entrance into the front gopura of the Pasupatīśvara temple at Karuvūr.

p.b: Local Chief Chokkaliṅga Nāyaka. Paridāvi, Vaigāsi 23 = c. 17th-18th century A.D.

inscription.p:
One Veṅgaḷa-nāyakka who was a pradhāni of Kāraveṭṭi-nāyakka Bommu-nāyakka made a
chattiram (feeding house) at Karuvūr for brāhmaṇa pilgrims. He gave some land in some villages lying
north of Karuvūr for maintaining the chattiram.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1905.)
Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vikritisvara temple at Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr.

p.b: Koṅgu ruler (?) Kōṉērinmaikoṇḍāṉ = c. 12-13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
The king assigned the entire tax revenue of Tiruveñjamākkūḍal to Āḷuḍaiyār temple in the same
village. The officers Vilāḍattaraiyaṉ and Kāḍuveṭṭi signed the document. Also two vēṭṭuva community
members are found as signatories.

l.ta:
āḷuṭaiyār tēvakaṉmikaḷukku namolai kuṭutta paricāvatu tiruveñ[ca*]mākkūṭal
āḷuṭaiyā-

ṟku tiruveñcamākkūṭalaik kuṭuttom ivūrk kuḷakkaṭṭukaḷum •••••
lceṭ[ṭi]kuḷammu-

aṇaiaṭaiccu vāykkālāl pāñcu viḷaiyakkaṭavatuvum melkaraiyi[l*] ne[ṭuṅ]
kuḻiyilaṇaiyāl

kalmā-

kuṭukka ippaṭikku ivolaiyil nam eḻuttu iṭṭu kuṭuttom ippaṭi canti[r]ā-

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1905.)
Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vikritisvara temple at Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr.

p.b: Kongu ruler. Kōṉērinmaikoṇḍāṉ = c. 12th-13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
King's order communicated to the trustees of the Tiruveñjamākkūḍal-nāyaṉār temple: The ūrār
of the village had been giving to the temple a sum of 120 achchu in lieu of the ūrāṇmai-kāṇi of
Vikramachōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. As the temple people asked the king that some land may be assigned
to the temple instead of that money, the king agreed to make some land of six kalam seed capacity in
Uḻuntankuṟichchi alias Muḍikoṇḍachōḻapuram in Veṅgāla-nāḍu as tax-free dēvadāna The taxes that were
exempted were iṟai, puravu, chiṟṟāyam, ēlvaai, ugavai, cheṉṉīrveṭṭi, etc. The official signatories
were Ellāmvalla-chōḻa-mūvēndavēḷān, Vāṇarāyadēvan, Tiyagavinōdadēvaṉ, Chēdirāyadēvan,
Kaḷappāḷarāyaṉ, Kāḍuveṭṭi, and Muṉaiyadaraiyaṉ.

l.ta: tu ūrāṇmaikkāṇi oṟṟi koṇṭu avvūrāṉkaḷ avvaccu śrīpa-
m nam carakkukku naṅkaṉmikaḷ kaikkoṇṭu ivvaccukku viṭṭa ni-

ciyāṉa muṭikoṇṭacoḻapurattu namakkut tiṟappāyc cevakappaṟṟāy varukiṟa
irupattaiyveli nilat-

ti[l*] āṉaikāḻippoyyiṟ kiḻ āṉaikāḻinelvitai kāṟcey nelvitai
mukkalam immukkalattu-

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1905.)
Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vikritisvara temple at Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr.

p.b: Koṅgu Kōṉērinmaikoṇḍāṉ Year 10 = c. 13th-14th century A.D.

inscription.p:
The king converted a village called Muttāyam in Veṅgāla-nāḍu that was lying waste as tax-free
dēvadāna of the temple. A number of taxes exempted are given. The village was named as Rājākkaḷ-
tambirāṉ-nallūr. The officers Kachchirāyaṉ, Viḻuppādarāyaṉ and Kāliṅgarāyaṉ signed the order.

l.ta:
vatti koṉerimai koṇṭāṉ veṅkālanāṭṭu
tiruveñca[m]ākkūṭaluṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil tāṉattāṟku

toṭṭamum kuḷamuṅ kuḷappa(r)rippum uṭpaṭṭanilat

til paḻantevatāṉam paḷḷiccantam nikki nikkiṉi[ṉ*]ṟa nilattukku oṭṭaiccu

veḷaikkāṟar ciṟukkaṉkaḷ kāt[ti]kaippaṭi-

yum cimāṉ makaḻmai nallerutu naṟpacu kāṇam ney eṇṇai uppāyamum
taṟiyiṟai cekkiṟai taṭṭoli pāṭṭamum īḻampuñce pāṟaikkāṇamum

eḻuttu ||§

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1905.)
Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Vikritisvara temple at Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr.
inscription.p: Gift of some tax-free land in Nāgamalai in Veṅgāla-nāḍu by the king.

l.ta: eṭṭām poyil kiḻ mekkaṭaiya nilam nālumā-

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1905.)
Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vikritisvara temple at Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr.
inscription.p:
Gift of a kaḻañju of gold for a lamp by Māṉāparaṇap-pallavaraiyaṉ Piḷḷaittiru of Tiruvēl in
Veṅgāla-nāḍu in Vīrachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: [yu]ṭaiya civappirāma[ṇa]ṉ nāyakaṉ tiruvāḷaṉāṉa etirili coḻapaṭṭaṉ uḷḷi-
ṭṭārum vaṭukaṉ āṇṭāṉāṉa tiruñāṉa campantap paṭṭaṉum ivvaṉai-
vomuñ cantirātittavarai kuṭaṅkoṭu kuccikoṭu pukuvāṉ oru viḷakke

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1905.)
Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vikritisvara temple at Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr.

p.b: Pāṇḍya Jaṭāvarman Sundara-pāṇḍya, Year 6 = c. 14th century A.D.

inscription.p:
The araiyar, pulappar and iḷamāḍam communities of Talaiyūr-nāḍu in Maṇalūr-nāḍu on the
south bank gave some charity deed to the temple making a village called Pāpputtuṟai in their nāḍu as
dēvadāna.

l.ta: nāṭṭut talaiyūr nāṭṭu araiyarkaḷ pulapparkaḷ iḷa-
ivvū[r*] nāṉ kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nattamum kuḷamum kuḷapparippu-
m naṉcey puṉcey meṉokkiya maram kiṉokkiya kiṇa-
vvūṟ kaṭamai vaṟkam uppāyam tiruvācal viṇiyokan tirumukac-
ḻakoṉ eḻuttu i[ppa]ṭikku ivai talaiyūr n[āṭ]-

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1905.)
Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vikritisvara temple at Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr.
inscription.p:
The king assigned the village of Nandaṉūr with all its taxes. The officers Vilāḍattaraiyaṉ and
Kāḍuveṭṭi signed the royal order.

l.ta: āmai tavaḻi meṉokkiya maramum kīḻnokkiya kiṇa-
yam elavai yukavai maṟṟum eppeṟpayiṟp paṭṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1905.)
Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the south wall of the Pannirumuḷi-Amman shrine in the Vikritisvara temple at Veñjamāṅgūḍalūr.

p.b: Pāṇḍya. Sundara-pāṇḍya Year 3 = c. 13th-14th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that one Sundara-pāṇḍiya-dēvaṉ alias Kaṇḍiyadēvar constructed the shrine of the
goddess Paṇṇēimoḻiyār with its ardhamaṇḍapa. Different parts (paḍai) of the structure were uttaram,
eḻutagam, kapōtam, and viyāḷam, besides the pavement stone and the roof tile (taṭṭōḍu).

l.ta:
kopparakecari paṉmarāṉa tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvirapāṇṭiya
tevaṟku yāṇṭu muṉṟāvatu tiruveñcamākkūṭal āḷuṭaiya nāya-

ṉārkoyil tampirāṭṭiyār paṇ[ṇ*]emoḻiyār koyil a[r*]ttamaṇṭapam
uḷpaṭa uttaram eḻutakam kapotam viyāḷam āka paṭainālum pākkallum tiru-

vantipāvum taṭṭoṭum iṭuvittu taḷamum icaippicceṉ cuntarapāṇṭiya
tevaṉāṉa kaṇṭiya tevar ceyvitta taṉmam§ cakatkopālaṉ§

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1905.)
Modakkūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the north wall of the central shrine the Meypporuḷ-nathasvamin temple at Moḍakkūr.

p.b: Kongu Chōḻa Kōṉērinmaikoṇḍāṉ Year 15 = c. 13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
This may be a sequel to the king's order recorded in the Karur inscription above (136/1905). This
is a royal communication to the kaṇmāḷa community of Veṅgāla-nāḍu, Araiya-nāḍu, Iḍaippuḻudi-nāḍu,
Vaḍakarai-Maṇalūr-nāḍu, Taṭṭaiyūr-nāḍu, and Kiḻaṅga-nāḍu granting them the privileges of using
double conches and the bērikai drum for both joyous and sorrowful occasions, wearing chappals, and of
using lime paste for their houses. The officers who signed the document were Viḻuppādarāyaṉ and
Kāliṅgarāyaṉ.

l.ta:
koneriṉmai koṇṭāṉ talaiyūrnāṭu
veṅkālanāṭu araiyanāṭu iṭaippuḻutināṭu vaṭakarai maṇalūrnāṭu
taṭṭaiyūrnāṭu kiḻaṅkanāṭu uḷḷiṭṭa paṟṟil kaṇmāḷarkku patiṉaiñcāvatu
āṭimātam mutal taṅkaḷuk-

ku naṉmaittimaikku iraṭṭaic caṅkum ūtip perikaiyuḷḷiṭṭaṉa
koṭṭivittuk koḷḷavum tāṅkaḷ puṟappaṭaveṇṭum iṭaṅkaḷukku pā

rakṣai
kottukkoḷḷavum taṅkaḷ viṭukaḷukkuc cāntiṭṭuk koḷḷavum coṉṉo-

celvatākat
taṅkaḷukku veṇṭiṉa iṭaṅkaḷile kallilum cempilum veṭṭik ko[ḷka]
ivai viḻuppātarāyaṉ eḻuttu yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1905.)
Modakkūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the south wall of the central shrine the Meypporuḷ-natha-svamin temple at Moḍakkūr.
inscription.p: Built in the beginning. One Kāḍaṉ Māṇikkam of Talaiyūr gave some money for a sandhi lamp.

l.ta: •••virarājentira tevaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉ munṟāvatukku etirāvatu talaiyū[r]

•••peya[ri]l kāṭan mā(ṇ)ṇikkanen ikkoyili[l] kā(ṇ)ṇi uṭaiya
civappirā-

tampim[ār*]kkum

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1905.)
Modakkūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On the south wall of the central shrine the Meypporuḷ-natha-svamin temple at Moḍakkūr.
inscription.p:
Records a king's order to the trustees of many temples in his territory that he had relinquished
the taxes oṭṭachchu, vēṇḍukōḷ, etc that the temples were paying to the government and that they need
not pay them to any of his officers and the local rulers. The temples included those at
Tiruppāṇḍikkoḍumuḍi, Karuvūr, Veñjamākkūḍal, Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Aviṉāsi, Tirumukkūḍal,
Maṉṉiyūr, and others in Naraiyaṉūr-nāḍu, Poṅgalūrkkāl-nāḍu, Talaiyūr-nāḍu, Maṇalūr-nāḍu, Araiya-
nāḍu, Kiḻaṅga-nāḍu, Veṅgāla-nāḍu, etc. in Vaḍa-Koṅgu.

l.ta:
vatti koṉerimaikoṇṭāṉ vaṭakoṅkil naraiyaṉūr nāṭṭu
aṭikkiḻttaḷamum [poṅ]kalūrkkāl kiraṉūr uḷḷiṭṭa ūrkaḷum alaṅkiyam
taṉi •••l poṅkalūrkkālnāṭu talaiyūrnāṭu vaṭakarai maṇalūrnāṭu araiyanāṭu
kiḻaṅkanāṭu veṅkālanāṭu taṭṭaiyūrnāṭu iṭaippuḻutināṭu kā[ṅk]ākavanāṭu
viracoḻavaḷanāṭu valuppunāṭu kuṟupunāṭu

vaṭaparicāranāṭu kāñcikkūvalnāṭu pūnturaiṉāṭu kiḻppāliṉāṭu vaṭakaraināṭu
innāṭukaḷil civa

ṇak kāṇiyāḷaṟkum tānat[tā]rkkum
tiruppāṇṭikkoṭumuṭi tāṉa[ta*]tāṟkum karuvūril tāṉa[ttā]ṟkum
veñcamākkūṭal tāṉattāṟkum tirumurukanpūṇṭit tāṉattāṟkum
aviṉāciāḷuṭaiyār koyil tāṉattāṟku[m] tirumukkūṭal tāṉattāṟkum
maṉṉiyūr [tāṉa*]ttāṟkum namolai kuṭutta(p)paṭi-

kaḷāl uḷḷa oṭṭua[c]cu iḻaṉtom kūttāṭuṉāyanāṟkut tāṅkaḷ
iṭṭukkuṭutta amaippi[ṉ]paṭikku namolai yiṭṭut taṇṭak kaṭavatallavākavum
nāṭupiṭitta cāmantarkaḷukkut tāṅkaḷ kuṭuttuvarum veṇṭukoḷ kuṭukkak
kaṭavarkaḷallavākavum namolai eṭuttuvaru[m]mavarkaḷukku nam-

iṭakkaṭavarkaḷal[lavā]kavum ivarkaḷukku kaiyarici erittiṭak kaṭavarallavākavum
ippaṇam iraṇṭum paṇa[m] kuṭuttuppotum ūrkaḷ iṟuttup
potuvārkaḷākavum itukku namakku ivarkaḷ tanta accu aṟunū[ṟ] • • •

[pā]ṭāka kuṭuttamaikku patuvārar accu tarakkaṭavatallavākavum ippaṭikku
cempiluñ cilaiyilum veṭṭik koḷvārkaḷākavum ippaṭikkup piṭipāṭāka
namolai kuṭuttom ivai toṇ[ṭaimān] eḻuttu ivai kurukularāyaṉ eḻuttu
āṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1905.)
Modakkūr, Karur Taluk, Tiruchirāppaḷḷi District.
On a stone set up in front of the Vighnesvara temple in the same village at Moḍakkūr.
inscription.p:
Refers to some bad incident involving the death of some person taking poison and some decision
made by the temple trustees invoking a copper-plate charter supposed to be available at
Perumbaṟṟappuliyūr (Chidambaram). Refers to some Mahabharatha related story of poisonous pond
(nachchuppoykai).

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1905.)
Buḍḍireḍḍipaṭṭi, Harur Taluk, Selam District.
On the central shrine of the ruined Amman temple near Buḍḍireḍḍipaṭṭi.

p.b: Vijayanagar Sāḷuva Dammadēva-mahārāya (Narasimha II), Raudri, Tai 1 = 1500 A.D.

inscription.p:
For the merit of Tipparasa-uḍaiyār, who was an executive agent of Narasaṇa-nāyakkar, who was
himself the executive agent (kāryattukkukaḍavar) of the king, one Tāttireḍḍiyār gave the tax income of a
village called Maṉṉayavārpaṭṭi, a hamlet of Tāḻainattam in Kundāṇi-sīrmai which was his
nāyakkattaṉam.

edition.l:
śrīgaṇādhipatayenama [||*] svasti samasta mahārācātirāca rācaparamēcura uttira dhakṣiṇa
pūṟuvapaccima catusamuttiṟātipati nāraci[m]hatEva mahāvaracu[ guḷa]
kumāra[ka]mma[teva]mahārāyar piṟitivirācciyam paṇṇā [ni]ṉṟa kālattil
avarkāryattukku kaṭavarāṉa naracaṇa nāyakkar kāriyattukku kaṭavarāna tipparaca
uṭaiyaṟku tammāka iravuttiri varusam [tai] mācam1§kku ātivārattil ma[ka]ra
caṅkiṟā[n*]ti puṇṇiya kālattil tāttiraṭṭiyār nammuṭaiya nāyakkattaṉamāṉa
kuntāṇic cīrmaiyāṉa kāyalūrt stataḷamāṉa tāḻainattattilp piṭākaiyāṉa
maṉṉayavārpaṭṭi tāḻai nattattu u[ṭaiyār]kum nācci[yā]rkkum
tirunantāviḷakkukkum naiv[et]tiyattukkumāka kuṭutta

l.ta:
taṉmacātaṉam antak kiṟāmattukkuc cellum nañcai puñcai nāṟpāṅkellai
meṉokkiya maram kīḻṉokkiya kiṇaṟum māvaṭaimaravaṭai •••••

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1905.)
Tripurāntakam, Marakapur Taluk.
On the north wall of the kitchen called vantamiddi in Tripurānthakēśvara temple at Tripurānthakam.
inscription.p: Damaged. Mentions construction of a tank.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 201 of 1905.)
Tripurāntakam, Marakapur Taluk, Kurnool District.
On the north wall of the kitchen called vantamiddi in Tripurānthakēśvara temple at Tripurānthakam.

p.b: Chōḻa Rajēndra III Year 15, Raudra, Tulā, Purva 13, Monday, Rēvati = 1260, Oct. 18.

inscription.p:
Records that a horse merchant of Malai-maṇḍalam, called Iravi Mūrtti Nāyakkaṉ made a flower
garden with a well at Tripurāntakam in Moḍapaḷḷ-nāḍu of Vēṅgai-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvana[ cakra] vattikaḷ iruvirapāṇṭiyar muṭittalai koṇ[ṭa*]ru[ḷi]ya
mahārājādhirāja narapati śrīrājēndracoḻateva[r]ku yāṇṭu patiṉaiñcāvatu
raudri-

l.ta:
yum
peṟṟa revati nāḷ [ma]lamaṇṭalattu kutiraicceṭṭikaḷil [kurālam] ira[vi]mu-

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1905.)
Tripurāntakam, Marakapur Taluk, Kurnool District.
On the north face of a Nandi-pillar, at the entrance into the dark room (chikatimidde) in the
Tripurānthakēśvara temple at Tripurānthakam.
inscription.p: In Tamil verse. Mentions a certain Kīrtinārāyaṇa-Vēḷāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 317 of 1905.)
Pushpagiri Vallur (Mandala) Taluk, Kadapa District Andhra State.
On a broken slab lying on the steps leading to the river from the eastern gopura of the Sandana Mallēśvar
temple at Pushpagiri.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to refer to the contents of the inscription No. 316 of 1905. Consecration
of the god Kēśavadēva on the southern side of the Rudrapāda temple and a grant of land at Vallūru by a
Pradhāni officer of the king.

l.katr: ttāṁ vāyōharētu vasundarām shashṭim varsha sahaśrāṇi

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 323 of 1905.)
Chintalapatturu (nearPushpagiri) Vallur (Mandala) Taluk, Kadapa District.
On the four corner slabs built into the inside of the dome of the antarala-mandapa of the Indranāthvara
temple.,
inscription.p:
In Grantha and Tamil charecters. These are in five fragments probably of the same inscription
The seond (B) mentions the Gōḷagi-maṭha.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1905.)
Upparapalle Chennur (Mandal) Taluk, Kadapa District Andra State.
On a pillar of the mandapa in front of the ruined Siva temple called Mobbude-valam near Upparapalle.

p.b: Saka 1181, Siddhārtti, Vaikasa, su. Purnima, Monday (irregular) = 1259 A.D.

inscription.p:
Grantha and Tamil scripts. Mentions the construction of the maṇḍapa by the son of Pālebhaṭṭu
Narasimhabhaṭṭa of Kauśika-gōtra at Cheṟuvanūr.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 381 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sundara Varada-Perumal temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p: Unfinished. Mentions Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 382 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Sundara Varada-Perumāḷ temple at
Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Gift of 90 sheep for a lamp by a merchant of Kīrttimārttāṇḍa-Kālapr[i*]ya aṅgāḍi,
in Siṟu-Timiri-nāḍu, a district of Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam. The diety is called Kīrttimārttāṇḍa-Kālapr[i*]ya
dēva. Some maṇṟāḍis took charge of the sheep.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] kovirājakecarivanmakku yāṇṭu 6 āvatu paṭuvūr koṭṭattu
ciṟutimiri ṉāṭṭu kirttimāttāṇṭa kā[ i-

priya] ttaṅkāṭi vyāpāri pati[mmā]ṟaṉ kumaraceṭṭi makaṉ campakkaṉ
kirttimāttāṇṭak kāipriyadēvarkku nantāviḷakkukku vai-

l.ta:
tta cāvāmūvā perāṭu toṇṇūṟu kaiyuṟṟom kaṇṇantaiyāṉa eḻunūṟṟuva
maṉṟāṭiyu-

m koṉuḻāṉ ca[ka]tevaṉum nalluḻāṉ ainnūṟṟuva maṉṟāṭiyum kaṭampa[ntai]
nampaṉum pā[ḷi]kaṉ

mūlaṉum kumarantai paṭṭaṉum koṉuḻāṉ kavaṟai maṉṟāṭiyum kumarantevikiyum
teruvaṉ tirumā

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 383 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Sundara Varada-Perumāḷ temple at
Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
This inscription records a private agreement among certain merchants, that on marriage and other
occasions, a certain amount of money should be set apart to meet the repairs of the Kēśava-Perumāḷ
temple at Kāvēripākkam, alias Vikrama-chōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. The merchants are referred to as
Vaisya-vāṇikar Dharma-tāvaḷa Abhaya-tavaḷa-nagarattār.

l.gr:
śubhamastu svastiśrī [||*] man mahāmaṇḍalēśvaran harihararāya vipāṭa
bhāṣai

l.ta:
maṅkalattu nakarattār naṭutteruvil
kecavapperumāḷ koyilukku itteruvil vai

ṉam eṅkaḷ teruvil
kalliyāṇappaṇam yāttiraippaṇam kaiviṭu motirappaṇam [pūṭaca]poṉ vakai
pattukku mūṉṟupaṇam inta vakaippaṭiyiluḷḷatu eṅkaḷ teruvil
kecavapperumāḷ kōyilukku tiruppaṇikkum

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 384 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Sundara Varada-Perumal temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Fragment. Mentions Karaivaḻi-Kandapurappēṭṭai in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam and the nagaram of
Lōkamahādēvipuram. Provisions for food offerings from the interest of gold.

l.ta: yamutu potu añciṉukkum patteṭṭuk kuttalarici nicatam mūṉṟu sa
yir nicata mūṉṟu nāḻiyāka rājakecariyāle mū[ve]ḷaikku

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 385 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sundara Varada-Perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,

p.b:
Vijaya-nagara Vēnkaṭapatidēva-Maharaya Śaka 1514, Nandana, Makara, Purva 12, Hasta
(irregular) = 1592 A.D.

inscription.p:
Registers certain arrangements made for celebrating a festival (in the temple) of Aḻagiyarāma-
Perumāḷ. The donors were some adhikāris.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 386 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south and west walls of the central shrine in the Sundara Varada-Perumal temple at
Kāvēripākkam.

p.b:
Vijaya nagara Vīra-Kampana-Uḍaiyār, Saka 1292, Sādhārana, Kumbha, Purva 7, Rōhiṇi,
Saturday = 1370, Feb. 3.

inscription.p:
Mentions the free village (taṉiyūr) of Kāvēripākkam, alias Vikrama-chōḻa-Chaturvēdimaṅgalam
in Paduvūr-kōṭṭam, a district of Jayaṅgoṇḍa Chōḻa-maṇḍalam. Records the sale of their hamlet Kaḍapēri
in Kalavaippaṟṟu by the śabha of the village to some Brāhmaṇas for the price of 400 vīrachampan-
kuḷikai.

l.ta:
śrīvīrapokkaṇṇa
uṭaiyar kumārar śrīvīrakampaṇa uṭaiyākkuc cellā niṉṟa

miyum peṟṟanāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu paṭuvūr koṭṭattu
taṉiyūr kāverippākkamāṉa

ṇam paṇṇik
kuṭutta paricāvatu emmūr melpiṭākai immaṇṭalattu ikkoṭṭattu

kalavaip paṟṟu kaṭapperikku ellai kīḻpāṟkellai kāverippākkattu
kīḻnīrkālukku melkarai taṇṭukaraikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai ikkālukku
vaṭakku tiruppāṟkaṭal taṇṭukaraikku vaṭakkum melpāṟkkellai veḷ[ḷa]n
tāṅkalukku kiḻakkum kaṭapperiyil niṉṟu peruṅkāvanallūrkku pokiṟa vatikku
kiḻakkum

melpāṟkellai naṅkaiāṇṭāṉpaṭṭi ellaikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
āṟṟaṟaip peruvaḻikku teṟkum intaṉāṟpāṟkkellaikku uṭpaṭṭa nilattil
tevatāṉam tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam piṭārippaṭṭi nīṅkalāka nattameṭu kuḷakkaṭṭu
nañcai puñcaiērivāy puṟavaṭai avaṉinārārāyaṇaṉ peruṅkōyil
nīrviḻukkāṭum maṟṟum epper-

kaḷum uṭppaṭṭa intakaṭapperikku emmilicainta
vīracampaṉ kuḷikai pa

ippaṇam nāṉūṟṟukkum intakaṭapperi vilai
āvatākavum ippaṭiviṟṟuk kuṭutta kaṭapperi ivarkaḷukku viṟṟu oṟṟi

ṉaṅkaḷukkum urittākak kaṭavatākavum ippaṭi vilaikkuuṟa
viṟṟupporuḷaṟak kaikkoṇṭu viṟṟu vilai

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 387 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Varadarāja-Perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p: Gift of tax income from a land for the celebration of a festival called Sundarapāṇḍiyan śandi.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 388 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Varadharaja perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Damaged. In two fragments. A Stops with date. B Seems to record some land gift for the festival
of Sītā-Kalyāṇam. Śaka, Year wrong in both (1551-Sarvadāri, 1569-Virōdi).

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 389 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central Shrine of the Varadarāja Perumāḷ Temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Registers a tax-free gift of land to the temple of Aḻgiyaperāmāḷ at Kāvirippākkam alias Vijaya
Gaṇḍagōpala chaturvēdi maṅgalam by Saḷukki Nārayanaṉ Alluvīramarasan. The land was made as
kuḍinīṅgā-tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam, as registered in uḷvari of the sabhā of the village.

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 390 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine of the Varadaraja perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Kampana uḍaiyār, son of Vīa Bokkana uḍaiyār, Śaka 1295, Āṉaṉda, Karkaṭaka,
Apara 11, Wednesday, Kāttigai = 1374 July 5.

inscription.p:
Part-A mentions sale of house-sites by three Brahmana indivituals. Part-Bis related to the above
though continuation is broken. The village Kāvidippākkam is mentioned as a taṉiyūr (independent
village).

l.ta:
tipati
śrī[pok]kaṇṇa uṭaiyār kumārar kampaṇauṭaiyārkkuc cellā niṉṟa

• • ttevaperumāḷ karuṇākara nampiyum aiyakkimaṇṭalapuruṣa ulakanāta paṭṭarum
uṟuppaṭṭūr

māṇam paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu keṅkaikoṇṭacoḻac ceri
melkūṟṟil karuṇāka • • •

ttukku inta nilaṅkaḷukku vilaiyāka viṟṟu
ivvūril taṭṭāril eḻāyirat • • •

•••ttaṭiyil tayāmukacceriyil puṉṉaikkuṇṭilukku teṟkku tevatānattukku
vaṭakkum picciceṟu uṭpaṭa taṭi eṭṭum poṉvāriyaṉ kālukku meṟkku
ulakanāta paṭṭar [kaṟketapā]ṭakam periyaperumāḷ kalikku pāṭakam iraṇṭum
ākataṭi patiṉoṉṟum itukku uḷḷa sakala

vatu tirumettaḷi teruvil teṉciṟakil muṭupaivaḷaiyyaḻakiyār maṉaikku teṟkkum
maṉaikku paṉṉiraṭik kolāl kiḻmel eṇkolt taraiyum teṉvaṭal kol
eṇkolum itukku uṭpaṭṭa paṭappaiyum inta maṉaiyum ittaṭi

•••ṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu paṭuvūr koṭṭattu taṉiyūr kāvitippākkamāṉa
vikkiramacoḻac catu
m ippaṇam aimpatukkum viṟṟuk kuṭukka inta

urittākavatākavum inta [ni]laṅkaḷukku epperpaṭṭak kalaṉ
illaikalaṉuḷavāṉāl nāṅkaḷ tīttu kuṭukka kaṭavomākavum ivarkku viṟṟu
kuṭuttamaṉaikku ellaiā • • •

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 391 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On a stone built into the floor close to the south wall of the central shrine in the Varadarāja-Perumāḷ
temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
The record registers an endownment of 736 kaḻañju of gold to the assembly Kāvadipākkam
alias vaṇinārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimangalam present at the hall called Chiṟṟambalam by a lady called
Nambirāṭṭi (queen ?) the elder sister of one Tiruvānaṅgāmudi for the purpose feeding one guest
(viruttūṇar) daily.
(Published in SII., Vol. XII No. 104.)

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 392 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa of the Varadarāja perumāḷ Templeat Kāvēripākkam,

p.b:
Vijayanagar, Mallikārjunarāyar Śaka 1381, Pramāti, Kanni, Purva, ekadasi, Saturday,
Tiruvōṇam = 1459. Sept. 8.

inscription.p:
A big meeting of the shabā and accountants of the taṉiyūr Kavidipākkam decided in the presence
of Narasiṅa rāya-uḍaiyār regarding the sharing of the entire village property apportioned into sixty shares
(paṅgu). The conditions of enjoyment of the shares is given in detail. Kavidipākkam
alias Vikkiramachōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam was in Paḍuvūr-koṭṭam of Jayaṅgoṇḍa chōḻamaṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 393 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the slab in the south wall of the first prakāra of Varadarāja Perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Fragment. Refers to the accountants (karanṇattāṉ-madyastaṉ) of the different committees
(samvatsara-vāriyam, tōṭṭa-vāriyam and ēri-vāriyam) of the village sabhā, attesting to the document.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 394 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk,
inscription.p: shrine in the Varadarāja Perumāl temple at Kāvēripākkam, North Arcot District.
On a stone built into the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central.

Frament. The date is partly lost. It mentions the assembly of Kāvidippākkam alias
Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and of Viḍēlviḍuku •••in Māṅgāḍu-nāḍu, a subdivision of
Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam.
(Published in SII Vol. XII No. 82.)

l.note: Letters are engraved below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 395 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south wall (the north wall?) of the mandapa in front of the Varadaraja Perumal temple at
Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Fragment. Mentions Kā[vi]dippākkam alias Avaninārāyaṇa chaturvēdimaṅgalam and ‘the great
men of the kuḍumbu committee’. The ‘tank committee’ undertook to look after a lamp gift.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 396 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Varadarāja Perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Fragment, Seems to relate to some tax-free land gift. A madhyastha wrote the document by the
orders of the sabhā (perumakkaḷ).

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 397 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On a stone built into the ceiling of the maṇḍapa in front of the Varadarāja Perumāḷ temple at
Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Fragment. Gift of gold for burning a lamp in a matha. The ūr-āḷvāṉ officer was to supervise the
gift.
(Published in SII Vol. XII. No. 79.)

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 398 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the ceiling of the maṇḍapa in front of the Varadarāja perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
This is a fragment. Seems to register an endowment of 249 kaḻñju of gold by certain
Kumārakramavittan.
(Published in SII Vol. XII = 98.)

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 399 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Varadarājaperumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p: Much damaged. Mentions (the temple called) Tiruppaṉrīśvaram.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 400 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On another pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Varadarāja perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p: [Published in SII, XIII, No. 312, pp. 165-6, where the king is tentatively taken to be Rājarāja I]

The sabhā of Kavidippākkam alias Avaṉinārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam,
comprising the members of ūr-vāriyam, udāsīna-vāriyam, ēri-vāriyam, and kaḻani-vāriyam, the bhaṭṭas
and the viśishṭas met at the Kāmakkōṭṭam and decided, as directed by the officer Veṇpaikkuḍināṭṭu-vēḷār
delegated by the king, that they should not carry out any of their routine activities (karaṇam) on the days
of saṃgrānti, new moon and full moon; and on other days too, the activities should not be performed
along with women representing their husbands. In the later case, the concerned male members were
bound to be present for the particular proceedings. This decision was ordered to be executed under the
supervision of the ‘ruler of the village’ (ūr-mēl niṟkum-kō) and the members of the samvatsara-vāriyam.

edition.l: kapperumakkaḷum kaḻaṉivārikapperumakkaḷum[ bhaṭṭarka] ḷum

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 401 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On another pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Varadarāja perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p: Fragment. Mentions some committees of the sabhā of Avaninārāyanachathurvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 402 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the forth pillar in the maṇḍapa in front of the Varadarāja perumāḷ temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Mentions one Poṉpaṟṟi uḍaiyān Śirisaraṇa perumāḷ of Karuvūr in Miḻalaikūṟṟam in Paṇḍināḍu as
the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 403 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On a slab setup in front of the masjid at Kāvēripākkam,

p.b:
Qutb Shah, Sultān Abdulla Pātaśāha, Śaka 1591, Kīlaka, Margaśira, Su 10, Friday =
November 22, 1669 A.D.

inscription.p:
In Telugu language and script. Records the building of the masjid at Kāvēripāka in Killē
Pargaṇa. It is said the construction was started in Hijri 1077 and completed in 1078. At the top of the slab
is an inscription in Persian, a counterpart of the present inscription. [This is discussed elaborately by
Z.A. Desai in ‘A Note on the Telugu Inscription of Abdulla Qutb Shah from Kaveripakkam’, Śrinidhih =
Shri K.R. Srinivasan Festschrift, 1983, pp. 311-22.]

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 404 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
inscription.p: At the foot of the entrance into the Amman Shrine in the Koṅkanīśvar temple at Kāvēripākkam,
Fragments. Mentions a liquid measure called anavarathan and a Śrikāryam Pūchakkarakēśavan.

No. 187.
(A.R. No. 405 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Muktīśvara temple at Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Damaged. Mentions perhaps a village gift by Sengēṉi Ammaiappan [Vīramiṇḍan]
alias Attimallan Vikrama-Chōḻa-Sambuvarāyan, to the temple of Abhimuktīśvaram at Kāviripākkam in
Paḍuvūrkōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa maṇḍalam.

No. 188.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
inscription.p: At the entrance into the gōpurā of the Muktīśvara Temple at Kāvēripākkam,

This is a fragmentary inscription. It registers a gift of five kaḻañju of gold, by the daughter-in-law
(marumagaḷ) of the chief Ayyakki Pangaḷa Aḍigaḷ.
[Published SII, XII No. 50.]

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 407 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On a stone built into the bund of the tank in Kāvēripākkam,

p.b: Chōḻa Parakēśarivarman, Year 10 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p: Fragment. Mentions the temple of Tiruvakkīśvaram.

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 408 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On another stone built into the bund of the tank in Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p: A fragment of the historical introduction of the king.

No. 191.
(A.R. No. 409 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On another stone built into the bund of the tank in Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Mutilated. Register an endowment for offerings and for burning a perpetual lamp. Mentions the
sluice (kumiḻi) committee of Kāvēripākkam, and a decision to collect uṇaṅgal (voluntary offerings) for
feeding and lamp service in the temple of Mahādēva.

Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On a fourth stone built into the bund of the tank in Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Fragment. Appears to register an endowment for offerings and for burning a perpetual lamp
entrusted with a committee called chiṟṟūrāṭchi-cheyvār.

No. 194.
(A.R. No. 412 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On one of the inscribed stones kept in the Public Works Department bungalow in Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Fragments. Seems to contain a śaka date, the first figure of which is 8 and the second 2, while
the third is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 413 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On another stone kept in the Public Works Department bungalow in Kāvēripākkam,
inscription.p:
Fragment. Mentions offerings to a deity called Tirunaṭṭapperumānaḍigal in Kāvidippākkam in
Paḍuvur-koṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 414 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On third stone kept in the Public Works Department bungalow in Kāvēripākkam.
inscription.p:
In Grantha and Sanskrit. Later part broken. Mentions something (details lost) done by a Chōḻa
feudatory named Parāntakap-pallavaraiyaṉ of Naṭār in Umbaḷa-nāḍu, son of Mumuṭichōḻa-Umpaḷa-nāṭṭu-
Mūvēntavēḷān, and grandson of Chempiyaṉ-Umpaḷa-nāṭṭu-Mūvēntavēḷāṉ. He was a favourite of King
Rājarāja.

No. 197.
(A.R. No. 415 of 1905.)
Kāvēripākkam, Arakkōṇam Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the fourth stone kept in the Public Works Department bungalow in Kāvēripākkam,'

p.b: Chōḻa Kulottuṅga chōḻa = c. 12th Century A.D.

inscription.p: Fragment. Mentions areca nut and betel leave offerings.

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 416 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Gudimallur.
inscription.p:
Registers a gift of land with tax exemption to the temple of Kadāraṅgoṉḍa chōḻīśvaramuḍaiya-
Nāyinār in Karaivaḻi-Valliyūr, a village of Kalavaippaṟṟu in Paḍuvūrkoṭṭam. It is said that as the channel
from the river to the village Kandāpuri-pēṭṭai Tenkāl became useless (iṟantapaṭiyālē) some lands of the
temple running through the flower garden were used to dig a new channel and for the same some lands
were given to the temple in exchange.

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 417 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Sadāsivadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 1472, Sādhāraṇa, Karkaṭaka, Purva 5, Monday,
Hasta (irregular) = 1550 A.D.

inscription.p:
Mentions Kalavaippaṟṟu in Perundimiri-nāḍu, a sub-division of Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam. Records that
ūravar of four villages, Mukam Vaṉṉivēḍu, Valli-ūr, Tiruvēkampachēri and Tiruvēlai-chēri included in
Kāveripākkam-sīrmai gave consent to the ūravar of Chekanāta-nallūr alias Pūṇḍi in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu for
digging river channel passing through their villages. This is said to be made for the merit of Krishnappa
Nāyakka son of Vayyappa-Nāyakkar and Pappu-Nāykkar, son of Vīrappa-Nāyakkar Nayiṉappa-
Nāyakkar of Vēlūr.

l.ta:
maṇṭalecuvara metiṉimīcura kaṇṭakaṭṭāri
cāḷuva catāci[va] tevamakā irāyar piṟutivirācciyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa
cakāṟttam

ṉ mel cellā niṉṟa cātāraṇa varuṣam ka[ṭaka] nāyaṟṟu
pūṟuvapakṣattu pañcamiyum comavāramum peṟṟaa

koṭṭattu peruntimiri nāṭṭu kalavaiypaṟṟu karaivaḻi pālāṟu vaṭapurivāṉa
kāveripā[kka*]ttu cir

ai[yi]l mukamvaṉṉiveṭu pallavarā(rrā)irum ciapparum
paḻantiparāku(p)parum taṇṭalam kiḻār vīraperumāḷum vāṇātarā[ya*]r ellapparum
vaṇṇakkicuratevarum tevantaiyār peṟṟaperumāḷum toṇṭai

[nayi]ṉār maṇṇaiappanayiṉār koyil tāṉa[t*]tār ñānamuttiy[ā*]r
puvanaṅkāttārum nāṟppatte[ṇ]ṇāyirapaṭṭar aracapparum kākkunā[yaka*]paṭṭar
ellapparum tirumalaipaṭṭaru[m*] maṟṟumuḷḷa tāṉattārum kaikkoḷamutalikaḷ
ce[viṭa]kaviṇṭarum atirā

yik koṭṭattu kalavaippaṟṟu [ka]raivaḻi nā[ṭ*]ṭu cekanātanallūrāṉa pūṇṭi
kaṭampuḻār kuruvīṉār maṟṟu uḷḷa ūravaṟku taṉmacātaṉam paṇṇikuṭuttapaṭi
taṅkaḷ kiṟāmattukku āṟṟukāl toṇṭaveṇṭukaiyil yiṟṟai nāḷāl
nāya[k*]kar vayyappanāya[k*]kar ayyaṉ kumāra kiṣṇappanāya[k*]kar
ayyaṉavarkaḷukku puṇṇiya

kkar nayiṉappa nāyakkar pappuṉāyakkaṟku puṇṇiyamākavum ciṉṉapommu
nāyakka[ru]m tippunāyakkarum karaivaḻi[kīḻ] kiṟāmattil uṇṭāṉa mutalikaḷum
valliūrāka uṭaiyār tiruvaṉanticuramuṭaiya nayiṉār caṉṉatiyil vaṉtiruntu
kāl toṇṭuvikka kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭapaṭi eṅkaḷ ellaiyile kāl toṇṭik
koṇṭu poṟattukku ellai kuṟitta viparam kālukkut talaippe[ḻai] vaṉṉi veṭu
ellaiyle [paṅko] •••••

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 418 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.
inscription.p:
Records that the chief Sambuvarāyan Vīrachampan remitted taxes on artisans and merchants
living in the dēvadana lands in favour of the Kiḍāraṅkoṇḍa Chōḻīśvaramuḍaya-Nāyinār temple at
Malliyūr. He also gifted 7500 kuli of land in several places free of taxes.

l.ta:
vatu patiṉnālāvatu nāḷ irunūṟṟu irupa[ti]l eḻutiṉa tirumuka[p*]paṭikku
malliyūruṭai-
yār kiṭāraṅkoṇṭa co[ḷī][cu]ramuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil tāṉattāṟku iṉā-
yaṉār[k*]ku paḻaiya tevatāṉamāṉa tirumaṭaiviḷākam malliyūr tirupuva[ṉa*]-
cuntaraviḷākamum nirkāriyaṉ tāṅkalum naṉcey puñcey nāṟpāṟ[ke]-
llaikku[m*] uḷpaṭṭa nilamum ivvūril ēṟum puṟakalaṉai kuṭiceṭ[ṭi]-

kku kuṭima[k*]kaḷ maṟṟum ēṟum puṟakalaṉai kuṭima[k]kaḷ perāl kaṭam[ai]yu[m*]
palavarikaḷum āyamum kā[cāya]mum

ammaṇantāṅkalu[m] kātti[kai*] poṉ vicaiya[rā]yaṉ tāṅkalum malliyarā[ya*]
[tāṅ]kalu[m*] iv[vū]rkaḷ

nañcey [pu]ñcey nāṟpāṟkellaiin uḷpaṭṭa nilamum peṭṭai vaṭakālil
[ni]lam nālu veli[yu]m peṭṭai te-

ṉ kālil nilam iraṇṭuveliyum toḻkuḷattūr pi[ṟi]nta viracampaviḷākattu
nila[n] eṭṭu [ve]liyum •••nilam

iraṇṭu veliyum vaṉṉipeṭu pi[ṟi]ntu viracampa viḷāka[m] nilam aiñcu veliyum
vāca[lū]ṇi nilam iraṇṭu ve • • •

viḷakku oṉṟukku kuḻi [ai]nnūṟu āka vaṉṉipeṭṭil viṭṭa kuḻi āyiramum
peṭṭai [vaṭakā]lil viṭṭa kuḻi [ā]yiramum peṭ[ṭai]teṉ-

kālil viṭṭa kuḻi aiñnūṟum koḷattūril viṭṭa kuḻi āyiramum [a]mmaṇan
kālil viṭṭa [ku]ḻi [nā]lāyiramum āka kuḻi e[ḻā*]yirattu

ainnūṟum caṟuva[mā]ṉiya iṟaiyiliyāka naṭakka[vu]m colli viṭṭom
ittaṉma[m] cantirātittavarai naṭakkumpaṭi ka[llilum]

cempilum veṭṭi koḷḷa[vu*]m inta taṉmattukku virotam paṇṇiṉār
uṇṭākil keṅkai iṭai kumari iṭai kurā-

[ṟ]pacu koṉṟār pāvaṅ koḷvāṉ ippaṭi cammatittu [ca]ṟuvamāṉiyamāka
kuṭutteṉ ca[mpu]varāyaṉ vīracampaṉeṉ

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 419 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.

p.b:
Pāṇḍya Māṟavarman Kulasekharadeva, Year 12, Mesha, Purva 14, Friday, Aviṭṭam =
1280/1325 A.D.

inscription.p:
The inscription is partly built in and so some portions are missing. This records a decision taken in
a meeting of the nāṭṭār and agampaḍi-nāṭṭār of Kalavaippaṟṟu, Āndi-nāḍu, Attippaṟṟu, and
Murugamangalappaṟṟu comprising the Valangai and Iḍangaijātis, who met in the courtyard called
Gengaikoṇḍchōḻan-tirukkāvaṇam in the temple of Kiḍārangoṇḍachōḻīsuvaram at Valliyūr in Karaivaḻi in
Kalavaippaṟṟu in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam in Jayangoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The big gathering wanted to punish a
notorious culprit called Kollattaraiyaṉ who had become a traitor to god and king. The latter was hiding in
the vimāna roof (tirumalai) of the temple and so the nāṭṭār wanted to dismantle the roof to catch the
culprit. As the temple trustees and functionaries (tānattār, māhēcurar, kavarippiṇā, poṟkōyil kaikkōḷar,
śrivīrapattirar) objected to this, the nāṭṭār got permission from them for dismantling the roof by
promising to renovate it and restore the worship by contributing specified quantity of paddy on
landholdings and some money contribution on looms, merchandise, etc.

l.ta:
vatu
paṉṉiraṇṭāvatu mEṣanāyaṟṟup pūṟuvapakṣattu catutteciyum
veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa aviṭṭattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacOḻamaṇṭalattu

valliyūr uṭaiyār kiṭāraṅkoṇṭacoḷicuramuṭaiya nāyiṉār kōyil

muṟukaṉmaṅkalappaṟṟu nāṭṭārum akampaṭiyārumāṉa nāṭṭārum maṟṟum
valaṅkaikkaṭaitta cātiyuḷḷārum iṭaṅkaikkaṭaitta cā-

varukaiyil ivaṉ

kollumaḷavukkut tirumalaiyai [iṟakki] kolla vēṇṭumeṉṟu collukaiyil
ikkō[yil] mākēcurar tāṉattār kavarippiṇā poṟkōyiṟ kaikkōḷarum

tirumalaiyai iṟakkavoṇṇāteṉṟu collukaiyil tirumalai[yiṟakki]*

kalanellu araippaṇamum puñceppaṟṟukku ēroṉṟukkuk kalatā[ṉiyam]*

naṭattik kuṭukkak •••••

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 420 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.
inscription.p:
Registers a remission of taxes by the chief in favor of all the temples (palataḷi) at Karaivaḻi-
Kalavaip paṟṟu from his seventh, Year, as those temples were experiencing difficulty to conduct worship
until the sixth, Year. The remission order is addressed to the nāṭṭār of Kalavaippaṟṟu.

l.ta:
cakaloka cakkaravatti irāca nārāyaṇan campuvarāyan kalavaip paṟṟu
nāṭṭārkku taṅkaḷ paṟṟil karaivaḻi kala[vai*]ppaṟṟil palataḷi pūcaippātikku
koḷḷum vari āṟāvatu varaiyum koṇṭaittāle pūcaiyum samārātanaiyum
tiruppaṇiyum naṭavātapaṭiyāle eḻāvatu mutalukku

aḷaveṟṟam uḷppaṭa pūcaippāti uḷḷa nilattukkuk koḷḷum vari kaḻittu im
mutal pūcaikkum camārātanaikkum tiruppaṇikkum kuṭutta aḷavukku tānattārkku
eṟa pūcaippātiyāl uḷḷa nilam aḷavu[eṟ]ṟam uḷppaṭa pātiyākap pakuntu
kuṭuttu

iṟaiiliyāka cantirātittavaraiyum cella kalluveṭṭik
kuṭuttut tāḻvaṟa naṭattippotavum coṉṉom [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 421 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.
inscription.p:
Registers an order of Bommu-Na[ya]naLingama-Nāyakarexempting taxes on the looms of the
Kaikkōḷa weavers to conduct Chittiraifestivalin the temple. The taxes had to be paid to the temple at
reduced rates. Some concession is also given if the river water feeding the local tank becomes scarce.

l.ta:
śrīmatu pommuṉāyiṉaliṅkama ṉāyakkar
valliyūr kaikkoḷaṟku taṟikkaṭamai varikkuk kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭatu mātam

kālveṭṭumāṉiyattil pūṟuvam nam kāṇi oṇṇupam kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭu cittirai
tiruṉāḷukku anta[mi]nel taṟiellām māṉiyamākavum maṇṇaiyārkaḷum
āṟṟukkāl taṇ[ṇi]

[na]ṉaittu vantakāltaṇṇi ku[ṟ*]aintu [pā]yavum āṟṟukkāl taṇṇikuḷattukku
vārāmalyiru[n]tāl kaṇṇan taṟivarāmalirukkavum yintapaṭi kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom
cittirai tiruṉā[ḷ] naṭantā[l*] cittirai

mātam taṟi ellām caṟuvamāṉiyamāka mātam oṉṟukku taṟikku koyilukkum
paṇṭārattukkum araippaṇamākavum kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom ūṟkaṇakku
yiruṉik[ki*]ṉāṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 422 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.
inscription.p:
Refers to a quarrel (chiliku) between the right and left and castes which lasted for four, Years and
consequent desertion of the temple streets. To encourage resettlement, tax concession was offered to the
new settlers.

l.ta: ṣam tai mātam mutal tiyati valliyūr uṭaiyār ma[ṇ*]ṇai nāyanār
[ku*]ṭi ēṟakkaṭavarkaḷ ākavum kuṭi ēṟi iṭattu taṟikkaṭamaivācal paṇam
aiñcu paṇam koḷḷakkaṭavatu ākavum paṭṭaṭai nāṇayattuku pattil
aiñcu māṉiyattu āṇṭu oṉṟukku taṟioṉṟukku oru paṇam ko[ḷḷavum]
maṟṟum uḷḷa cakala upātikaḷum uṭpaṭa āṇṭoṉṟukku taṟi oṉṟukku • • •

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 423 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.

p.b: Pāṇḍya Jatāvarman Perumāl Sundara-Pāṇḍya dēva = c. 13th Century A.D.

inscription.p: Built in the beginning. Fragment. Seems to record a gift of land.

l.ta:
• • vaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī koṟcaṭa paṉmarāṉa perumāḷ cuṉtarapāṇṭiyatevaṟku
yāṇṭu

kaṭampaṅkuṇṭil amaraṅkuṇṭil teṉ varampu •••••

•••mum iṅku viṭṭukkiḻakku cikāḻināṭa[ṉ] vaitta amaṇpaṭṭi kuḻi irunūṟṟu
aim[pa*]tum kūṭiṉālu •••••

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 424 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south and east walls of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.
inscription.p:
Records an order of Tirumallināṭaṉ, son of Rājanārāyanaṉ Sambuvarāya to the ūravar of
Amṇaṉtāṅgal in Kalavaip-paṟṟu saying that he had remitted all taxes in the village in favour of the temple
of Kidaraṅkoṉḍa-sōlīśvara-muḍaiyār at Malliyūr for offerings and other expences.

l.ta:
campuvārayaṉ olai kalavaippaṟṟu amaṇantāṅkal ūr(ā)varkaṇṭu
taṅkaḷūr palataḷi pūcaippātiyum uṭaiyār puṟ[ṟi]ṭaṅkoṇṭa nāyinār
tevatāṉam pūcaippāti oruveliyum paṭṭaviruttiyum veṭṭiyāṉukku iṭum
paṇamum nikki eṭṭāvatukku etirāmaṇṭu āṭi mā-

tamutal naṉcey puṉcey toṭṭappaṟṟuppuṟakkalaṉai uṭppaṭṭakaṭamaiāyam
komuṟṟavappeṟu putiyitu uḷḷiṭṭa pala [n*]ellāyaṅkaḷum poṉvari

ū-

aricikāṇam[vi]ruttuppaṭi aricikāṇam nalleru[tu] naṟkkiṭāk kiṟṟuvari
vilakkaṭaippu [ō*]laimervai vācaloṭṭukkāttikaippaccai vācalpaṇam
kuḻippaṇam maṟṟum ippaṟṟil koḷḷum pala upātikaḷum uṭpaṭa malliyūr

uṭaiyār kiṭāraṅkoṇṭa coḷicuramuṭaiyaṉāyinārkku amutupaṭikkum
kaṟia(m)mutupaṭikkum [ve]ñcaṉattu tirumeypapūccukkum tirupparivaṭṭattukkum
tiruviḷakku eṇṇaikkum palapa[ṇi]nimantattukkum eṭṭāvatu varaiyum aṭuttu
[kā]-

ṇi eḻutippeṟukaiyi[l*] etirāmāṇṭu mutalukku tavirntu immutalukkum
tiruppaṇikkum

ṭu mutalukku ūraṭaṅkalum caṟuvamāniya[m*] āka cantiṟāyittavaraiyum naṭakkumpaṭi
paḻaiyateva-

tāṉam āṉapaṭiyāle kuṭutteṉ irācanārāyaṇa campuvarāyaṉ tirumalli
nātaṉeṉ

inta taṉmam iṟakkuvāṉ keṅkaiiṭaikumariiṭaikurāl pacukkoṉṟāṉ
piṟumavatti ko-

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 425 of 1905.)
Guḍimallūr, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the south and east walls of the central shrine in the Bhūmīśvara temple at Guḍimallūr.
inscription.p:
Records that the king remitted taxes payable both in money and in kind on lands in Kalavaip-
paṟṟu, whose crops had suffered due to the natural causes as such lands could not otherwise be taken up
for cultivation. This order was to be engraved in the temples at Tiruvallam, Gudimallūr (in the temple of
Maṇṇai-Nāyanār) and Kalavai.

l.ta:
irācanārāyaṇaṉ campuvarāyaṉ kalavaippaṟṟu
nāṭṭārkku taṅkaḷ paṟṟu āṟāvatuvaraiyum naṭṭuppāḻ nāṟṟaṭippāḻ
vitaittuppāḻ ivaiyiṟṟukku poṉvari cūlavari uḷḷiṭṭa paṇavaṟkamum
aricikāṇamum koṇṭu potukaiyil ittaippaṟṟip payir
ceyvārillātapaṭiāle eḻāvatu āṭi mātamutal naṭṭuppāḻ nāṟṟaṭipāḻ
vitaittupāḻ ivaiyi[ṟṟu]kku poṉvari cūlavari uḷḷiṭṭa

paṇavaṟkamum aricikāṇamum cantirātittavaraiyum koḷḷātapaṭi colli viṭṭa
aḷavukku tiruvallattil koyililum maṇṇaināyaṉār koyililum kalavaiyil
koyililum cantirātittavaraiyum cella kalveṭṭikkoḷḷumpaṭi

colliviṭṭa aḷavukku ippaṭikku vaḻuttuṇaināyaṉār āṇai tāḻvu illai
inṉāḷ mutalukku ippaṭikku kalluveṭṭuvittu [koṇṭu]tāḻvaṟa
naṭat[tipp]otavum [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 426 of 1905.)
Puduppāḍi, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Bhāradvājeśvara temple at Puduppāḍi.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Mentions burning a lamp at mahādēvar temple. Two individuals undertook to burn the
lamp out of interest from one kāsu that they received from some donor.

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 427 of 1905.)
Puduppāḍi, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Bhāradvājeśvara temple at Puduppāḍi.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records the gift of one kāsu for a sandhi lamp to the temple of Tirukkuraṅkūṭṭi-
Mahādēvar in Puduppāḍi. The donor was one Tirunāgīsuradēvan.

l.ta:
vikki[rama] coḻatevarkku yāṇṭu āṟāvatu tirukkāmakkoṭṭappuṟam
putuppāṭit tirukkuraṅkūṭṭi

•••tirunā[kicurat]e[va]nen kuṭutta kācu onṟum kaik koṇṭu ippoliyūṭṭāle
viḷakkeṟikkak kaṭavomāka sa[mma]ti[t*]tu

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 428 of 1905.)
Puduppāḍi, Walaja Taluk, North Arcot District.
On the west and south walls of the central shrine in the Bhāradvājeśvara temple at Puduppāḍi.
inscription.p:
Built in at the end. Sale of land by the ūr of Puduppāḍi in a tirukkāmakoṭṭa-puṟam
Perumbānappāḍi to the temple of Tirukkuraṅkūṭṭiśvaramuḍaiya-Mahādēvar. The land sold was situated
on the bank of the Pālār and had become unfit for cultivation in consequence of a flood in the river, which
led to the fields getting sited up with sand. The land was made tax-free by the ūr on receiving the tax
money.

tu veṇkuṭai mānilam maṉṉuyir toṟum iṉṉaruḷ
cura

tu niḻaṉparappi nikka muṟai[m*]aiyil ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñ cellat taṅkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 429 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
Mutilated. Registers purchase of land from a Brahmin lady by one Pārthasāradhi a temple
official of Śrīkrishṉapurattu Emperumān for feeding pilgrims going to and returning from Śrivēṅgaḍam
(i.e. Upper Tirupati). The land was made tax-free by the sabhā of the village on receipt of the tax-money
from Pārthasārathi.

l.ta:
hāram iṭuvataṟku
vilai koṇṭuṭaiya pūmi śrījaṉanātavatikkuk kiḻakku śrīrā-

jarāja vāykālukku teṟkut mutaṟkaṇṇāṟṟu mūṉṟāñcaturattukkuḻi irunūṟṟu
nālum pālāciriyaṉ

mūṉṟāñ caturattu meṟpaṭiyāḷ pakkal vilai koṇṭuṭaiya kuḻi nāṟpattu
nālumākak kuḻi aṟunūṟṟo-

ttāṉ śrīpātam ittaṉmañ ceytāṉ talai
melaṉa ivai ivvūr

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 430 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north, west and south walls of the central shrine in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
Sale of land, free of taxes, by the sabhā of Jananātha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Mēnmalai-
Taṇiyal-nāḍu (a district) of Jayaṅgoṇḍa-Chōḻamaṇḍalam to Pārthasārathi (mentioned in the above
inscription) for feeding pilgrims going to and returning from Śrivēnkaḍam.
