inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 431 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
Gift of 12 sheep for a lamp to burn at the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭānam Uḍaiyār by Aṇukkaṉ
Kāmakkoṭi.

l.ta:
viḷakku oṉṟukku viṭṭa āṭu panniraṇṭum
kaikkoṇṭom

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 432 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at Tiruttaṇi.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rajakēsarivarman, Year 4 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription registers an agreement given by the sabhā of Tiruttaṉiyal in Kunṟavattana-
kōṭṭam, that their Annual Committee would supply daily an uḻakku of oil for a perpetual lamp in the
temple of Thiruvīraṭṭāṉam-uḍaiya-Mahādēva in lieu of interest on an amount of 12 kaḻañju received from
the temple.
(Published in SII Vol. XIII, No. 82.)

l.ta:
ri vaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu nālāvatu kunṟavattana
koṭṭattut tiruttaṇiyal

varuṭaiya poṉ paṉṉirukaḻañcum ippoṉ paṉṉirukaḻañcukkum
viruttiyāka ivvūrp

val
uḷḷataṉaiyu muṭṭāmai aṭṭi oru tirununtā viḷakku erippomākavum
ivveṇṇai koirācakecari pa

ṟku yāṇṭu nālāvatu mutal etirāṇṭu
tōṟum

ykiṉṟa vāriyap perumakkaḷom ivveṇṇai muṭṭāmaik kuṟaivaṟup
pomākavum nicatam vuḻak keṇṇai [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 433 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
Tamil verse. Records that a certain Nambi-Appi built the temple at Tiruttaṉiyal with granite. The
verse (venba) is said to have been composed by Perumānaḍikaḷ. This Nambi Appi figures in another
inscription (AR. 435 of 1905).
(Published in SII Vol. XII, No. 94.)

l.ta:
tiruntu tiruttaṇiyal ceñcaṭai īcarkku karuṅkallāl kaṟṟaḷiyā niṟku virumpiyā
naṟka-

laikaḷellām naviṉṟa cīrnampiappi poṟpamaiya ceytāṉ purintu |||—
iveṇpā peru-

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 434 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at Tiruttaṇi.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēśarivarman alias Rājēndra-Chōḻadēva I, Year 19 = 1031 A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of 30 cows for a lamp. The temple is called Tiruvīraṭṭānamuḍaiya-Mahādēvar in
Tiruttaṇiyal, in Kuṉṟvarttanakōṭṭam. The donor Nandi Nandiy-ēṟn alias Taṇiyal Muttaraiyaṉ, a paḷḷi of
the village.

l.ta:
viḷakku oṉṟi[ṉu] •••[mup]patu i[p]pacu
muppatuṅ koṇṭu nicati uḻakku ney cantirātittavaṟ aṭṭuvenāneṉ iv-

vūr tuvarāyaṉ orumanavaṉ ayaca[mpa]ṉāna janaṉāta [tu]varāya •••ṭu nāḻiyāl
aṭṭuvenṉāneṉ itu panmā

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 435 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a remission of taxes made by the assembly of Tiruttaṇiyal in the 18th,
Year of Aparājita-vikkirama-varmaṉ, on 1000 kuḻi of land situated to the north of the temple, purchased
by Nambi Appi from the cultivators of the village and given to the dharmigaḷ of the village for providing
offerings to and burning two twilight lamps in the temple of Tiruvīraṭṭānattudēva in the same village. The
donoris evidentlythe builder of the temple mentioned in above inscription (A.R. 433 of 1905).
[Published in SII Vol. XII, No. 95.]

l.ta: lum vanta iṟai mutal viṟṟu ā
mākki itaṉukku nicati nāṉāḻi arici iraṇ-
yomāyil aṉṟāḷ kovukku nicati kāṟpoṉ taṇṭap-
mariyiṭai ceytār ceyta pāvaṅ koḷvomāṉom [|*] allatu i

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 436 & 437 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east and north walls of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at
Tiruttaṇi.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rajēndra Chōḻa I, Year 10 = 1022 A.D.

inscription.p:
Much damaged. Inscriptions AR. No. 436 & 437 of 1905 together from one inscription. Gift of gold
for burning a perpetual lamp in the Subramaniyap-piḻḻaiyār temple at Tiruttaṇiyal alias Jananātha-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam by one Tillai Irāmaṉ of Kāvēripākkam, alias Parichana-chintāmaṇi-
chaturvedimaṅgalam in Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam. The gift of money was entrusted with the sabhā of Tiruttaṇiyal,
who set apart some land income for the lamp service. Another gift of 90 sheep was madeby the paṇimagan
Pāmbuni-kiḻān, who was supervising (kaḍaikkāṇum) the local affairs.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉi ••••• [neṭuntu]yalūḻiyu[ḷiṭai]tuṟai nāṭum tuṭar
• vaṉavelippaṭarvaṉavāciyuñ cuḷḷiccūḻmatiṭk koḷḷippākkaiyu naṇṇaṟk

••••• meṟpaṭic ca

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 438 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On a stone set up close to the entrance into the in the Viraṭṭānēśvara temple at Tiruttani.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēśarivarman (Uttamachōḻa ?), Year 16 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription. Registers a sale of garden land by the sabhā of Tiruttaṇiyal, a
village in Kunṟavattana-Kōṭṭam, which was their mañjikkam (common property ?), to one Vēḷān Nīlan
of Siruvēḷūr in Iḻaiyūr-nāḍu on the southern side of the river, a subdivision of Vēsālippāḍi.
[Published in SII Vol. XIX, No. 388.]

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 439 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the stone set up close to the garbhagriha of the Subrahmanya temple at Tiruttani.

p.b: Chōḻa, Maduraikoṇḍa Parakēśarivarman, Parānthakā I, Year 32 = 939 A.D.

inscription.p:
Built in at the bottom. Gift of land by a lady Kaṇḍi Naṅgai, a relative of certain Parakēśari
Muttaraiyaṉ of Ōviyūr to the temple of Subrahmaṇyap-pḷḷaiyār, who was pleased to stand on the hill at
Tiruttaṇiyal (near) Aguvūr, the eastern hamlet of Peruṅgāñji in Ōgavala-nāḍu (a district of) Melūr-
kōṭṭam, which formed part of Jayaṅgoṇḍa Chōḻa-maṇḍalam. The characters of the inscription are
comparatively of later period; it must be a later copy of the original.

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 440 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Arumugasvamin temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
Much damaged in the middle. Mentions some provisions for the festival days of the diety
Seṅkaḻunīr piḻḻaiyar at the hill of Tiruttaṇiyal.

l.ta:
pakṣattu •••yum
ve •••peṟṟa [co]ti nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattuk
kuṉṟavattaṉakkoṭṭattu meṉmalaittaṇiyal

•••[ti]ruttaṇiyal tirumalai mel niṉṟaruḷiya ceṅkaḻunir[piḷḷaiyāṟku] •••
ponakamariyāti naṭuve cila nāḷ kālavice[ṣa] •••kaḷaippaṟṟi viranaka •••••

••••• yil nāṅkaḷum tāṉattārum porunta naṭattikku[ṭu]ppatā[ka] ••••••••••
irupaṇamu •••[kū]li āḷvācalukku araipaṇamum ••••••••••

[pan]tampiṭikkavum cipātantāṅka akuvūr oṉṟupāti •••yāka cantirātittavarai
naṭakkak kaṭavatu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On a stone built into the floor of the antarāla-maṇḍapa in the Arumugasvamin temple at Tiruttani.
inscription.p:
Seems to record a decision taken by the assembly of Tiruttaṇiyal towards some provisions for
performing the śrīkāiryam of Subrahmaṇya temple on the top of the hill. Refers that they met in a hall
(ambalam) constructed by one Sāttūruḍaiyāṉ Sāttaṉ of Maḻanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prahāra of the Arumugasvamin temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
In Telugu characters. Says that it (perhaps the prāhāra) was the charity of the mahāmaṉḍalēśvara
Thiruveṅgala nāthā rāja-dēva-Chōḻa Mahārāja of Vell[a]mḍu. The daḷavāyi Chidramana executed the
work.

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Vijayaraghava-Perumal temple at Tiruttaṇi.

p.b:
Vijayanagar, Sadāśivadēva Mahārāya, Śaka 1471, Saumya, Makara, Purva 11, Rōhiṇi,
Monday = 1549, Dec. 31.

inscription.p:
Gift of the income (lakkam) of a village by the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-Chinna
Timmayadēva-Mahārāyar to the temple of Perumāḷ Vīrarāgavat tambirāṉ in Tiruttaṇi in Taṇigai-nāḍu a
subdivision of Kunṟavarttana-kōṭṭam. The gift village Viḷakkanēri-kuppam was included in Taṇigai-nāḍu
in Nārayaṇapura-paṟṟu in Chandragiri-rājya which was the sīrmai of the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north, west and south walls of the central shrine and maṇḍapa of the Vijayaraghava-Perumal
temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
The long meykkīrtti of the king occupies most of the inscription (lines 1-8). The last line records a
land sale executed by the sabhā of Tiruttaṇi to one Araiyan Muḍikoṇḍān alias Rājadhirāja Kāraikkāḍu
Uḍaiyāṉ. The later provided it as a bhaṭṭaviṟti endownment to recite dhanmaśāsanam (dharmaśāstra ?).

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 445 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north, west and south walls of the central shrine in the Vijayarāghava-Perumāḷ temple at
Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
Fragment. Begining missing. Year lost. Sale of land perhaps by the sabhā of Jananātha-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Mēnmalai-Taṉiyal-nāḍu.

l.ta:
kaṭalṉaṭuvuḷ palakalañ celutti caṅkirāma vijayortuṅka •••ṭārattaraiyaṉai

piṟakkamum ārttavaṉ naka[ra]por-

maṅkalattu

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 446 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vijayarāghava-Perumāḻ temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p: Fragment, containing a portion of the meykkīrtti introduction of Rājēndra Chōḻa I.

l.ta:
tirumanni vaḷara irunila maṭantaiyum poṟccayap pāvaiyuñ

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vijayarāghava-Perumāḻ temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
Sanskrit in Telugu script. Records that a certain Nārāyana-rājā built the temple and its gōpura. He
claims descent from Karikāla-chōḻa. The major part of the inscription contains a mythical genealogy of
the donor tracing from Vishṇu, Brahmā, etc,
Published in SII, X, No. 226 [See also Swell's List of Antiquities Vol. I, page 158.]

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 448 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vijayarāghava-Perumāḷ temple at Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p: In Telugu script. This contains Telugu version of the above inscription (No. 228).

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 449 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Vijayarāghava-Perumāḻ temple at Tiruttaṇi.

p.b: Choḻa, Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēśarivarman, Parāntaka I, Year 34 = 941 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a gift of reclaimed land after purchase by the kanapperumakkaḻ of Ti[ruttaṇi]yal in
Kuṉravattana-kōṭṭam to (the temple of) Tiruvānandapuram for a flower garden. The sabhā of Tiruttaṇiyal
seems to have made the land tax-free.

l.ta: [mu]ṉṉūṟu kuḻi nantavāṉañ ceytu koḷvatākavu(m) mivvaṉaittukku(m) mi-

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 450 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vijayarāghava-Perumāḻ temple at
Tiruttaṇi.
inscription.p:
Sanskrit in Telugu script. Records that a certain Bommarāja, whose genology is given at
considerable length, renewed the grants made by his ancestors. He is said to be descended from
Nārayanajāja mentioned in No. 228 above.

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 451 of 1905.)
Tiruttaṇi, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vijayarāghava-Perumāḷ temple at
Tiruttaṇi.

p.b:
Vijayanagar, Sadāsivadēva-Mahārāya, Śaka 1486, Raktākshi, Simha, Purva 5, Friday = 1564,
August 11.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. The king boasts his victories including the expedition to Ceylon. Stops after
mentioning Taṇigai-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 452 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple.
inscription.p: Fragment. Mentions ManumasittiAraiyar, for whose merit some gift seems to have been made.

l.ta:
tiruvāykkeḻvi munpāka tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum
pā[ṇ*]ṭiya • • •

eṅkaḷci • • ttamaiyaṉār maṉumacitti araiyarkku naṉṟāka uṭaiyār [tiruvālaṅ]
kāṭuṭaiyaṉā • • •

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 453 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Gift of 12 sheep for a lamp by Vaḍukan Pavaḻakkuṉṟu alias Rājadanañjiya-muvēnavelāṉ of
Ūṟṟukkāḍu alias Aḻagiyasōḻanallūr in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam.

l.ta:
ttu āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyāṟkku ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk koṭṭattu
ūṟṟukkāṭāṉa aḻakiyaco-
ḻanallūril ūṟṟukkāṭuṭaiyāṉ vaṭukaṉ pava[ḻa]kkuṉṟāna rājataṉañciya muve

āṭu panniraṇṭum kaikkoṇṭu cantirātittavarai celuttak kaṭaveṉāneṉ
maṉṟāṭi ceri ayyā[ṟa]n tevaṉen

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 454 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.

p.b:
Vijayanagar, Dēvarāya-Mahārāya Śaka 1357, Rākshasa, Kaṉṉi, Purva 10, Monday, Thiruvōṉam
= 1435, Sept. 5.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Sale of land by the trustees of Tiruvalangadudaiya-Nāyanār temple at Paḻaiyanūr in
Mēlmalai-Paḻaiyanūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam to an individual called Mallukāchchiṉar
son of Kevura Aṇṇagaḷ of Chimittirikkal in Pāṇḍiya-nāḍu.

l.ta:
āyirattu munūṟṟu
aypattueḻil mel cellā niṉṟa irākṣata

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 455 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. Registers a public sale of land by the ūr of Poḷipākkam in Naḍuvumalai-Perumūr-
nāḍu to a merchant of Tirumayilāppil in Puliyūr-koṭṭam. Refers to a survey of the country (ulagaḷavu).

taṉnirupatumalar maṉnava-

rumūr kiḻavar cuppaiyan viramalaiyun tiṇaiyānacuntaraṉ peṟṟāṉum pulikiḻān
paramayan vikiramacoḻaṉu muḷḷiṭṭa i-

nokkippoṉa meṭṭuvāykkālukku va-

ṭṭaviruttikkut teṟkiṟkuṇṭiliṉ
kīḻvarampukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai kaliṅku niṉ-

ṟum [p]āynta teṟkiṟkālukkut teṟkum innāṉkellaikkum uṭpaṭṭa taṭi
palaviṉāl tiruvulakaḷaviṉpaṭi tū[y]nilam aṟunūṟum pat-

tāntarattil viṟṟuttarak koḷviruḷiroveṉṟu kūṟa immoḻi keṭṭu
etirmoḻi koṭuttor immaṇṭalattup puliyūr koṭṭa

ttut tirumayilāppil viyāpāri oṟṟikoṇṭānātittateva[nā]na tirttavāric
cileṭṭi [yen yāniṟō] pu[ṅ]kaḷūr melkaḻaniyil iṉṉāṉ kel-

llaikkuṭpaṭṭa taṭi palavināl tiruvulakaḷaviṟpaṭi tūy nilam pattāntarattiṟ kuḻi
aṟunūṟum āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 456 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Sale of the dēvadāna village of Meduvūr in Naḍuvin malai-Perumūr-nāḍu by the trustees and the
ūravar of Paḻayanūr to Cheluvappiḷḷai Chittiramēḻikkōn son of a temple woman (dēvaraṭiyāḷ) called
Aṇḍamuṟa nimirnta-māṇikkam. It is said the lands were lying fallow as the irrigation tank had breached.
The lands were made tax-free.

l.ta:
ccakkaravarttikaḷ maturaiyu[m] īḻamum p[ā]ṇṭiya[n]
muṭittalaiyuṅ ko-

lattu maṇaviṟkoṭṭattu menmalaip paḻaiyaṉūr ṉāṭṭup paḻaiyaṉūr uṭaiyār
tiruvālaṅkā-

ṭuṭaiyār koyi[li]l kannināyaṟṟup patinā[ṟāṉ ti]yati cevvāykkiḻamaiyum
pū[r]vapakṣat-

tiyaiyum peṟṟa cittirai nāḷ ammaiyappaṉ tiruma[ṇ]ṭapattu iruntu
āticaṇṭe
yār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār koyil tānattomum uḷḷū[r]t tanimāhe

mā[ṇa]k kaiyyeḻuttu ikkoyiṟ tevaṭiyāḷ
nāyakappiḷḷaiyārāna aṇṭamuṟa nimi[r*]nta

māṇikkattinmakaṉ celvappiḷḷaikku cittarameḻic coḻakonuk[ku] yittevar
tevatānam naṭuvinmalaip

perumūrnāṭṭu metuvūr viṟṟukkuṭutta paricāvatu ivvūreri yuṭaintu kaḻaniyuṅ
kāṭeḻun

var tevatānametuvūr nilakkāṇit[tiṭṭu va]ri[c]eyiṟuttu epperpaṭṭa kaṭa[m]ai
kuṭimai pokkaṟukkak kaṭavatākavu

ṅ(k) kuṭutta paricāvatu ivvūr kiḻ[p]āṟkellai yetirilicoḻa maṭappuṟamāṉa
caṟ[kkarai]nallūrellai

kku meṟkkum teṉpāṟkkellai •••kāṭṭellaikku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai
mullaivāy ellaikku-

k kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkkellai tiruppaḻaiyanūrammai nācciyār maṭappuṟamāna
kāraikkālammaiyār viḷāka[t]

tu ellaikkut teṟkku innāṟpāṟkellaiyuṭppaṭṭa kāṭuṭ[pa]ṭa nañce puñce
uṇṇilamoḻiviṉṟi i[v]

vūr tirumuka kalveṭṭuppaṭiye ūraṭaṅka[lā]ka viṟṟukkuṭuttuk kaikkoṇṭa
aṉṟāṭu naṟpaḻaṅkācu iru-

pattaiñcum kaikkoṇṭu vi[ṟ*]ṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu kuṭuttom uṭaiyār
tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār ko-

ttom aruḷāl ikkoyiṟ koyiṟkaṇakku perumpūturuṭaiyāṉ aruḷāḷan
vaṭukan eḻuttu ||—

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 457 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Jatāvarmaṉ Sundara-Pāṇḍiyadēva I, Year 23 = 1274 A.D.

inscription.p:
Engraved below the above inscription. Incomplete. Seems to be some arrangement regarding the
cows of the temple of Thiruvālaṅkāḍuḍaiyār of Paḻaiyanūr in Paḻaiyanūrnāḍu in Maṉaiyil-kōṭṭam a
subdivision in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] koccaṭapaṉmar tripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī cuntarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku
yāṇṭu [ 25vatu] jeyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu maṉa[yi]l koṭṭattu
paḻaiyaṉūr nāṭṭu paḻaiyaṉū-

l.ta:
ruṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉār [śrī]paṇṭārattu niṉṟa pacu[vā]lum
aypatte • • •

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Registers a public sale of land freed from taxes by the sabhā of Vaḷaikuḷam alias Nittavinōda-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Mēlmalai-Vēlūr-nāḍu to a native of Kaḷḷikkuḍi alias Purovarinallūr in
Purapparaḷai-nāḍu (a district) of RājarājaPāṇḍināḍu. The purchasear, Kaḷḷikuḍiyāṉ Araiyaṉ Uyavantān
alias Rājanārāyaṇa-jōti-vayanāṭṭaraiyaṉ gifted the land to the temple for a lamp service.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara ṉāmātu viḷaṅka jayamā[tu] virumpat
taṉnirupatumalarmaṉnavar cūṭi

ceṅkol vaḷara ticaitoṟum cella veṅkali nīkki meyyaṟan taḻaip[pa]k
kaliṅkamiriyak kaṭal malai ṉaṭātti irucuṭa(r*)

rājavatikku kiḻakkum mummuṭi coḻa vāykkālukku vaṭakku orukaṇṇāṟṟu
ṉālāñcatirattu teṟkaṭaiyakkuḻi āyira nikki itaṉ vaṭakkum kiḻakkum āka-

kkuḻi āyirattaiññūṟum eṅkaḷūr kaṭṭaḷaippaṭiye iṟai iḻiccitāka koḷvīr
uḷirovenṟu kūṟa immoḻikeṭṭu etirmoḻi kuṭut[o]

[ṉ] rājarājap pāṇṭiṉāṭṭu puṟappaṟaḷai nāṭṭu kaḷḷikkuṭiyāṉa purovarinallūr
kaḷḷikkuṭiyānaraiyan uyyavantāṉana rājaṉārāyaṇajo-

ra vilaiyāka uṭaiyār
tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyārkku oru tirunantā viḷakkukku nīṅkaḷ
viṟṟuttarilyāṉu[me]

tu koḷvatāṉa emmil icainta vilaipporuḷ muṟṟum āvaṇakkaḷiye
kiḻikaicelakkoṇṭu viṟṟuk kuṭuttom innilattukku nīrkkīnta[pa]-

[ṭi nīr*]pāyap peṟuvatākavum innilattukku periṟai ciṟṟiṟai maṟṟum epperp
paṭṭaṉavum koḷḷap peṟātomākavum ittevar koyilile kalli-
ttavaraicelvatāka viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu kuṭuttom vaḷaiku[ḷa]
ninṟa nampipaṭṭan aḻakiya rākavapaṭṭan kirāñcimalaikiniya niṉṟān paṭṭan va • • •
l.note: The last word is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 459 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
The king granted, 10 vēli of land, a tax-free dēvadāna in the village of Maṇavil in Maṇavil-
nāḍu, a sub-division of Maṇavir-kōṭṭam. The royal secretry Mīṉavaṉ-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ wrote the royal
order. Kūḻapiḍāraṉ is said to have requested the king for the grant.

l.ta:
tti koṉeriṉmai koṇṭāṉ jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattu meṉmalaippa-

ḻaiyaṉūr nāṭṭup paḻaiyaṉūrttiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār koyili[l] tevakanmi
koyiṟkaṇakkaṉukkum śrīmāhe-
k kaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukkum ittevarkku yāṇṭu āṟāva[tu nā]ḷ

iṟuppatāka maṇaviṟ koṭṭattu maṇavil nāṭṭu maṇavilile [pati]ṟṟu
velinilam āṟāvatu mutal tevatāṉa

iṟaiyiliyāka iṭṭu variyilārum varikkūṟuceyvārkaḷum eḻuttiṭṭa uḷvari
potakkāṭṭac coṉṉom

iṉnilam āṟāvatu mutal ittevarkkut tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyākak
kaikkoṇṭu nivantam celuttappa-

ṇṇuka ||— eḻutiṉāṉ tirumantiraōlai miṉavaṉ muventaveḷāṉ ivai maḻavarāyaṉ
eḻuttu ivai ciṅkaḷarā-

yaṉ eḻuttu ivai vaiyirātarāyaṉ eḻuttu ivai coḻarājaṉ eḻuttu ivai
villavarājaṉ eḻuttu ||—

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 460 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Records the queen Kāḍavaṉmādēvi[yār] made a gift of 108 kaḻañju of gold to the temple of
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu in Paḻaiyanūr-nāḍu. The sabhā Perumuḷaiyūr, a brahmadēya in Kākkalūr-nāḍu, a sub-
division of Īkkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam received the money and undertook to give 200 kalam of paddy each, Year and
a specified quantity of ghee daily to the temple in lieu of interest. The inscription seems to be a later copy
of the original one, as the writing is similar to the above inscription.

l.ta:
ippoṉ nūṟṟeṇ kaḻañcukkum palicaiyākap
paḻaiyaṉūr nāṭṭu tiruvā-

laṅkāṭuṭaiyārkku āṭṭāṇṭu toṟum ceyyakkaṭava nivantam āṇṭu varai
aḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 461 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Gift of gold for two lamps. The sabhā of Pulvēḷūr in Eyiṟ-kōṭṭam received an endowment of 30
kaḻañju from one Arigaṇḍaperumāḷ for maintaining two lamps in the temple of Tiruvālaṅgāḍuḍaiyār.
[Nos. 460 and 461 are engraved in comparatively later characters of about the 12th century. They may be
copies of old inscriptions, though no statement to that effect is made.]

l.ta:
āvatu arikaṇṭaperumāḷ
pakkal eyiṟ koṭṭattup pulve-

yār ceyyak kaṭava nimantam uṭaiyārkku
tirunantāviḷakku iraṇṭukku pirutimāṇi-
kkattināl nitta muṭṭāmal aḷakkak kaṭava eṇṇai uri [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 462 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Assignment of the proceeds of certain taxes paid by some tirunandāviḷakku-kuḍi to the temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu for maintaining perpetual lamps. The document is signed by Tikkarasan, an officer. The
taxes included pulvari-magaṇmai, kāṇikkai, and nāṭṭuvari . .

l.ta: tuc ceṉṟa nāḷ mutal pulvari makaṇmai kāṇikkai nāṭṭuvari uḷpaṭa uṭaiyār

tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟ tiruna[n]tāviḷakkukku uṭalāka viṭṭom innāḷ
mutal

[tāṅkaḷ] munpil āṇṭukaḷ tirunantāviḷakkukku aḷantu potum ney oḻiya
nām

koḷḷum pulvari makaṇmai kāṇikkai nāṭṭuvarikku varum paṇattukku ney
ko-

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 463 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Gift of 16 cows for a lamp in the temple of Tiruvālaṅgāḍuḍaiyār at Paḻaiyanūr in Paḻaiyanūr-nāḍu
in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The donor was Viṟpēḍu-kiḻān Dēvagaḷnataṉ Ponnan,
a merchant (vāṇigaṉ) of Antamiḻaga-perunderu in the same village.

l.ta:
[ti]ruvāykkeḻvi munnākat tiripuvanacakkaravat[ti]kaḷ śrī irācarāca
tevarkku yāṇṭu

maṇaviṟ koṭṭattup paḻaiyanūr nāṭṭup paḻaiyanūr uṭaiyār
tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyanārkku ivvūr antamilaḻakap perunteruvil vāṇikan
vaṟ-

peṭu kiḻān tevakaḷnātan ponnaṉ vaitta tirunantā viḷakku araikku viṭṭa
pacu

ṟukku arumoḻi tevanāl ney āḻākkāka vantaney cantirātittavarai
celuttakkaṭa vomākak kaikkoṇṭom

manṟāṭi puliyakkon varantaruvānum mummalaiyamāraṇanum uḷḷiṭṭom
itu

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 464 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Gift of land by Tikkarasar Visaiyādēvan who belonged to (the family of) Tyāgasamudra-
paṭṭaigaḷ for the pañcha-gavyam bath to the deity of Tiruvālaṅgāḍu temple.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tribhuvanac cakravattikaḷ śrīvicaiyakaṇṭa kopāla tevaṟku
yāṇṭu 29vatu cimha nāyaṟṟu aparapakṣat[ tu pra] thamaiyum veḷḷik
kiḻamaiyuñ catayamumāṉa nāḷ tiyākacamuttirap paṭṭaikaḷil tikkaracar
vicaiyātevaṉeṉ uṭaiyār

l.ta:
māṭiyaruḷa viṭṭa
ūrkkāl •••arakkuṉṟattu naṉcai nila •••oru veli nilam kaṭamai āyam kācukaṭamai
aṉaittāyamum innāyaṉārkku āṭiyaruḷa neykkuc cantirātit[ta*]

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 464A of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Beginning of the inscription not traceable. Gift of cows for burning a lamp by Vadakarai
nadalvan.

l.ta:
••••• tu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattup puliyūrk koṭṭattu māmpā[k] •••
karuppaṭṭi [ki]ḻa •••va • • āḷuṭaiy[āṉ ā]ṉa vaṭakaraiṉāṭāḻvānen immaṇṭalattu
maṇaviṟkoṭṭattut tiruppaḻaiyaṉūr nāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār
tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyārkku

•••ṉṟukku innāyaṉār tirunontāviḷakkukuṭi iṭaiyaril kālicāttaṉ pūman
pakkal viṭṭa pacu mūṉṟuṅ kaikkoṇṭu tiṅkaḷ nāḻi neyyaḷakka kaṭavatākavum
ippaṭi cammati[t*]tu kaikkoṇṭeṉ kālicāttaṉ pūmaṉeṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. 465 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Partly built in. Gives elaborate details relating to the war of Pāṇḍya succession in which the
Singhalese generals Lenkāpura-Daṇḍanāyakka and Jayadratha Daṇḍanāyakka took part on behalf of the
Pāṇḍya king opposing the Chōḻa side. Appreciating the important role played by his chief commander
Paḻaiyaṉūr-uḍaiyān Vēdavaṉam-uḍaiyāṉ Ammai-appaṉ alias Aṇṇan Pallavarājan in the succession
dispute, the king granted to the chief ten vēli of land free of taxes in the village Rājarājan-Paḻaiyaṉūr in
Nenmali-nāḍu. The king's order relating to the grant is addressed to the trustees of the
Tiruvālaṅgāḍuḍaiyār temple.
[Published in Epigraphia Indica vol. XXII, No, 14.]

l.ta: kaṭalcūḻnta pārmātaruṅ kalaimātarum aṭal cūḻnta por

mātarum cīrmātarum ama[r*]ntu vāḻa nāṟkaṭal cūḻ puviēḻum pā[ṟ]kaṭal cūḻ
pukaḻpara-

ṟiyil niṉṟu [pā]rippat teṉṉavaruñ ceralaruñ ciṅkaḷarum mutalāya maṉṉavarkaḷ
tiṟai

cumantu vantiṇṭic cevippa ūḻiūḻi oru ceṅkol ēḻupārum iṉitaḷippac
cempoṉ vira[si*]-

[ṅṅā]canattu ulakuṭai mukkokkiḻāṉaṭikaḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kovi rāja
kesari paṉmarāṉa

teḻiṉāl tiripuvaṉaccakravatti koneriṉmai koṇṭāṉ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa-
maṇṭalattu meṉ-

malaip paḻaiyaṉūr nāṭṭup paḻaiyaṉūr uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār
koyilil tevakaṉmikku-

varak kaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukkum [|*] paḻaiyaṉūr uṭaiyāṉ
vetavaṉamuṭaiyāṉ ammai a-

ppaṉāṉa aṇṇaṉ pallava[rāja]ṉ īḻattāṉ parākkiramapāku āḻvāṉ pote
tuṭaṅki coḻarājyattukku

tap paṇṇi[ṉapaṭiyāle] ••••• mceyyappārtta iṭattu itukkup parikāramāka
kula-

īḻattāṉ paṭaiyaiyu-
m ippaṭaik[kuk kārāṇavar āṉa] ila[ṅkāpura ta]ṇṭanāyakkaṉum jakat

raiyuṅ koṉṟu [maturai vācalilē ivarkaḷ talaikaḷai] taippittu ivvūrile
kulacekaraiyum pukaviṭṭuc ce-
llā niṟka ••••• ku virotamāyiruppaṉa ceyyappārttu ivaṉ taṉ paṭai

nilaiyāṉa pulaicceri māttōṭṭam vallikāmam maṭṭivāḻ uḷḷiṭṭa ūrkaḷile
paṭaikaḷu-

m pukutaviṭṭu paṭavukaḷuñ ceyvikkiṟapaṭi keṭṭu itukkup parikāramāka īḻattāṉ
marumakaṉārāy īḻarājyattukkuṅ

kaṭavarāy muṉpe pontirunta civallavarai aḻaippittu ivarkku
veṇṭuvaṉavuñceytu ivaraiyum i-

varuṭaṉe veṇṭum paṭaikaḷaiyum ūrāttuṟai vallikāmam maṭṭivāḻ uḷḷiṭṭa

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
This records that the king granted the village of Kōṭṭūr alias Chōḻavichchādhara-
chaturvēdimangalam in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam as tax-free dēvadāna to the
temple of Tiruvālaṅgāḍuḍaiyār at Paḻaiyanūr in Mēṉmalai-Paḻaiyaṉūr-nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-
maṇḍalam. The temple at Kōṭṭūr is referred to as Ilambayangōṭṭur-uḍaiyār. Reference is also made to the
Revenue Survey of the village in the 16th year of the reign of Sungandavirtta Kulottunga-Chōḻa [I]

l.ta:
ppaḻaiyaṉūr nāṭṭup paḻaiyaṉūr uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār koyilil
śrīmā

[niva]ntaṅkaḷukku iṟuppatāka maṇaviṟ koṭṭattuk kaṉṟūr nāṭṭuk koṭṭurāṉa
coḻaviccātira

ruvilampayaṅkoṭṭuṭaiyārkkuttevatāṉa iṟaiyili iṭṭa nilam nikki nilam
nūṟṟupatteṭṭu muṉ[ṟu]mākkāṇi araikkāṇi-

yum patiṉoṉṟāvatu pacāṉmutal tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāka iṭṭum
tiruvilampayaṅkoṭṭuṭaiyārkkuc cu[ṅka]ntavir[t]ta kulo-

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 467 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the east wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Sale of 6 vēli of land, freed from taxes, by the sabhā of Perumūr alias Rājanārāyaṇa-
chaturvedimangalam in Perumūr-nāḍu to Pūṇḍi-uḍaiyān Pavaḻakkuṉṟan Vaṉṉāḍuḍaiyān of
Chōḻamaṇḍalam, who got it for the temple of Tiruvālaṅgāḍu for feeding pilgrims (apūrvi-āṇḍār).

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 468 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Records that the (enclosing) hall was constructed by Ammaiyappan Paliyanji Pallavarayan. The
alphabet shows that he probably belonged to the 13th century.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 469 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Gift of 250 current pudukkāsu by Paḻaiyanūr-uḍaiyān Vēdavanamuḍaiyān Karuṇākaran alias
Amarakōn, for two lamps to the shrine of the goddess Periyanāchchiyār of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam shrine in
the Tiruvālangāḍu temple.

l.ta:
ṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu meṉmalaip paḻaiyaṉūr nāṭṭu paḻaiyaṉūr āḷuṭaiyār
tiruvālaṅkāṭu-

ṭaiyār koyilil tirukkāmakkoṭṭattu periyanācciyāṟku immaṇṭalattu
innāyaṉār teva-

ipperiya-

aṉaivomum poṉ koṇ

ṭu poliūṭṭāka tāṅkaḷ kaiykkoṇṭa aṉṟāṭu naṟputukkācu irunūṟṟu

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 470 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple.

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Mallikarjunadeva-Maharaya, son of Devaraya-Maharaya. Saka 1373,
Pradmōdūta = 1450 A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of the services of some tiruviḷakku-maṉṟāḍi for maintaining four lamps in the temple. The
gift was made by the Sādam officers Aḻagiyanayinār and another person for the merit of the king.

l.ta:
śrīmaṉ mahāmaṇṭalecura ariya(r)rāyavipāṭaṉ pāṣaikku tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ
teva-

rāya mahāirāya[r*] kumārar mallikāṟcunateva mahāirāyaṟku cellā niṉṟa
cakāttam
l mel cellā niṉṟa piṟa[m*]otūta varuḻam uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkā-

ṭuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku tirununtāviḷakkukku mallikāṟcunarāyaṟku tanmamāka
cātamāṇikkanāyinār a • • ntiranātarum aḻakiya nā[yi]nārum tanmamāka

viṭṭa tiruviḷakku maṉṟāṭi vaṭukakoṉ akakkoṉ pottaracaṉ uḷḷiṭṭārum
ko ••••• koṉ pottaṉtāṭai veṅki inta kuṭimaikaḷai a[ppan]

caṉṉatiyile nāḷtoṟum nālu tiruviḷakku cantiṟātittavaraiyum niṟceyippu ne • •

uṇṭākil keṅkaikaraiyil kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa pāvattile

pokak kaṭavarkaḷākavum ippaṭikku cammatittu taṉmacātaṉam paṇṇi kuṭuttom
ivai māṇikka[nāya]ṉ eḻuttu ivai attanātaṉ eḻuttu ivai aḻakiyanāyaṉ
eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 471 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.

p.b: Vijayanagara Mallikarjunaraya, son of Devaraya-Maharaya, Saka 1372, Sukla = 1449 A.D.

inscription.p:
A similar gift, as in the above inscription, for a lamp by the same Sādam officers, for the merit
of the king.

kaṇṭan

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 472 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.

p.b: Vijayanagara Kampana Udaiyar, Ananda, Mārgaḻi, Purva 5, Sunday, Mūlam = 1374 A.D.

inscription.p:
The temple trustees gift of 200 kuḻi of land at Kāraippākkam as tirunāmattukāṇi to the temple by
the trustees of the temple.

l.ta:
ñcamiyum ātittavāramum peṟṟa mulattu nāḷ
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu maṇaviṟkoṭṭattu paḻaiyaṉūrnāṭṭu
paḻaiyaṉūr āḷuṭai[ya*] nāyaṉār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku
śrīmaṉmahāmaṇṭa

ṣaikku tappuvarāyarkaṇṭaṉ
muvārayarkaṇṭaṉ vīra śrīkampaṇa uṭaiyārkku

vaṟsarattu uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil

kāraippākkattil

uttaraṉp[e]ṭu uṭaiyāṉ karumpaṉ ammaiappa veḷāṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 473 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
States that Sūrappa-timmarasaiyar, the executive agent of Amaram Timmarasayaṅgār ordered
the remission of the nīrkūli tax on some land at Sakkiranallūr which was the service tenure
(sēvagamāniyam) of a temple girl.

l.ta:
teti uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkā
uṭaiya nāyiṉār śrīkāriyañceyvār śrīmatu amaram timmaracayaṅkār
kāriyattukku kaṭavar cūrappa timmaracaiyarum (tāṉa) tāṉattārum cilācātaṉam
paṇṇi kuṭu[t*]tapaṭi cakkiṟanallūril tevaraṭiyar cevaka māṉiyattukku
ṉiṟkūlikku cevakattukku orupaṇam koḷḷukaiyil anta paṇam

veṇṭā(m)meṉ[ṟu caṟu]vamāṉṉiyamāka viṭṭu yim muṟai •••ācantira
ātittavaraiyum naṭakkakkaṭavo(m)mākavum atikāri

ivai aṉṉamaracar eḻuttu ivai ku[ḻā]ṇṭā[r*] a[tta]muḻā[r*] eḻuttu ivai
kecaveṭṭai kuḻāṇṭā[r*] eḻuttu ivai [vaḻu]tippaṭṭar eḻuttu ivai
caṅkarapaṭṭar eḻuttu (ivai) ippaṭikku ivai [am]maia[p*]paveḷār
cittirakuttar eḻuttu ivai amma[y*]ppaveḷāṉ kailāyam eḻuttu§

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 474 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Records endowment of 1,000 pudukkāsu for three lamps by Paḻaiyanūr-uḍaiyān
Vēdavanamuḍaiyān Ammaiyappa[n] Pallavarāyan. The money was given through one Kūḻāṇḍār.

l.ta:
kaṭal cūḻnta pārmātarum kalaimātarum aṭal cūḻnta pormātarum
cirmātarum amarntuvāḻa nāṟkaṭalcūḻ puvieḻum pāṟkkaṭal cūḻ pukaḻparappi āti
ukamāmenṉac cotimuṭi puṉaintaruḷi aṟucamaiyamum jampūtamum neṟiyininṟu
pā[r]kkiṭappattenṉavaruñ ceralaruñ ciṅkaḷarum mutalāya manṉavarkaḷ
tiṟaitavintu vantiṇṭic cevippa ūḻi ūḻi oru ceṅkol eḻupārum
initaḷippac cempon virasi

āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu mel
malaippaḻaiyanū[r] nāṭṭup paḻaiyanūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār
koyilil aṇṭamuṟa nimi[r*]ntāṭiyaruḷiṉa nāyanār tirumunpu ittevar
tevatānam paḻaiyanūrp paḻaiyanūruṭaiyān vetavaṉamuṭaiyānammaiyappa[n]
pallavarāyaneṉ vaitta tirinuntāviḷakku muṉṟum paḻaiyanūruṭaiyān
vetavaṉāmuṭaiyān karuṇākaran vaitta tirinuntāviḷakkonṟum āka viḷakku
nālum ivviḷakku nālukkumikkoyil civappirāmaṇaril kā

pan śrī kaṇṭanātanum ivvaṉaivom pon koṇṭu
poliyūṭṭāka nāṅkaḷ kaikkoṇṭa putukkācu āyiramum kaikkoṇṭu
viḷakkonṟukku arumoḻitevan uḻakkāl viḷakku nālukku ney nāḻiyāka
vanta ney koṇṭu

viḷakku nālukkum āṇṭār kūḻāṇṭār pakkal nāṅkaḷ kaikkoṇṭa
putu[k*]kācu āyiramum kaik koṇṭom ivvanaivom ippaṭikku ivai
tirucciṟṟampala paṭṭan eḻuttu kā

pan pūtipaṭṭaṉ
eḻuttu kaucika[n*] śrī kaṇṭanā

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 475 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Mutilated. Beginning portion of the inscription is missing. Mentions a sale of 500 kuḻi of tax
exempted land by the temple trustees to one Malligārjinadēvar for 250 paṇam to provide food offerings.

l.ta: aññūṟṟukkum iṟaiili caṟuvamāṉṉiyam āka vilaiāka niṟceytta

ippaṇam irunūṟṟu ay[m*]patukkum vilai āṉa kuḻi añnūṟukku varum
kaṭamai poṉvari uḷḷatu tamakku nāṅkaḷ vilai āka vilai

māṇam
paṇṇikkuṭuttu inta paṇam irunūṟṟuay[m*]patum kiḻikaiccilavaṟa
paṟṟikkoṇṭom intakuḻi aññūṟṟil uṇṭāṉa irupūmuppū viḷainta kaṭamai

poṉvari uḷḷatu uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku
mattiāṉakālattuccantikku

m antikkālattu cantikkum āka nāḷ oṉṟukku canti iraṇṭukku amutupaṭi
kuṟuṇiyum kaṟi amutu tayir amutukku kācu iraṇṭum āḻākku neyyum āka nāḷ
toṟum uṭaiavarai amutu ceyya paṇṇi inta

sātam nāṉāḻiyum
ikkoyil tevaraṭiyāḷ ariyavaḷ makaḷ ammaiappanācci peṟakkaṭavaḷ
ākavum intacanti cantiṟātittavaraiyum

naṭatta kaṭavom ākavum nāṅkaḷ inta iraṇṭu cantikkum inta kuḻi
aññūṟṟukku varum vāram kaṭamai poṉvari uḷḷatu tāṉattile kaikkoṇṭu
intaccanti cantiṟātittavaraiyum naṭattakkaṭavom ākavum ippaṭi cammatittu
iṟaiili vilai

māṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom mallukāṟcciṉa tevarkku
uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil tāṉattārom ippaṭikku
kūḻāṇṭār vakai iraṇṭukku ivai kūḻāṇṭār eḻuttu ivai kūḻāṇṭāṉ
eḻuttu

tevakaṉmiyaḷ vakai iraṇṭukku ivai tirucciṟṟampalam paṭṭar eḻuttu ivai
jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻapaṭṭar eḻuttu ivai caṅkarapaṭṭar eḻuttu ivai
tiruvālaṅkāṭapaṭṭar eḻuttu ivai puḻutipaṭṭar eḻuttu ivai
vipūtipūṣaṇapaṭṭar eḻuttu ivai keṅkātarapaṭṭar eḻuttu koyilkkaṇakku
vakai iraṇṭukku ikkoyil kaṇakku uttaraṉpeṭuṭaiyāṉ ammaiappaṉ
ammaiappaveḷāṉ eḻuttu ikkoyil kaṇakku perumputūr •••••

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 476 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.

p.b:
Vijayanagara. Sadasivadeva-Maharaya, Saka 1484, Rudhirodgarin, Mithuna, Purva 7, Sunday,
Atta = 1562, June 28.

inscription.p:
Registers a poliyūṭṭu endowment of 1500 paṇam made by Vedamarga-pratishthacharya
Dharamasivacharya, the Head of the maṭha called Uḷmaḍam at Tiruvālaṅgāḍu for the celebration of the
vasanta festival in the temple. The money was used for constructing the waste weir to the reservoir of
Paḻaiyanūr.

l.ta:
pakṣattu cattamiyum ātitta vāramum atta nakṣattiramum peṟṟa yiṟṟai nāḷ
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu vaṭakarai maṇavil koṭṭattu paḻaiyaṉūr
ṉāṭṭil paḻaiyaṉūr tiruvālaṅkāṭṭil uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāvuṭaiya nayiṉār
śrīkāriyam ceyvārum ikkoyil tāṉattārum tiruvālaṅkāṭu uḷmaṭam
vetamāṟka

caraṇāravinta tiyānapārāyaṇarāṉa tanmacivācāriyaṟku tiruvacanta
tiruṉāḷ poliyūṭṭu cātaṉam paṇṇikkuṭutta innayiṉār antamil
aḻakapperumāḷum ṉācciyār ceppentiḷamulai nācciāṟkum ca

ttu nāḷ teṟpattiruvulāc
ceytaruḷavum koṭikkampattukku vaṭakku taṉmacivācāriyar uṭaiya
vacantamaṇṭapattil ē[ṟi]aruḷi tiruṉāḷ pattukkum nāḷ oṉṟukku maṇṭapa
ciṟappukku amutupaṭi

inta tirunāḷai
nokki cellum cilavu uṇṭānatum śrī paṇṭārattile poliyūṭṭāka naṭatta
paḻaiyaṉūr erikku teṟku kaliṅku kaṭṭa kuṭutta paṇam

pālaivāyil paṅku
tirukkoyil pāti nikki niṉṟa pātiyum mahājanaṅkaḷ kaiyil taṉmacivācāriyar
āyirattu aññūṟu paṇattukku

koṇṭa kiṟāmam pātiyum tirukkoyilile kaikkoṇṭu inta vacanta
tiruṉāḷukku varuṣam varuṣamtoṟum inta kaṭṭaḷaipaṭi ellām tirukkoyil
mutalile cantiṟātitta varaiyum naṭatti varakkaṭavom ākavum inta
vacantamaṇṭapattil amutu ceytaruḷina piṟacātam paṇṇi ā[ka]vākattukku
viṭṭavar viḻukkāṭu ṉāliloṉṟum tanmacivācāriyar peṟṟukkoḷḷa kaṭavar
ākavum inta tanmattukku akitam eṉṟiyile naṭatta kaṭavom ākavum inta
tarumattukku akitam paṇṇiṉavan keṅkaikaraiile

edition.p: The following three lines are along with the above inscription in different hand style of the same period.

l.ta:
paṇam aimpatu ippaṇam aimpatukku iṉṉilam vilaiyāvatu ākavum itukku
uḷḷu maṭakka

irāyavipāṭaṉkol kuḻi nūṟu comaracarkku naṉṟāka vāḻamaramu māmaramu
vaikkiṟati

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 477 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Records that Vaḷḷuvanpākkiḻān Dēvan alias Teluṅgarāyan made a gift of 70 kāsu for food
offerings. The sabhā of Perumūr alias Rājanārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Naḍuvinmalai Perumūr-
nāḍu received the gift for the service. The donor belonged to Va[ḷḷu]vanpākkam in Oḻugaiyūr-nāḍu, a
sub-division of Paḍuvūr-kōṭṭam.

l.ta:
tiruvāykkeḻvi muṉṉākat tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī
kulottuṅkacoḻa tevaṟkku

āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu
maṇaviṟ koṭṭattup paḻaiyanūr nāṭṭup paḻaiyanūr āḷuṭaiyār
tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār tiruvaraṅkil aṇṭamuṟanimintaruḷi[ya*] nāyanārkkut
tiruvantit tiru

voṭṭattukkup piṭṭamutu amutuceytaruḷukaikku imaṇṭalattu paṭuvūr
koṭṭattu oḻukaiyūr nāṭṭu vaḷḷuvanpākkattu vaḷḷuvanpākkiḻān tevanāṉa
teluṅkārayaṉ makaṉ uṭaiyapiḷḷaiyāna vāṇakovaraiyan pakkal
innāyanārkku naṭuviṉmalaiperumurnāṭṭup perumurāna
irācanārāyaṇaccaturvetimaṅkalattu mahācapaiyom innimantañ
celuttukaikkuk kaikkoṇṭa aṉṟāṭu naṟpaḻaṅkācu

eḻupatuṅ kaikkoṇṭu piṭṭamutu amutu ceytaruḷa nāḷoṉṟukku añcupattām
ūṟkālāle kuṟuṇi [n]ānāḻi uriy nellum neyyamutu uḻakkum uppu
āḻākkum orupalakkaruppukkaṭṭiyum aṭaikkāyamutu pākkuppattum ilaiyamutu
paṟṟukkālum cantiratittavarai celuttakkaṭavomākavum orukuṭi iruntatākilum
irunta kuṭiye celuttak kaṭavatākavum ūraiviṭṭup puṟampe poyiruntomā-

kilum iruntiṭ[ā nā]ḷile iruntu celuttak kaṭavomākavum ippaṭi
innimantañ celuttak kaṭavomākac cammatittom perumurāṉa irācanārāyaṇac
caturvetimaṅkalattu mahā

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 477A of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttaṇi Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the north wall of the first prakara of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Fragment. Records sale of tax exempted land at Kāraippākkam by temple authorities for a flower
garden.

l.ta:
ttut tuvātaciyum peṟṟa cataiyattu veḷḷikkiḻamai nāḷ
jayaṅkoṇṭa co •••••

āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyanā[yaṉā*]r koyil tāṉattārum uḷḷūr
māhecurarum irun • • •

kāraippākkam erikiḻ ciṟupuḷḷūr uttamanampināyaṉ nantavaṉappaṭṭikkut
teṟkum meṟkum • • •
iḻicci viṟṟu ṉantavaṉap paṟṟu āka viṭṭa nantāvaṉapurattu

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 478 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and built in the middle. Gift of 4 cows for a lamp by Chēdirāyaṉ Vaḍuganāta-
nantipaṉman.

l.ta:
ṣanāyaṟṟu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu maṇaviṟkoṭṭattu paḻaiyaṉūr
nāṭṭup paḻaiyaṉūr uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyanāya-

•••cetirāyaṉ [va]ṭukanāta ṉantipaṉmaneṉ iṉṉāyanār tiruvaraṅkil
aṇṭamuṟanimintaruḷi[ya*] nāyanār tirumuṉpu vaitta cantiviḷakku

•••• m kaikkoṇṭu cantirātittavarai pala cantikaḷtoṟum muṭṭuppaṭāmal
cantiviḷakku erippomāka ippacu nālum kaikkoṇṭu •••

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 479 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.

p.b: Vijayanagara Virapratapa Devaraya, Saka 1349, Plavanga = 1427 A.D.

inscription.p:
Beginning of this inscription is not traceable. Records a royal order (rāyasam) saying that the
income from several taxes from the dēvadāna villages and the revenue income (mutal) from the villages
held by the jīvitakāṟar, adikārikaḷ, ūravar, and tānikar should be used for the temples themselves for
conducting worship and festivals, etc. It was also ordered that the temple villages (dēvamaṇḍalam)
should not given for lease. The taxes included veṭṭi, kaṭṭāyam, arisikāṇam, naṟpasu, naṟkiḍā,
nallerumai, āyam, talaiyārikkam, sāda and pala-kāṇikkai. Mentions that the royal order was executed
by Ariyappa Daṇṇāyakkar and Bhikshāvritti Aiyagaḷ.

l.ta:
āyirattu muṉṉūṟṟu
nāṟpattu oṉpatiṉ meṟcellāniṉṟa pala

•••ṟ coḻamaṇṭalattu vaṭakarai maṇaviṟ koṭṭattu meṉmalaip paḻaiyaṉūr
nāṭṭil uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nāyaṉārko-

•••vipūtikāṇikkai [ka]ḻi[t]tu atikāri kaṭaikkūṭṭaṉ aṅkacālaiivakaḷai nokki
atikāri jo[ṭi]mukappārvai viceṣātāyam

veṭṭi kaṭṭāyam aricikāṇam nallerutu naṟpacu naṟkiṭā nallerumai āyam
ta[lai]yārikkam cātam maṟṟum palakāṇikkai inta vakaippaṭi mutal uḷḷatum
jīvitakāṟar atikārikaḷ uḷvaritāṉikar kaikkoṇṭa

l mutal
uḷḷatum uṭaiyār śrīpaṇṭarattile mutaliṭṭuk koṇṭu

raiyum vayiravanāyiṉāraiyumuḷḷiṭṭuk koṇṭu mahāpūcai
tiruppaṇi tiṅkaḷt tivasam tirunāḷ nitta uṟcavaṅkaḷum tāḻvaṟa naṭattik

vamaṇṭalaṅkaḷ iyātoruvaṟkum kuṭukkakkaṭavatu
allavākavum inta irāyasappaṭi cantiṟātittava-

raiyum naṭakkakaṭavatu ākavum itukku akitam paṇṇiṉār uṇṭu āṉāl
civatturoki kurutturoki itukku akitam paṇṇiṉār keṅkaikaraiyil kurāl
pacuvai koṉṟa pāvattile poka kaṭavarkaḷ cokkanāyiṉār tiruvāṇaikku
tapp[ā]rāka kaṭavum ippaṭi ivai paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 480 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Records that Gopparasar-Mādi-arasar consecrated an image, for the merit of Mallaṇa-Uḍaiyar.
Also refers to the renovation of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 481 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Registers a sale of land by the ūrār of Paḻaiyanūr in Paḻaiyanūr-nāḍu, a dēvadāna
of the Tiruvālaṅgāḍu temple in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu panniraṇṭāvatu
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu maṇaviṟ koṭṭattu āḷuṭaiyār
tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiyār tevatāṉa-

m paḻaiyanūr nāṭṭup paḻaiyanūr ūrom vilaiyāvaṇak kaiyyeḻuttu eṅkaḷūr
vaṭakaḻaniyiṟ kaṇṭarātittan kaḻaniyil nilam kīḻpāṟkellai ceṟuviṟ kīḻavarampu
teṟku nokkik kaṭampa maṭuvile taicca varampukku meṟkum tenpāṟke-

llaitirukkāḷattippiccan vāykkāl kaṭa[m]pamaṭu pāynta vāykkālukkut ten
varampukku vaṭakku melpāṟ kellai munmaṭaikkuṇṭilil melvarampukkuk
kiḻakkuk [ka]yaṟ pākkattu maṭappurattiṟ kiḻvarampukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai • • •

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 482 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the south wall of the first prakārā of the Naṭarājaśvamin shrine in the Vaṭāranēśvara temple at
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu.
inscription.p:
Gift of a sum of 40 kāsu for a lamp to the shrine of Tiruvarangil-Aṇḍamuṟa-nimirandaruḷina-
Nāyanār at Tiruvālangāḍu in Tiruppaḻaiyanūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam. The donor was
Aṟanilaivisāgan Tirailōkyamallan Vatsarājan of Arumbākkam in [I]llattūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of
Kunṟavattana-kōṭṭam, who is said to have renderd the Bhārata into elegant Tamil and found out the path
of Śiva (i.e., the Śaiva creed). Two maṉṟāḍis received the money for maintaining the lamp.

l.ta:
kaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ
koṇṭaruḷi virāapiṣekamum vicaiyāapiṣekamum paṇṇiyaruḷiya

ṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu kuṉṟavattanak koṭṭattu pāratantaṉṉaiaruntamuḻpaṭuttic
civaṉeṟikaṇṭa [a]ṟanilaivicākaṉ

ṉūr nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭu uṭaiyaṉāyaṉār tiruvaraṅkil

On the third gōpura of the Vataranyesvara temple at Tiruvalangadu, right of entrance.
inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Contains only the historical introduction of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 484 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the third gōpura of the Vataranyesvara temple at Tiruvalangadu, right of entrance.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman Kulōttuṅga I, Year 46 = 1116 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Sale of land to the temple at Tiruvālaṅgāḍu in Naḍuvilma[lai] by the
sabhā of Rājanārāyaṇa-chaturvedimangalam in Naḍuvilmalai-Perumūr-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 485 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the third gōpura of the Vataranyesvara temple at Tiruvalangadu, right of entrance.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rajakesarivarman Rājādhirāja I, Year 31 = 1049 A.D.

inscription.p:
Sale of land, free of taxes, by the sabhā of [Sin]gaḷāntaka-chaturvēdimangalam in
Naḍuvinmalai-Peru[mūr]-nāḍu. One Ādittan Pichāṉ had donated the sale price to the temple of
Tiruvālangāḍu in Paḻaiyaṉūr.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 486 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the third gōpura of the Vataranyesvara temple at Tiruvalangadu, right of entrance.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rajakesarivarman Rājādhirāja I, Year 28 = 1046 A.D.

inscription.p:
Sale of land by the assembly of Nittavinōda-chaturvēdimangalam in Mēnmalai Mēlūr-nāḍu to the
temple. The donor who paid the sale price of the land seems to be Rājēntira-Kaḷappāḷan and the land was
meant for maintaining a water-shed.

tiṅkaḷertarutaṉṟoṅkal veṇkuṭaikkiḻ nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ
puṇar

tu maṉṉu pallūḻiyuḷ teṉ[ṉa]var

mepukaḻirāmakuṭamuvar keṭa muṉi

tāka[va*] mallaṉuñ cakkevukenṟāṅkarum paṭai[yālāṅka]-

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. 487 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the third gōpura of the Vataranyesvara temple at Tiruvalangadu, right of entrance.
inscription.p:
Sale of land, freed from taxes, by the ūrār of Paḻaiyanūr to an adigārigaḷ called Neṟkuṉṟaṅ-
kiḻān Kāman Chandirasēkaran of Mēlūr in Mēlūr-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam who had deposited the sale price
in the temple treasury. The latter was permitted to reclaim the land, probably for maintaining some
temple service.

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 495 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the third gōpura of the Vataranyesvara temple at Tiruvalangadu, left of entrance.
inscription.p:
Sale of tax-free land by the ūrār of Paḻaiyanūr to adigārigaḷ Kuvaḷai-vēli-uḍaiyān Kōyil
Chittanār alias Pañchavan-mūvēndavēḷān, who assigned the same to the temple for the supply of water
from Pālāṟu.

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 496 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the third gōpura of the Vataranyesvara temple at Tiruvalangadu, left of entrance.

p.b: Chōḻa Rājādhiraja I = c. 11th Century A.D.

inscription.p: Damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 497 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the west wall of the Bhramaralakamba shrine in the Vataranyesvara temple at Tiruvalangadu.

p.b:
Vijayanagara. Tirumalaiyadeva-Maharaya, Saka 1493, Prajapati, Makara, Purva 7, Monday,
Asuvati = 1572, January 21.

inscription.p:
This is a record of poliyūṭṭu agreement deed given by the trustees of the Tiruvālaṅgāḍu temple
given to the Head of the Uḷmaḍam (maṭha), Immaḍi-Dharma-Śivāchārya who gave a sum of 1000 pon to
the temple. The money was used for digging a tank and its channels and the additional income (atika-
lakkam) was used for various expenses in connection with some service instituted in the name of the
donor according to the stipulations of the grant. It was also agreed to give one fourth of the prasādam to
the maṭha. The donor came in the lineage of the Śaiva teacher Anantaśivāchārya and his pupil
Ponnambala-Dharmaśivāchārya.

l.ta:
ṇṭalecura
rācātirācarācapara[mecura] pūṟuvatekṣaṇa paccima uttara camuttirātipati
śrī[virappiṟutāpa tiru]malaiy tevamakārāyar piṟitivirācciyam paṇṇiyaruḷāniṉṟa
cakāttam

mel cellāniṉṟa piṟacoṟpati caṅvaccarattu makaraṉāyiṟṟu
pūṟuvapakṣattu cattamiyum comavāramum acuvatinakṣattiramum peṟṟa yiṟṟai nāḷ
ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu vaṭakarai maṇavil koṭṭattu paḻaiya-

ṉūrṉāṭṭil paḻaiyaṉūr tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭai[nāyiṉā]rkoyil āticaṇṭecurar
aruḷāl śrīkāriyam ceyvārum tāṉattārum yimmaṇṭalattu yikkoṭṭattu
[yiṉ]ṉāṭṭil uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya nayiṉār koyil uḷmaṭamuñ
cikecamakāmuṉi ākiṉamāṉa śrīmāveṭāṭalicuracaraṇāravinta tiyāṉa
pārāyaṇarāṉa patamvākkiyappiṟamāṇa pārāvārapāriṇarāṉa
śrīmāvetamāṟkapiṟati

pakācāriyarāṉa vetāntācāriyarāṉa aṉantacivācāriyar
piriya ciṣ[ya*]rāṉa pon-

ṉampala taṉmacivācāriyaciṣyarāṉa yimmaṭa taṉmacivācāriyar ayyaṉ
avarkaḷukku poliyūṭṭu taṉmacāsaṉam paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi
tevamaṇṭala[ti]cammāṉa •••ṭaiyūril taṉma civacamuttiram navamāka
erikaṭṭivi[tta]tukkum āṟu kālukaḷ ayapaṭiyaḷturavukaḷ kaṭṭivittatukkum ceṉṟa
poṉ

yinta āyiram poṉ ceṉṟu •••paṇṇi payir eṟi pūṟuvalakkam niṅkalāka
atikalakkam kaṇṭuvarukaiyil viṟtikku cilavāka uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkāṭuṭaiya
nayiṉāṟkum ṉācciyārva-

ṇṭārkuḻal ṉācciyāṟkum taṅkaḷ upayamāka kālacantikkupiṟaku uccikkālattukku
muṉṉāka taṉma civvācāriyar ayyaṉ [ca]ntikaṭṭaḷai yiṭukayil cāttukāppu
e[ṇ]ṇai teṅkāyika paḻam

taṅkaḷ upayamāka vacantamaṇṭapamum kaṭṭivittu vicuvaṉātaṉaiyum
vicālākṣi ammaṉaiyum eḻuntaruḷappaṇṇukayil ṉāḷ

kku Ø mūṉṟumā tiruviḷakku
eṇṇai iraṇṭumā āka canti oṉṟukku

eṇṇai ney teṅkāy paḻam cakkarai
tenkaṟikāyimel veccatukkum

yintappaṇam oṉṟekālum amutupaṭi eḻukuṟuṇiyum ṉāḷtoṟum
naṭattivarakkaṭavomākavum amutuceytaruḷiṉa piṟacātam taṉmakaṟtā
tanmacivācāriyar ayyaṉ maṭattukku ṉālil oṉṟu naṭattikkaṭavomākavum
toppu tiruṉāḷ kāttikai māsam cotinakṣattirattile naṭattivara

naṭattivarakkaṭavomākavum yinta taṉmattukku yātoruttar akitam paṇṇinavan
keṅkai karaiyile kārāmpacuvatai paṇṇiṉa toṣattile

poka kaṭavaṉākavum yikkoyil tāṉattār yivai kūḻāṇṭār eḻuttu yivai
kūḻāṇṭār eḻuttu yivai tāṉattār eḻuttu yivaitāṉattār eḻuttu koyil

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 498 of 1905.)
Tiruvālaṅgāḍu, Tiruttani Taluk, Chittoor District.
On the dhvajastambha in the Vatāranyēśvara temple at Tiruvālañgāḍu.
inscription.p: Records the setting up of a bronze dhvajastambha with its pedestal.

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 499 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prakārā in the Jambukēśvara temple at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
Sale of land and house-sites belonging to their shares by Tāmaṇi Tēvaṉ alias Niṟai-uḍai-chōḻa-
pēraraiyaṉ and his younger brothers who had kāṇi rights in Kārichāttaṉ-kuṟai in Kīḻpalāṟu of Pāchchil-
kūṟṟam in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, to the temple of Tiruvāṉaikkāvuḍaiya-nāyaṉār in Pāṇḍiyakulāchaṉi-
vaḷanāḍu. It is said that the village had twelve shares (paṅgu) in all, in which the present sellers had four
shares. A number of people in this and neighboring villages signed the document as witnesses and
Perumāṉ Aḻagan alias Maṇavāḷa-muttaraiyaṉ gave surety deed for the payment of the sale price
(kiḻipuṇai-tīṭṭu). The reason for the sale is not given.

l.note: rājarāja = [Picture.] This short form to denote Rājarāja is found in some following inscriptions too.
l.ta:
yum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa attattu nāḷ
pāṇṭiyakulācaṉi vaḷanāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil
āticaṇṭe
l.note: rājarāja = [Picture.] This short form to denote Rājarāja is found in some following inscriptions too.

teṉ[pāṟ]kellai innāyaṉār

tevatāṉam tirunāmattukkāṇi appūtikuṟaikku vaṭakkil ciṟṟāṟṟukku vaṭakkum
mipāṟkellaiappūtikuṟai ellaikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai āṟṟu nīroṭu

muṉṉāḷil innāyaṉār civanāmattil viṟṟa paṅku

ippaṅku nālum nikki niṉṟa

inta kācu [na]lāyiramum

ratevarkaṉmikaḷuk[ku tāmaṇi] tevaṉāṉa niṟai
uṭaic coḻapperayaṉum ivaṉ tampi uṭaiyāṉ [rāja rāja*] nāṭāḻvāṉum

uṭaiyāṉ ātiyāṉa coḻapāṇṭiya perayaṉum ivaṉ tampi ti-

riyairuntāṉum kulaiyaṉ tāḻiyāṉa vitiviṭaṅkanāṭāḻvaṉum ivvaṉaivom
ippaṅku nālāl vanta nilattukkum maṉaikkum innilattu meṉokkiṉa maramuṅ
kiḻṉokkiṉa kiṇaṟum maṉṟuṅ kaṉṟunilaipāḻum āṟum āṟiṭupaṭukaiyum uṭpaṭa
ikkācu nālāyiramume vilaiyāvutākavum ituve

porumāvaṟutip poruḷcelavolaiyāvatākavum ituvallatu veṟu

vilaiporuḷaṟakkoṇṭu viṟṟu vilai

māṇa icaivutiṭṭukkuṭuttom
uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil āti-

ratevarkaṉmikaḷukku maṇitevaṉāṉa niṟaiuṭaiccoḻapperayaṉum
ivaṉ tampi uṭaiyāṉ rājarāja* nāṭāḻvāṉum periyāṉ nārāyaṇaṉum ivaṉ
tampi ampalavaṉ poṉṉampala nāṭāḻvāṉum āti āṉa
co[ḻa]pāṇṭiyapperayaṉum ivaṉ tampi tiriyairuntāṉum kulaiyaṉ tāḻiyāṉa
vi-

tiviṭaṅka nāṭāḻvāṉum ivvaṉaivom ippaṅku nālukkum ivarkaḷ
veṇumeṉṟapote

tevarkaṉmikaḷukku
tāmaṇitevaṉāṉa niṟaiuṭaic coḻapperayaṉum ivaṉ tampi uṭaiyāṉ
[rājarāja*]nāṭāḻvāṉum periyāṉ nāyaṇaṉum ivaṉ tampi ampalavaṉ
poṉṉampala nāṭāḻvāṉum uṭaiyāṉ ātiyāṉa coḻapāṇṭiyap peraya-

num ivaṉ tampi tiriyairuntāṉum kulaiyaṉ tāḻiyāṉa vitiviṭaṅkanāṭāḻvāṉum
ivvaṉaivom ippiramāṇa icaivu tiṭṭeḻutiṉeṉ mātānamaṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ
kaḻumalaviṭaṅkaṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai tāmaṇitevaṉ [ni]ṟaiuṭaic
coḻapperayaṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku kiḻāṟṟūr kāvaṟkāṇi uṭaiya cittaṉ
perumāḷā-

ṉa kiḻāṟṟūr nāṭāḻvāṉeḻuttu ivaiuṭaiyāṉ āti āṉa coḻapāṇṭiyap
perayaṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku ivai tiruveḷḷaṟai piṭākai putukkuṭiyāṉa
inta irājentiracoḻa maṅkalattuk kāvakāṇi uṭaiya perumāṉ aḻakaṉ
maṇavāḷa muttarayaṉeḻuttu itu uṭaiyāṉ rājarāja
l.note: rājarāja = [Picture]. This short form to denote ṟājarāja is found in some following inscriptions too.

l.ta:
maikku itu kuṭantai uṭaiyāṉ [tiru]cciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ
mātuyarantittāṉeḻuttu itu periyāṉ nārāyaṇaṉ sayiñai āṉamaikkum
kuvalaiyaṉ tāḻiyāṉa sayiñai āṉamaikkum ivai uṭaiyār
tiruvāṉaikāvuṭaiya nāyaṉār tevatānam tirunāmat[tukkāṇi] kiḻāṟ[ṟūr] kiḻavaṉ
cuntaraviṇṇa-

ṉ niṉṟa nampi eḻuttu itu
tiriyairuntāṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku uṭaiyārśrīkayilāyamuṭ[ai*]yār koyil
pūcikkum catācivapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ituperiyāṉ ampalavaṉāṉa poṉṉampala
nāṭāḻvāṉ sayiñai āṉam[ai*]kku ivai kiḻāṟṟūr araya[ṉ eḻu]t[tu] ippaṭi
aṟiveṉ turaiyūruṭai-

yāṉ vaṭakaraināṭṭu muventaveḷāṉeṉ ippaṭi aṟiveṉ taṇṭaṅkuṟai
vikkiramacoḻanallūruṭaiyāṉ viḷānāṭṭu veḷāṉeḻuttu ippaṭi aṟiveṉ
ālaṅkuṭaiyāṉ miṉavaṉ vallavaraiyaṉeṉ ippaṭi aṟivaṉ periyāḻvāṉ
āram • • veḷāṉ ippaṭi aṟiveṉ tapañāyakkuṟai aracūr ki • • tillaippirāṉ
āṇṭāṉ cattiyakara-

ṉapperumāṉeṉ ippaṭi aṟivēṉ śrīnaraciṅkapaṭṭaṉeṉ ippaṭi aṟiveṉ
icaṇaikkuṟaittillaināyakaṉ cempaiyaṇṭāṉeṉ ippaṭi aṟiveṉ aracūr
kemutavaṉ āḷumpirāṉ poṟkoyil nampi eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 499A of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prakārā in the Jambukēśvara temple at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
This is related to the above inscription referring to the deed called kīḻipuṇai-tīṭṭu given by
Maṇavāḷa-muttaraiyaṉ who had kāṇi rights in Pudukkuḍi alias Rājēndirachōḻa-maṅgalam, a hamlet of
Tiruveḷḷaṟai in Vaḍavaḻi-nāḍu. He gave the deed asserting that he received in one kiḻi (pouch) the entire
sum of 4000 kāsu, the price amount of the four shares, from the temple trustees and handed over the
same to the sellers of the share.

l.ta:
rateva kaṉmikaḷukku
rā[ja*]rā[ja] vaḷa nāṭṭu vaṭavaḻināṭṭu tiruveḷḷaṟaippiṭākai putukkuṭiyāṉa
rā[je]ntira coḻamaṅkalattuk kāṇi uṭaiya kaḷḷaril perum[ā]ṉ aḻa-

kaṉāṉa maṇavāḷa muttarayaṉeṉ kiḻippuṇaittiṭṭukkuṭutta paricāvatu kāricāttaṉ
kuṟaiyil oṭamuṭaiyāril tāmaṇi tevaṉāṉa niṟ[ai] uṭaic coḻapperayaṉum
ivaṉ tampi uṭaiyāṉ rājarāja

nāṭāḻvāṉum periyāṉ nārāyaṇaṉum ivaṉ
tampi am[pā]lavaṉ poṉṉampala nāṭāḻvāṉum uṭaiyāṉ āti āṉa-

coḻapāṇ[ṭi]yapperayaṉum ivaṉ tampi tiriya iruntāṉum kulaiyaṉ tāḻiyāṉa
vitiviṭaṅka nāṭāḻvāṉum pakkal kāricāttaṅkuṟaiyil viṟṟa paṅku nālukkum

tevarkaṉmikaḷ pakkal oru kiḻippaṭa vāṅki inta tāmaṇitevaṉāṉa-
neṟi uṭaiccoḻapperayaṉum ivaṉ tampi uṭaiyāṉ rājarāja

nāṭāḻvāṉum
periyāṉ nārayaṇaṉum ivaṉ tampi ampalavaṉ poṉṉampala nāṭāḻvāṉ
uṭaiyāṉ ātiyāṉa coḻapāṇṭiya perayaṉum ivaṉ tampi tiriyairuntāṉum
kulaiyaṉtāḻiyāṉa vitiviṭaṅka nāṭāḻvāṉum ivvaṉai-

varpakkalum kuṭuttu kuṭuttapaṭikku kiḻippuṇaiy niṉṟu
kiḻippuṇaittiṭṭukkuṭutteṉ uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil
āticaṇṭe

muttarayaṉeṉ ivai eṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi aṟiveṉ civañāṉakuṟai

aracūr kavuciyaṉ tillaippirāṉ āṇṭār cattivaṉapperumāḷ[e]ḻuttu ippaṭi
aṟiveṉ civañānakkuṟaikkoḻuvaṉ āḷumpirāṉ poṟkoyil nampi eḻuttu
ippaṭi aṟiveṉ vaḷavaṉ nallūr ālaṅkuṭaiyāṉ miṉavaṉ pallavar[ai]yaṉeṉ
ippaṭi aṟiveṉ ta • • miḻalai nampiyeṉ ippa-

eḻuttu ik[kācunā]lāyiramum kaikkoṇṭamaikku ivai neṟi

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 500 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prākarā in the Jambukēśvara temple at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
Records that Tuṟaiyūr-uḍaiyān Arayaṉ Nittakalliyāṇaṉ alias Pāṇḍiyaṉ Rājarājaṉ made a gift of
dry land measuring 7 vēli and odd to the temple of Tiruvāṉaikkāvuḍaiya-nāyanār in Viḷā-nāḍu in
Pāṇḍikulāchaṉi-vaḷanāḍu. It is said the gift land is part of the land that had been purchased by his
mudaliyār (father) and that subsequent to his death it became the property of the present seller and his
mother.

l.ta:
ttu navamiyum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa urocaṇināḷ
rājarāja vaḷanāṭṭu-

ppācciṟ kūṟṟattuk kīḻpilāṟṟut tuṟaiyūruṭaiyāṉ arayaṉ
nittakalliyāṇaṉāṉa pāṇṭiyaṉ rājarājaṉeṉ taṉmatāṉap piramāṇa icaivu
tiṭṭu pāṇṭikulācaṉi

vaḷanāṭṭu viḷānāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkavuṭaiyanāyaṉār koyil
āticaṇṭe
tevarkaṉmikaḷukku iṟṟai nāḷāl nāṉ uṭaiyār tiruvāṉai-

kkāvuṭaiya ṉāyaṉāṟkut taṉmatāṉamākak kuṭutta nilamāvatu innāṭṭut
taṇṭakkuṟaiyil taṇṭaṅkuṟai uṭaiyāṉ tevāṇṭaṉāṉa teṉkaraināṭṭu
veḷāṉum

taṇṭaṅkuṟai uṭaiyāṉ perumāḷum ceyyāmaṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ teṟṟi
poṟkoyil veḷāṉum uḷḷiṭṭār pakkal muṉṉāḷ eṅkaḷ mutaliyār pāṇ-

ṭiyarāyar vilaikoṇṭu uṭaiyārāy ivar apāvattu eṉṉutum eṅkaḷ
mātākkaḷutumāṉanilattu uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkā[u]ṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku
ivvā[ṭ]-
ṭai[mārkaḻi]mātattu nāṉ taṉmatāṉamāka [nī]r v[ā]rttukkuṭutta puṉcey nilam
innilam eḻe iraṇ[ṭu*]māvukkum icainta kiḻpāṟkellai murimuriveli-

kku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai kāveri niroṭukālukku vaṭakkum mipāṟkellai
innāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇiyāl [appūti ku]ṟai ellaikku kiḻakku-

m vaṭapāṟkellai paḻaṅkāveriniroṭu kālukkutteṟkkum āka icainta
innāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭuvuṭpaṭṭa nilattu ta[ṇṭaṅ] kuṟai uṭaiyāṉ āḷa-

ppiṟantāṉāṉa teṉkaraināṭṭu veḷāṉ uḷḷiṭṭār kāṇi āṉa nilam mukkāṇi ¿ nilam
kālearaimā araikkāṇi nīkki mikutikkuṟai uḷḷaṭaṅ-

inta murimuriveli ellaikkukkiḻakkum māvoṭu toṭṭamum
[i]lāṭarā[ya]ṉ paṟṟu(m) eṉṟu perkūvappaṭṭu eṅkaḷutāṉa puṉceynilattu
appūti-

yār oḻukkaikku meṟku nercer mikutikkuṟai uḷḷaṭaṅkaviṭṭa nilam orumā m āka
ināyaṉāṟku taṉmatāṉamāka [nīr]vārttu kuṭutta puṉcey nilam

innilam e[ḻe] iraṇṭumāvum mikuti kuṟai uḷḷaṭaṅka ivvāṭṭai mārkaḻi
mātate taṉmatāṉamāka nīr vārttuk kuṭuttu innāḷ varaiyum
ṇam paṇṇikkuṭāte niṉṟamaiyil iṟṟai nāḷāl taṉmatāna

yūruṭaiyāṉ pāṇṭiyarāyaṉeṉ innilam eḻe iraṇṭumāvil meṉokkiṉa
maramuṅ kīḻnokkiṉ kiṇaṟukaḷum maṉaiyum maṉaip paṭappaiyum āṟum āṟiṭu-

paṭukaiyum potuvum potuvāriyum pākamum pākāciriyam maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa
u[ṭai]maikaḷum [uṭ]paṭa taṉmatāṉamākak kuṭuttamaiyil iṉilattukku ivarkaḷ
veṇṭi-

māṇam paṇṇik kuṭukkak kaṭaveṉākavum
kuṭuttilenākil inta taṉmatānap [pira]māṇa icaivutiṭṭe

(taṉ)matāṉappiramāṇa icaivutiṭṭukkuṭutteṉ uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya
nāyaṉār koyil āticaṇṭe

yaṉ nittakalliyāṇaṉāṉa pāṇṭiyarā[ja]ṉeṉ ippaṭikku ivai
tuṟaiyūruṭaiyāṉ araiyaṉ nittakalliyāṇaṉāṉa pāṇṭiyarāyaṉeḻuttu
ittaṉmatāṉa-

icaivutiṭṭeḻutiṉeṉ ivvūr ūṟkaṇakku mutteyiluṭaiyāṉ
maṇṭaiyāḻvāṉ mātuyaraṉtittāṉāṉa tillaikkūttap piriyaṉe eḻu-

ttu ippaṭi aṟivukkut taṇ[ṭa*]ṅ kuṟai uṭaiyāṉ teṉkaraiṉāṭṭu ve(ḷ)ḷāṉ
sayiñai āṉamaikku ivai ciṅkappirāṉ paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ivvūṟk kāṇi u-

ṭaiya nakaruṭaiyāṉ kuṉittāṉ ampalakkūttaṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku ivai
iruñciūṟ tiruvaraṅkamuṭaiyāṉ kovintapaṭṭaṉeṉ ippa-

ṭi aṟiveṉ araṅkuṭaiyāṉ [vammi] tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉeṉ itu
taṇ[ṭa]ṅkuṟai uṭaiyāṉ peṟṟāṉ perumāṉ aṟivu sayiñai āṉamai-

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 501 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prākarā in the Jambukēśvara temple at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
This records a sale of dry land by Kaḻaṉivāyil-uḍaiyān and his younger brother to the temple.
The sellers had kāṇi rights in Ichaṇaikkuṟai, a kuṟaipaṟṟu of Kīḻpalāṟu in Pāchchil-kūṟṟam in Rājarāja-
vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
ṟa cotināḷ teṉkarai paṇṭikulācaṉi vaḷanāṭṭu uṭaiyār
tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyi[l] āti

vaḷa nāṭṭup pācciṟ kūṟṟattuk kiḻpalāṟṟuk kuṟaippaṟṟu
icaṇaikkuṟaik kāṇi uṭaiya kaḻaṉivāyiluṭaiyāṉ a-

ṉumaṉamuṭaiyāṉum ivaṉ tampi nampiyum ivviruvom puṉcey
nilavilai

kkuṟait talaikkuṟaiyil nāṅkaḷ viṟṟukkuṭukkiṟa nilattukku icaitta
kiḻpāṟkellai ūṟpotu talattuṟai mukkāl nilattu

kku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai ciṟṟāṟu niroṭukālukku vaṭakkum
mipāṟkellaiūṟp[o]tu amaṇkuṭaiyāṉtaṭi nila[t]-

[tu]kkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai vaṭavāṟṟu [ni]roṭu kālukkutte[ṟku]m āka
iṉṉāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭuvuṭpa[ṭ]ṭa taṭi

innilam oṉṟe iraṇṭumā mun(ra)tirikaiyum iṟṟai
ṉāḷāl viṟṟukkuṭuttuk koḷvatāṉa emmi-

vāyiluṭaiyāṉ aṉumaṉamuṭaiyāṉum ivaṉ tampi nampiyum ivviruvom
iṉṉilam oṉṟe iraṇṭumā muntirikaikkum

ikkācu iraṇṭāyirattu irunūṟṟāṟume vilai[y]āvutākavum ituve
porumāvaṟutip poruṭ celavolaiyāvutāka[vu]m i-

tuvallatu veṟu p[o]rumāvaṟutipporuṭ celavolai kāṭṭakkaṭavatallātakavum
iṉṉilattu meṉokkiṉa maramuṅ

kiḻṉokkiṉa kiṇaṟukaḷum maṉaikaḷum potuvum potātiyum [maṟ]ṟum
epperppaṭṭa urimaikaḷum akappaṭa mikutikkuṟaivu-
[ḷ]ḷaṭaṅka nilam oṉṟe iraṇṭumā muntirikaikkum ivar veṇṭumpote

māṇamāvatākavum
ippaṭi cammatittu vilaikkaṟa viṟṟup poruḷaṟakko-

māṇa icaivuttiṭṭuk kuṭuttom uṭaiyār
tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya nā[ya]ṉār koyil āti

varkaṉmikaḷukku kaḻaṉivāyiluṭaiyāṉ aṉumaṉamuṭaiyāṉum ivaṉ tampi
nampiyum ivviruvom ippaṭikku ivai kaḻaṉi-

vāyiluṭaiyāṉ aṉumaṉamuṭaiyāṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku i[vvū]rk kaṇakku
vaṭikuṭaiyāṉ kaṇakku koṭṭuṭaiyā-

•••aṟivu •••poṉṉampalakūtta[ṉ] eḻuttu itu kaḻaṉivāyiluṭaiyāṉ nampi
sayiñai āṉamaikku

ivai nāṇaṟkuṟai aracūr keḻutavaṉ tirucciṟṟampalanampi eḻuttu itu
meṉāṭṭuṭaiyaṉ aṟivu sayiñai āṉamaikku-

m ippaṭi aṟivukkum ivai miceṅkiḷināṭṭu veḷāṉeḻuttu itu
pāṭaluṭaiyāṉ amutāṇṭāṉ aṟivusa-

yiñai āṉamaikkum ippaṭi aṟivukku ivai pūṇṭi uṭaiyāṉ
pañcanetivāṉeḻuttu itu pālaiyūr ki-

ḻavaṉ kāḷaṉ aṟivu sayiñai āṉamaikkum ippaṭi aṟivukkum ivai oḷipati
uṭaiyāṉ iravāṭuvāṉ tiruv[e]ṇ

ṇāmalaip perumāṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai taṇṭaṅkuṭaiyāṉeṉ ippaṭi aṟi
•••viḻupparaiyaṉeṉ

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 502 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prākarā in the Jambukēśvara temple at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
Sale of dry land, 3 vēli and odd, by the ūrār of Ichaṇaikkuṟai to the temple for 6210 kāsu. Many
meembers of the ūrār body have signed the document.

l.ta:
tamaiyum cevvāyk [kiḻa]maiyum peṟṟa coti nāḷ
teṉkarai pāṇ[ṭi]yakulācaṉi vaḷa[nāṭṭu u]ṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya

vaḷanāṭṭup pācciṟ kūṟṟattuk kiḻpalāṟṟukkuṟaipaṟṟu icaṇaikkuṟai
ūravarom puṉce[y] nilavilai[p]

kkuṟaittalaikkuṟaiyil nāṅkaḷ viṟkiṉ[ṟa nilaṅkaḷukku] icainta
[kiḻ]pāṟkellaimalaiyāḷaril taiyakkoṇṭanampiceṭṭiyār nilattukku meṟkum
teṉpāṟkellai ciṟṟāṟṟu niroṭukālukku vaṭakkum mipāṟkellai

kaṭampaṅkuṭaiyāṉ iniyā[ṉ] peṟṟāṉ nila[ttu]kku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
vaṭavāṟṟu niroṭu kālukkut teṟkum āka icainta ināṉkellai[yuḷ
naṭu]vuṭpaṭṭa taṭi nālināl nilam

ṭṭu mitupoy pūvaṇamuṭaiyāṉ taṭi nila[ttu]kku icainta kiḻpāṟkellai
koṇṭanampi ceṭṭiyār nilattukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai [ci]ṟṟāṟṟu
niroṭukālukku vaṭakkum mipāṟkellai meṉāṭṭarayaṉṉilattukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭa-

pāṟkellai vaṭavāṟṟu niroṭukālukkut teṟkum āka icainta
iṉṉāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭuvuṭpaṭṭa nilam

iṉṉi[la]m nālumākkāṇiyum iṅku
viṭṭu mitupoy amaṇ-kuṭaiyāṉ taṭinilattukkum curaikkuṭai-

yāṉ taṭi nilattukkum kalaiyaṉ tulaivaṉ taṭinilattukkum icainta
kiḻpāṟkellaikaḻaṇivāyiluṭaiyāṉ uḷḷiṭṭār nilattukku meṟku
teṉpāṟkellai ciṟṟāṟṟu niroṭukālukku vaṭakkum [mi]pāṟkellai koṇnampi
ceṭ-

ṭiyār nilattukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai vaṭavāṟṟu niroṭukālukkut teṟkum
āka icainta innāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭuvuṭpaṭṭa taṭi oṉṟiṉāl nilam

inta nilam
oṉṟe orumā[mu]kkāṇi araikāṇiyum iṅku viṭṭu mitu poy

kalattuṟai mukkālukku icainta kīḻpāṟkellai āḻvār cāttaṅkuṭiyuṭaiyār
arintavaṉ [vi]ḻupparaiyar nilattukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellaiciṟṟāṟṟu

tukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellaivaṭavāṟṟu niroṭukālukku teṟkkum āka
icainta iṉṉāṉkellaiyu[ḷ] naṭuvuṭpaṭṭa nilam (1/2) mukkāṇi iṉṉilam araiye
araimāvum intattaṭi oṉpatiṉāl nilam

ṇiyum iṟṟai nāḷāl viṟṟukkuṭuttukkoḷvatāṉa emmi[l]licainta
vilaipporuḷ aṉṟāṭu naṟkācu

ikkācu āṟāyirattu irunūṟṟerupattu
mūṉṟum āvaṇakkaḷariye kāṭṭeṟṟik kiḻikaiccelavaṟak koṇṭu viṟṟu vilai-

teva[r]kaṉmikaḷukku
icaṇaikkuṟai ūravarom innilam muṉṟe iraṇṭumāvaraik kāṇikkum
ikkācu āṟāyirattu irunūṟṟorupattu mūṉṟum vilaiyāvatāka[vu]m ituve

poruḷmāvaṟi[ti]p poruṭcelavolaiyāvatākavum ituvallatu veṟu poruḷ
māvaṟutip poruṭcelavolaik kāṭṭakkaṭavatallātatākavum innilaṅkaḷil
meṉokkiṉa maraṅ kiḻnokkiṉa kiṇaṟum keṇikaḷum maṉaikaḷum maṉaip-

kaḷum
aka[ppaṭṭa mi]kutikku[ṟaivu]m uṭpaṭa iṉṉilam muṉṟe iraṇṭumā
araikkāṇikkum ivarkaḷ veṇumeṉṟa pote

ṇamāvutāka[vum]
ippaṭi cammatittu vilaikkaṟa viṟṟup poruḷaṟakkoṇṭu viṟṟu vilai

tevarkaṉmikaḷukku icaṇaikkuṟai ūravarom ivarkaḷ colla inta
pu[ṉceyvi]lai

riyāṉ pañcanetivāṇaṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai icaṇaikkuṟai uṭaiyāṉ
uyyavantāṉ tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku ivai
micaṅkiḷināṭṭu veḷāṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai kiḻmāntūruṭaiyāṉ
kūttaṉum arumpā-

kkiḻāṉ maṉṟuḷāṭuvāṉum sayiñai āṉamaikku ivai nāṇaṟkuṟai ara •••tavaṉ
niṉṟa nampi eḻuttu itu kuṉṟattuṭaiyāṉ maṅkalāṇṭāṉ sayiñai [ā]namaikku
itu ālattūrāṉ etirili coḻaccaruppetimaṅ-

ṉ ā[ti]varāhapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikkivai
putuppākkamuṭaiyāṉ nāyakaṉ koṭanāṭṭu viḻupparayaṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku
ippaṭikkum nāṇaṟkuṭaiyāṉ ṉe[ṉ*] tiruvai oḷi-

matiuṭaiyāṉ vaṭukaṉ teṉkaraināṭṭu viḻupparayaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi ivai
puliyūrkiḻavaṉ tiruvāṉaikkāvaluṭaiyāṉum mil[ā*]ṭṭuṭaiyaṉ tiruvaḻivāṭi
uṭaiyāṉum sayiñai āṉamaikku ivai oḷimati uṭaiyāṉ [iravāṭu]-

vāṉ ti[ruveṇ]ṇāvaṟperumāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai attuṭaiyāṉ
kaimmāṭṭāṅkāṉamaikku ivai tirunilapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai
icaṇaikkuṟai uṭaiyāṉ piccaṉ tiruppukalūr uṭaiyāṉāṉa ulakaḷanta-

coḻa tamiḻanāṭṭuveḷāṉum kaṭampaṅkuṭaiyāṉ iṉiyāṉ peṟṟāṉum sayiñai
āṉamaikku ivai ūṟkaṇakku vaṭṭuṭaiyāṉ kaṇakku koṭṭuṭaiyāṉ
poṉampalakkūttaṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai nallūruṭai

yāṉ accaṉ sayi[ñ*]ai āṉamaikku ivai micaṅkiḷināṭṭu veḷāṉ eḻuttu
itu patumāramuṭaiyāṉ tiri[ya]iruntāṉ sayiñai āṉamaikkum ippaṭikkum
immaṇaṟkuṭaiyāṉ teṉṉa tirumutukuṉṟamuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu [ma]tu pa • •

uṭaiyāṉ uṭaiyāṉ tikaḻiyum kāvaṉūruṭaiyāṉ veḷān tā • • ṉāṉa coṟṟum
sayiñai āṉamaikku ivai rājarāja
ṉ ātivarākapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai nāva-

lūr appūti maṟittulakaṅkāttanampi eḻuttu itu periyapiḷḷai sayiñai
āṉamaikku aracurakeḻutavaṉ tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai
maṇaṟkuṭaiyāṉ uṭaiyapiḷḷai eḻuttu ivai amutāṇṭā-

ṉ aṟivu sayiñai āṉamaikku aracūr keḻutavaṉ
tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyā[ṉe]ḻuttu ivai ceṇaikkuṟai māṇikka tevaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 503 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prākarā in the Jambukēśvara temple at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
Stones damaged in several places. Appears to record a sale of land by an individual who had kāṇi
rights in Ichaṇaikkuṟai.

l.ta:
maiyum cevvāykkiḻamai[yum] peṟṟa cotināḷ
teṉ[karai] ••••• pāṇṭikulācaṉi vaḷanāṭṭu uṭai[yār tiru] •••••

caivutiṭṭukkuṭutta paricāvatu icaṇaikkuṟai talaikkuṟaiyil •••••

ṟkellai uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku tevatāṉamāka •••••
kellaivaṭavāṟṟuniroṭukālukku teṟkum āka icain[ta] •••••

vum iṟṟai nāḷāl viṟṟukkuṭuttuk koḷvatāṉa emmilicainta
vilaipporu •••kācu

•••maṉaiyum maṉaippaṭappum •••eppeṟpaṭṭa •••maikaḷum akappaṭa mikutikkuṟai
uḷḷaṭaṅka iṉṉilam eṭṭumāva •••

vilaikkaṟa viṟṟu poruḷaṟakko • • •

kāvuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil āticaṇṭe •••uṭaiyāṉ iṉiyāṉ peṟṟāṉeṉ
ivaṉ colla inta

ṉ ātivarāhapaṭṭaṉeṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi ivai ••••• peṟṟāṉ sayiñai
āṉamaikku ivai ivvūr ūṟkaṇakku vaṭakuṭaiyāṉ •••
aṟiveṉ maṇaṟ •••yāṉ tirumuṅ •••ṇamuṭaiyāṉ meṉāṭṭarayaṉ aṟi • • sayiñai •••

sayiñaikkum •••

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 504 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prākarā in the Jambukēśvara temple at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
Stones damaged in several places. This states that one Oḷimati-uḍayāṉ Teṉkaraināṭṭu-
viḻupparaiyaṉ gave the kiḻippuṇai-tīṭṭu mentioning that he took money from the temple trustees and
handed over the same to various persons towards the price of the lands that they had sold to the temple.

l.ta:
tevaṟku ••••• maiyum peṟṟa
vicākattu nāḷ teṉkarai pāṇṭikulācaṉi ••••••••••

tevarkaṉmikaḷukku iṉṉāṭṭu iṭaiyāṟṟu nāṭ •••ṉ
teṉkaraināṭṭu viḻupparai ••••• vatu rājarāja

ṟṟattuk kiḻpalāṟṟu icaṇaikkuṟai talaikkuṟaiyil ūravar ••••• ṇṭumā
araikkāṇikkum •••

nāṉūṟṟirupattañcum kaḻaṉivāyiluṭaiyāṉ aṉumaṉamuṭaiyāṉum ivaṉ ta[mpi
nam]piyum viṟṟukku •••iraṇṭu māmuntirikaikkum
yāṉ perumāṉ kaṇṇaṉ viṟṟa nilam oṉṟemākāṇi • • •

ṇattil meṟpaṭṭa kācu patiṉorāyiratteḻunnūṟṟi
ṟupattoṉpatum inta āticaṇṭe

ṟpaṭṭaperkaḷukku vāṅki kuṭuttu kiḻippuṇaittiṭṭuk kuṭutteṉ uṭaiyār
tiruvā[ṉaikkāvuṭaiya] nāyaṉār koyil āticaṇṭe

ṭu viḻupparaiyaṉeṉ ippaṭikku ivai oḷimatiuṭaiyāṉ teṉkaraināṭṭu
viḻupparaiyaṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai pūṇṭiuṭaiyāṉ periyāṉ •••••
pañcanativāṇa[ṉeḻuttu] •••••

ṇṭa nāṭṭuviḻuppa[r]ai[ya]ṉ sayiñaikkum ippaṭi aṟivukkum ivai
kāḷaṅkuṭaiyāṉ micaṅkiḷināṭṭu veḷāṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku iṉiyāṉ peṟṟāṉ
sayiñai āṉamaikku •••••

itu kaḻaṉivāyiluṭaiyāṉ kaimmāṭṭāṅkāṉamaikku ivai

kkuṭaiyāṉ ulakaḷantacoḻa maḻanāṭṭu veḷāṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku ivai
māṟampāṭi uṭaiyāṉ veṭṭumpaḷa •••••

kiḻavaṉ ••••••••••

No. 289.
(A.R. No. 505 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prākarā in the Jambukēśvara temple, Tiruvānaikāval, Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
Stones damaged in several places. Appears to record a sale of land to the temple by an individual
who had kāṇi rights in Ichaṇaikkuṟai.

maṇam paṇṇik kuṭukkak

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the east wall of the third prākarā in the Jambukēśvara temple at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p: Sale of dry land, measuring 1 vēli and odd for 2125 kāsu, by a kāṇi holder of Ichaṇaikkuṟai.

l.ta:
maiyum cevvāyk kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa coti
nāḷ pāṇṭikulācaṉi vaḷanāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya

vaḷa
nāṭṭu pācciṟ kūṟṟattu kiḻ[pa]lāṟṟu icaṇaikkuṟaikkāṇiuṭaiya
pālaiyuṭaiyāṉ perumāṉ kaṇṇaṉeṉ puṉcey nilavilai

ṇa icaivutiṭṭuk kuṭutta paricāvatu icaṇaikuṟait talaikkuṟaiyil nāṉ viṟkiṉṟa
nilattukku icainta kiḻpāṟkellai piṭā[ri] melai oḻukkaiyil melaivelikku
meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai ciṟṟāṟṟu

nīroṭukālukku vaṭakkum mīpāṟkellai āḻvār cāttaṅkuṭaiyār arintavaṉ
viḻupparayar nilattukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai vaṭavāṟṟu niroṭu
kālukkutteṟkum āka iṉṉāṉkellaiyuḷ naṭuvup

iṉṉilam oṉṟemākkāṇiyum iṟṟaināḷāl
viṟṟukkuṭuttukkoḷvatāṉa emmilicainta vilaipporuḷ aṉṟāṭu naṟkācu
m āvaṇakkaḷariye kāṭṭeṟṟik kaiccelavaṟakkoṇṭu viṟṟu vilai

ṉilam oṉṟe mākāṇikkum ikkācu iraṇṭāyirattoru nūṟṟirupatteñcume
vilaiyāvutākavum ituve poruḷmāvaṟitip poruṭcelavolaiyāvutākavum
ituvallatu

veṟu poruḷmāvaṟutip poruṭcelavolaikkāṭṭakkaṭavatallāttākavum
iṉṉilattu meṉokkiṉa maraṅkaḷum kiḻnokkiṉa kiṇaṟukaḷum keṇikaḷum
maṉaiyum maṉaippaṭap(pa)paiyum potuvum

potātiyum maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa urimaikaḷum akappa(pā)ṭa mikuti kuṟaivu
uḷḷaṭaṅka nilam oṉṟe mākāṇikkum ivarkaḷ veṉummeṉṟa pote

ṇamāvutākavum ippaṭi cammatittu vilai
vilaikkuṟaviṟṟup poruḷaṟakkoṇṭuviṟṟu vilai

kaṉmikaḷukku pālaiyūṭaiyāṉ perumāṉ
kaṇṇaṉeṉ ippaṭikku ivai pālaiyuruṭaiyāṉ kaṇṇaṉ sayiñai āṉamaikku
ivvūr ūṟkaṇakku va-

ṭakuṭaiyāṉ kaṇakku koṭṭuruṭaiyāṉ kūṟṟutaittāṉāṉa
poṉṉampalakkūttaṉeḻuttu itu vaṇṭaluṭaiyāṉ aḻakāṇṭāṉ aṟivu
sayiñaikkum ippaṭi aṟivukkum ivai [mi]ṅkiḷināṭṭu ve

ḷāṉeṉ ippaṭi aṟiveṉ •••[vi]ḻupparaiyaṉeṉ itu p[eriya]ṉāṭṭu paramaṉāṉa
pūvaṉamuṭaiyāṉ aṟivu sayiñaikkum ippaṭi aṟivukkum [kura]ṇṭiyuṭaiyāṉ •••

periyāṉ •••ñcaṉe •••aṟiveṉ ivai arayaṉ mūvāyirattaccaṉeḻuttu itu
kaḻaṉivāyiluṭaiyāṉ aṟivu sayiñaikku ivai micaṅkiḷi nāṭṭu
veḷāṉeḻuttu ippaṭi

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. 507 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the kitchen in the Jambukēśvara temple, At Tiruvānaikāval at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
Incomplete Sale of land, lying in several plots, to the temple of Tiruvāṉaikkā, by one
Kāṭṭuḍaiyān Piḷḷāṉ Chēndāṉ, who had kāṇi rights in Vaḷavanallūr a dēvadāna of the same temple and
who was also a lease-holder (aḍaippu) of the whole village. The sale was made to pay to Pōyadeṇḍa-
nāyakkar (perhaps the tax collector of the day) the tax arrears that had accumulated over someyears on
this land. It is mentioned that this land was in his ownership before he had sold it to one Edirmalai-
uḍaiyān Vaḍavāyilnambi alias Maṉṟachchi-pallavaraiyaṉ. The latter person is said to have absconded
without paying the tax dues on the sold land, hence the present sale.

l.ta:
caṉi vaḷanāṭṭu viḷānāṭṭu uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiyanāyaṉār koyil
āticaṇṭe

tevar tiruvaruḷāl ivar kaṉ[mika]ḷukku nilavilai[yāvaṇa
i]cai[vu] tiṭṭu iṉnāṭṭu iṉnā-

[ya]ṉār tevatānam vaḷavanallūrk kāṇi uṭaiyeṉumāy ivvūrkku
aṭaippumāṉa kāṭṭuṭaiyā[ṉ*] [pi]ḷḷā[ṉ*] centāṉeṉ ivvūril eṉ
kāṇiyāṉa nilattu nāṉum eṅkaḷ periyatavappaṉār

kāṭṭuuṭaiyār āḷuṭaiyāṉum kāṇiyāṉa nilattu etirmalaiuṭaiyāṉ
vaṭavāyi[l*] nampiyāṉamaṉṟac cippallavaraiyaṉukku muṉṉāḷil nāṅkaḷ viṟṟa
viḷai nilattukkum puṉcey nilattukku[m*] maṉaika-

ḷukkum ivaṉ muṉṉiṉṟu kaṭamai kuṭimai yiṟāṉamaiyālum ivviḷainilam
puṉceyyumaṉaikaḷum uṭ[pa]ṭa eṉ[ṉa]ṭaippile certtu nāṉ iṟuppittu
vantam[aiyāl i]vūril ivvāṇṭu pacāṉakkaṭamai-

kku celavāka nellukkuṟuṇikku kācu āka vilaippaṭikkup puṉceyk kaṭamaikkum
celavākap poyateṇṭanāyakkarkku teṉkaraināṭṭu mākkalam uḷḷiṭṭa
kaṭamaikku ce • • •

uṭpaṭap piṟitta kācu aimpatiṉāyirattukku[m]e viṉiyokamum policai(t)
tuṇṭamum kācu taṇṭaniṉṟa kaṉṉaṭiyar uḷḷiṭṭārkkup pa[ḻaṅ]kācum eṟṟi
mārattammaṉum [vi]ca[y]aiyayaṉum • • •

[o]ṭukkukiṟa kācukku iṉta etirimalaiuṭaiyāṉ vaṭavāyil nampi
maṉṟaccappallavaraiyaṉ perāl kaṭamaiyāṉa puṉceyk kaṭamai

ikkācu patiṉāyirattu nāṉūṟṟu irupattu muṉṟum muṉṉiṉṟu oṭukkāmaiyil
ivviḷai nilattukkum puṉcey nilattukkum maṉai-

mi nāṉeṉṟum eṉ aṭaippi[l]e certtu ivaṉ kaṭamai
kuṭimai iṟāte uṭalaḻittup pokaiyil ikkācu eṉakkupparamāy iṟukka
veṇṭukaiyil inta malaiuṭai-

yāṉ vaṭavāyil nampiyāṉa maṉṟaccappallavaraiyaṉ kāṇiyāṉa tukkai[cuvā]mi
eṉṟu perkūvapaṭṭa nilattukku kiḻpāṟkellai pāṇṭiyarāyar
kāṇiyāṉanarikkuṭaiyāṉ paḷḷikku m[e]ṟkum teṉpā-

ṟkellai tiruveṇkāṭuṭaiyāṉ vāykkālukkum melpāṟkellai maḻavatarayar
kāṇi veppañceykku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai kaṅkaikoṇṭāṉ
vāykkālukku ••••••••••

iṉṉilam nāṉmāvaraiyum
īḻakkuṭaiyāṉ paḷḷa •••••••••• pāṇṭiyarāyar ••••• kkuṭaiyāṉ paḷḷattukku
kiḻakkum teṉpāṟke-

llai [tiruveṇkāṭuṭai]yāṉ vāykkālukku vaṭakku mipāṟkellaiviṟki[ṉṟa]

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. 508 of 1905.)
Jambukēśvaram, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the kitchen in the Jambukēśvara temple, at Tiruvānaikāval.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Sale of land, to the temple of Tiruvāṉaikkā, by Kāṭṭūruḍaiyāṉ Piḷḷāṉ Chēndāṉ
(mentioned in the above inscription) and his younger brother, who had kāṇi rights in Vaḷavanallūr, a
dēvadāna of the same temple.

yum caṉikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa coti nāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 509 of 1905.)
Kannanūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Bhōjēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Gift of money by Pōlucheṭṭi, a kavaṟai-cheṭṭi of Kāñchipuram, for building certain parts of the
temple.

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. 510 of 1905.)
Kannanūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Bhōjēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Mutilated. Records that the king granted the village of Vaṅgāram in Pāchchil-kuṟuvaṭṭam as tax-
free dēvadāna to the temple of Pōsalīsuramuḍaiya-Nāyanār at Kaṇṇanūr for celebrating a festival called
Raṇamukharāman-[Sandhi] instituted in his name.

l.ta:

po[yi]calicuramuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil tāṉattarkkut taṅkaḷ nāyaṉārkku

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. 511 of 1905.)
Kannanūr, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the right of entrance of the gōpurā in the Bhōjēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This is a general order of the king remitting the taxes chōḍi (jōḍi), sūlavari, arasupēṟu, etc. on
the dēvadāna villages of all Śiva and Vishṇu temples (mentioned by their names) in Chōḻa-maṇḍalam for
worship and renovation of those temples. After his expedition against the Gajapati king, he is said to
have made the grant which amounted to a total sum of 10,000 pon.
