inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 512 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in Siva (Jambunātha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
Sale of land by the ūrār of Paḍuvūr in Āṉmūr-kūṟṟam in Rājāśraya-vaḷanāḍu on the north bank to
a temple called Gangaikoṇḍa-chōḻa-īśvaramuḍaiyār in a neighboring brahmadēya village (name lost). The
ūrār undertook to pay themselves the taxes on the sold land.

l.ta: ṉṉavar vaitta cuntaramuṭiyu mintiranāra[mu]n teṉṟirai īḻamaṇṭalamuḻuva-

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 513 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in Siva (Jambunātha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
Register an endowment of 140 kaḻañju of gold by Kāchchuvan Kēchuvaṉ of Tiruveḷḷaṟai, for
feeding two Brāhmaṇas versed in the Vēdas. The temple is called Tiruvanaikkallil-Bhaṭārakar at
Tiruveḷḷaṟai. The mūlapariḍai of the village undertook to supervise the endowment.

l.ta: kāccuvaṉ kecuvaṉ turkkaṉeṉ ivūrk kallāl kaḻañcu perttatu

palicai [kāl] kāṇattāl muṉṉāḻi paḻavariciyu muḻakkup paruppum kāykkaṟiyum
puḷiṅkaṟiyum poṟikkaṟiyum iviraṇṭu vāḻai-
ppaḻamum tayir irunāḻiyum neyyāḻākkumāka ippaṭi yūṭṭuvomāṉo

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 514 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in Siva (Jambunātha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarmaṉ, Year 2 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
The inscription records an agreement given by mulaparuḍai of Thiruveḷḷaṟai to burn a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tiruvānaikkallil-Bhaṭṭārakar with the interest on 25 kaḻañju of gold endowed by
Marudan Āchchan of the sankarappāḍi in Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
[Published in SII, vol. XIII, No. 4.]

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 515 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunātha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
The King's name is engraved over an erasure. Gift of gold (30 pon) for offerings atnoon and for
a lamp in the temple of Tiruvānaikal Perumānaḍigaḷ at Tiruveḷḷaṟai. The donor was Kuvāvaṉ Chēndi wife
of Chāttuvāy Chēndan Mādēvaṉ, who belonged to the same village. She gave another 40 kaḻañju of pon
for the village tank.

l.ta: n pa[t]tu eṟṟi eḻupatiṉ kaḻañcu poṉ [ko]-

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 516 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunātha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
Gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for a lamp to the temple by Māyilaṭṭi alias Nakkaṉ Mallaṉ of
Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 517 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunātha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p: Gift of gold for a lamp by a cloth merchant (aṟugaivāṇigaṉ) of Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 518 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunātha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of 70 kaḻañju of gold left in charge of the mūlapaṟaḍai of
Thiruveḷḷaṟai by Chāttuvāy Kāñjan Dāmōdiran of the same village, for feeding a Brahmana versed in the
Vedas, every noon after offering is made in the temple of Tiruvānaikkal Perumānaḍigaḷ.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII, No. 27.]

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 519 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.b: Chōḻa Rājakēsarivarmaṉ, Year 3 = c.9th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This records another endowment of money made by a member of the maṇigrāmam of Uṟaiyūr
for feeding a Vedic Brāhmaṇa.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII No. 28.]

l.ta: y oru piṭiyum kāykaṟiyum puḷiṅkaṟiyum po • • •
kaṟiyum tayir nāḻi aṭaikkāy nālum i • • •

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. 520 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. The inscription stops with the name of the temple. Another inscription belong to
Vikrama Chōḻa starts in the same line.

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. 521 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
Gift of land after purchase for a flower-garden to the temple of Tiruvā[nai]kkal-Mahādēvar by a
Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyān Pāṇḍināḍu-koṇḍān of Perumbaṟṟappuliyūr.

l.ta: vaṉamāka koṇṭu viṭṭa kollait toṭṭam kāṭaṅkālip pe-
ruvaḻikku meṟku nānkellaikkuṭppaṭṭa taṭi munṟum itu

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 522 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of 30 cows for the maintenance of a perpetual lamp in the temple by
Sembiyan Maḻanāṭṭu-vēḷāṉ alias Koṟṟaṉ Māṟan of Kākaikaḷūr in Kilāl-kuṟuviḍam. This gift was left in
charge of the mulapariḍai. The high regnal, Year of king and the early script of the inscription make it
attribute to Aditya I.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII No. 313.]

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. 523 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p: Gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for a lamp by local individual called Chāttuvāy Kāḍaṉ Kāri.

l.ta: taṟkku vaitta poṉ muppatiṉ kaḻañcu can-

edition.l: tirātittavarai mulapariṭaiyār rikṣai Read rakṣai[||*]

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. 524 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarmaṉ, Year 9 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This record an endowment of 25 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the mūlaparaḍai of Tiruveḷḷaṟai
for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruvāṉaikkal Perumānaḍigaḷ by Kumaran Kōn of Iḍaikkāṭṭūr in
Kīraṉūr-nāḍu in Pāṇḍi-nāḍu, who was an adigāram officer under the Chōḻa king.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIX No. 218.]

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. 525 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p: Gift of gold for a lamp by a native of Nāṅgūr, a dēvadāna in Nāṅgūr-nāḍu.

l.ta: kaḷukku nāṅkūr nāṭṭu tevatānam nāṅkūr kāc-

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. 526 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p: Gift of 70 kaḻañju of gold for a lamp by Kaṇattaṉ Tiruttēyam, the madhyastha of Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. 527 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p: Built in at the rightend. Gift of sheep for a lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. 528 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarmaṉ, Year 8 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
It records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruvāṉaikkal
Perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Tiruveḷḷaṟai by Araiyan Tāḻi of the same village.
Published in SII vol. XIX, No. 195-A.

l.ta: ku iravum pakalum erippatāka vaitta poṉ mu-

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. 529 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
In verse form. Built in and portions missing. The king is called Danti Nantivarman, meaning
Nantivarman, son of Danti. He is also called Pallavamahārādhirāja. Refers to a Veḷḷāḷa and Peruṅgāviti.
Seems to be some gift to Nandimaṅgai (Nandimaṅgalam) by a Māṟpiḍugu-Iḷaṅgōvēḷār's son-in-law
Viḻuppēraraiyaṉ Chāttaṉ, lord of Chelli.
[Published in SII vol. XII No. 48 and AR. No. 88 of 1910.]

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] bhāra[dv][ ājakulati] *laka branmākṣatra kulōdbhava = pa-
veḷāḷaraiyar ta[ṅ] ••••• lainirava[ yaṉannanti] maṅkai

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. 530 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the rock in front of the Siva (Jambunatha) temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p: Built in at the beginning. Records a gift of gold.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. 531 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On a pillar left of the entrance of the rock-cut cave in Pundarikasha-Perumāḷ temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rājarāja I, Year lost = 985-1012 A.D.

inscription.p:
Much damaged. Words and phrases belonging to the historical introduction of Rājarāja-I can be
traced here and there.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. 532 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On a pillar in the rock-cut cave, Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Rājakēsarivarman, Year 2 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p: Fragmentary. It stops with the mention of Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII No. 5.]

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. 533 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On a pillar in the rock-cut cave, Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p: Damaged. Seems to register allotments for a festival and daily requirements of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. 534 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the second pillar in the rock-cut cave in Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarmaṉ (Uttama-chōḻa ?)., Year 8 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
It registers a gift of 20 kaḻañju of gold made by Irāyiravan Dēvi Ammanār the wife of ‘the lord
who died on elephant-back’ i.e. Prince Rājāditya, for providing for special offerings with 4 nāḻi of rice on
the days of two uvā (New Moon and Full Moon days), Sankrānti and Ashṭami, to god Śri Krishna and
goddess Śri-Rukmini in the big temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai, and another gift of gold for a lamp. This is said to
be the earliest reference to the worship of Krishna and Rukmini found so for in South Indian Inscriptions.
[Published in SII Vol. III No. 132 and noted in SII Vol. XIX No. 196.]

l.ta:
[y]āl amu[tu*]paṭi[ya]mutu ceyvikka vaitta poṉ tiruveḷḷaṟaik kallā[l*]
mutala-
•••rupatiṉ kaḻañcum viḷakkoṉṟukku vaitta poṉ tiruveḷḷaṟaik kallāl

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. 535 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On a pillar left entrance of the rock-cut cave in Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarmaṉ, Year 30 = c.10th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This is damaged. It records some provision made for special offerings to god in the ‘big temple’
at Tiruve[ḷḷaṟai] on the day of Tiruvōṇam asterism in Chitirai (lunar) month.
[Published in SII Vol. XIX No. 364.]

edition.l: ṟku yāṇṭa[ ṭu 15]- āvatu tiruve[ḷḷa]-

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(A.R. No. 536 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the third pillar in the rock-cut cave in Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple.
inscription.p: Damaged. Mentions queen of the Vēṇāḍu chief Śrī Vallavan Kōtai.

edition.l: [panma] ṟku yāṇṭu [5] āvatu veṇā[ṭ]-

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(A.R. No. 537 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the third pillar in the rock-cut cave in Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p: Fragment. Only the name [Vi]sayanalluḻān can be read.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. 538 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the fourth pillar in the rock-cut cave in Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Gift of 7 kaḻañju of gold entrusted with the mūlaparuḍai of the village for feeding a
brahmaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(A.R. No. 539 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the fourth pillar in the rock-cut cave in Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple at Tiruveḷḷarai.
inscription.p:
Beginning mutilated. Seems to make provision for offerings for a festival on the dvādasi days in
the month of Mārkaḻi. The donor was a local brāhmaṇa called Pāratāyaṉ Duvēdi Nārāyaṇaṉ Gōvindan.

edition.l: ṉ prahmadēyam rājakesa • • • (īncomplete•)

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(A.R. No. 540 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the fourth pillar in the rock-cut cave in Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple.
inscription.p:
Beginning damaged. Mentions the installation of Rukmiṇi-pirāṭṭiyār as the consort of Śrī-
Krishṇa in the big temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai and the provision made for offerings to the deity.

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. 541 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the margin of the well called Nālumulaikkēni at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
Records the construction of the well called Māṟpiḍugu perungiṇaṟu by one Kampan-Araiyan the
younger brother of a certain Visaiyanalluḻān of Ālambākkam. The work was commenced in the 4th, Year
of Dantivarman and completed in his 5th, Year. The tank was put under the protection of the ‘group of
3700’ of the village. The Tamil verse exhorts all to do good things in this life, remembering that life is
short and uncertain.
[Published in Epigraphia Indica Vol. XI No. 15.]

l.ta:
ṟku yāṇṭu nāṉkāvateṭuttukkoṇṭu ai[n]tāvatu muṟṟuvittāṉ

tampi kampaṉ araiyaṉ tiruveḷḷaṟaitteṉṉūrp peruṅkiṇaṟu itaṉ piyar

ulakam maṟiyavaimiṉey

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. 542 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the margin of the well called Nālumulaikkēni at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.
inscription.p:
Records that a merchant (vāṇigan) repaired the well (‘the big well built by a merchant’) which
had suffered considerable damage from floods and other causes. The well was renamed as Danma-
tāvaḷan.

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. 543 of 1905.)
Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District.
On the south wall of the ruined temple in Tiruvellaṟai.
inscription.p:
Fragment. Seems to register a sale of tirunāmattukkāṇi land belonging to the temple by the
temple trustees to Paḻuvūr-kiḻavan Nakkan Aravāṇḍār alias Maḻavarāyan, as the land was lying
uncultivated and as the temple offerings and rituals suffered due to shortage of income.

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. 544 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south base of the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b:
Rajakēsarivarman Vīra-Pāṇḍya, Śaka 1202, Year 1[5] Tulā, apara 10, Monday, and
Uttiram(irregular) = 1279 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Gift of money for a lamp. Mentions Vīra-Chōḻa-chaturvēdimagalam. The temple is
called Tirumēṟkōyil Chttiramēḻi Viṇṇagar Nāyanār Karumāṇikkāḻvār at Vichaiyamaṅgalam in Kuṟuppu-
nāḍu. The sabhā of Kaṟṟāyṉkaṇṇi alias Vīrachōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam received an endowment of 200
paṇam given by a Gōvindan Karumāṇikkāḻvān of Tirukkuḍandai for the lamp service.

l.ta:
tikaḷ śrīvirapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉaiñcāvatu tulānāyaṟṟu

miyum peṟṟa uttirattu nāḷ kuṟuppunāṭṭu
vicai-

yamaṅkalattut tirumeṟkoyil nāyaṉār cittirameḻiviṇṇakar nāyaṉār
karumāṇikkāḻvār koyil nampimāṟkum

sabhaiyom upaiya sātaṉam paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu tirukkuṭantaik

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(A.R. No. 545 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south base of the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p: Damaged. Gift of a flower-garden and a lamp by the ūrār and ūrāḷikaḷ of Kūḍūr.

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(A.R. No. 545A of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south base of the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: In characters of c. 16th century A.D.

inscription.p: Engraved below the above inscription. Refers to a flower garden.

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. 546 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north base of the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of money (29 achchu) by Chiṟiyān Dēvan alias Muḍikoṇḍachōḻa-mārāyan, a
Veḷḷāḷa of Chāttandai clan of the village for offerings in the temple of Tirumēṟkōyil Chittiramēḻi-
viṇṇagarāḻvār in Vijayamaṅgalam in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu. The gift is placed under the protection of the
‘ninety-nine’ belonging to the left hand (iḍangai).

l.ta:
lliyāṇṭu pattāvatu aippicait tiṅkaḷil kuṟuppuṉāṭṭu vicaiyamaṅkalattut
tirumeṟkoyil cittirameḻi viṇṇakarāḻvāṟkut tiruppaṭimāṟṟukku

cāttantaikaḷil ciṟiyāṉ tevaṉāṉa

l.note: Here some letters are scraped out.

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 547 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north base of the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Kongu, Tribhuvanachakravartin Vira-Pāṇḍya, Year 12 = c.13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a money gift for a lamp in the temple by a Veḷḷāḷa of Veṉtakaḷi clan of Kuṟuchchi in
Vaḍakarai-nāḍu.

l.ta: ṇitiṅkaḷ mutalāka vaṭakaraināṭu kuṟucci i veḷḷāḷaṉ veṉtakaḷil ve-

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(A.R. No. 548 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ temple at
Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Kongu, Jatavarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya, Year 23 = c. 13th century A.D.

inscription.p: Built in at the end. Gift of a lamp by a Veḷḷāḷa of Paḍaittalai clan.

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. 549 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ temple at
Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Kongu, Jaṭāvarman Sundara-pāṇḍya, Year 28 = c. 13th century A.D.

inscription.p: Built in at the end. Gift of a lamp.

l.ta: veṅkaṭamuṭaiyāṉum yeṉ tampi ciṅkarum ivaccoṉṟukku paṇam
ttar kuṭaṅkoṭu koyil pukuvārelām celuttuvatāka itu śrī •••••

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. 550 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the east wall left of entrance of the maṇḍapa in front of the the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka
Perumāḷ temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Kongu, Jatavarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya, Year 17 = c.13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Built in at the beginning. Records the gift of money for a sandhi lamp by a Sūriyadēvan
Kuvalayattaraiyan from Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: ••••• pperumāḷeṉ kaikkoṇṭa accu oṉṟum koṇṭeṉ ivviḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. 551 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the east wall left of entrance of the maṇḍapa in front of the the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka
Perumāḷ temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Kongu, Jatavarman Sundara-pāṇḍya, Year 24 = c.13th century A.D.

inscription.p: Built in at the beginning. Damaged, Gift for a lamp by a kaikkōḷa individual.

edition.l: ••••• koccaṭapaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉa [ ccakravat] tikaḷ śrīcuntarapāṇṭi-

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. 552 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south base of the maṇḍapa in front of the the central shrine the Kariyamāṇikka Perumāḷ temple
at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Gift of land to the temple of Chittiramēḻi-viṇṇagar at Vijayamaṅgalam by the nāṭṭāvar of
Kuṟuppu-nāḍu for the prosperity of the king and of the country.

l.ta:

right of worship (kōyil-kāṇi) to two śivabrāhamaṇas of Rājarājapuram in the local temples.

is, two woman of Veḷḷāḷan Paḍaittalaikaḷ clan of Vijayamaṅgalam and a merchant of Kāngayam in

inscription.p:

Pāṇḍyappērēri and granted all lands irrigated by it free of taxes and asked the ūrār to make provision for
offerings to the goddess.

inscription.p:

celebrating the tirukkalliyāṇam ritual of the goddess by a woman of VeḷḷāḷanChāttuvāyikaḷ for offerings
and for two lamps. The gift was entrusted with the ūrār of Vijayamaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple in
Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Refers to the renovation works of the maṇḍapa and registers the reengraving of the contents of
old inscriptions found on the walls of old structure. This new inscription refers to old gifts made for
lamps by different persons during the reigns of Kulōttuṅga-chōḻa and Vīrarājēndradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(A.R. No. 579 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple in
Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of two ahchu by a sāmanta of the king (Perumāḷ) named K[ul]ōttuṅga Chōḻa
Vikramādittadēvan.

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(A.R. No. 580 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple in
Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p: Gift of an achchu for a lamp by a member of VeḷḷāḷanPaṉaingāḍakaḷ clan of Kūḍūr.

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(A.R. No. 581 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple in
Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p: Gift of an achchu for lamp by a Veḷḷāḷa woman called Poduvan Pāvai.

l.ta: tiṅkaḷ [mu]tal kuṟuppu nāṭṭu vicaiya maṅkalattu veḷḷā-

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(A.R. No. 582 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the west wall right of the entrance of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple in Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Koṅgu, Parakēśari Kōṉērmaikoṇḍāṉ, Year 3 = c. 12th Century.

inscription.p:
End portion not available. Registers a royal order addressed to the śivabrāhamaṇas and temple
managers of the temples in number of nāḍus and towns. The purport is not known as the end portion is
not available. The places included Rājarājapuram, an aḍikkīḻttaḷam in Naraiyanūr-nāḍu,
Tiruppāṇḍikkoḍumuḍi, and some eighteen nāḍus including Pongalūrkkā-nāḍu, Teṉkarai-nāḍu, Aṇḍa-
nāḍu, Talaiyūr-nāḍu, Vaḍakarai-Maṇali-nāḍu, Kiḻanga-nāḍu, Vengala-nāḍu, etc.

l.ta: ṅkil naraiyaṉū[r] ṉāṭṭu aṭikkiḻttaḷamāna rājarājapuramum poṅkalūkkā ṉā-
ṭum teṉkarai nāṭumaṇṭa nāṭum talaiyūrṉāṭum vaṭakarai maṇali ṉāṭum • • •

[ya]ṉāṭum viracoḻavaḷaṉāṭum valuppukkāṉāṭum kuṟuppunāṭum vaṭaparicāra nāṭum
kāñci-
kkūvaṉāṭum pūnturaiṉāṭum kiḻpparai nāṭum vaṭakarai ṉāṭum iṉṉāṭukaḷil

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(A.R. No. 583 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the west wall right of the entrance of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the
Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple in Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p: Gift of an achchu for a lamp by a member of Veḷḷāḷar Kāḍakaḷ clan of Iḷammaḻai.

edition.l: ātrayagōtratuāḷavanti piḻaipoṟuttāṉum mā[ṭa]la [ gōtrat] tu • • •

inscription.head:
No. 369.
(A.R. No. 584 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the west wall left of the entrance of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the
Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple in Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Registers an endowment of 3 kaḻañju by a member of Veḷḷāḷa Chāttupāḍikaḷ clan of
Vijayamaṅgalam for offerings and for 90 lamps on the day of Śivarātri.

l.ta: kuṟuppu nāṭṭu vicaiyamaṅkalattu veḷḷāḷa cāttupāṭikaḷil uṭaiyāṉ tevaṉā-

veṇṭuvatukkuk kuṭutta poruḷ mukkaḻañcukkum amutupaṭi nālu cantikkum
tūṇippa-

takkum tiruviḷakkut toṇṇūṟum itukku veṇṭum viñcanātikaḷ • • •
aṭṭatāka

inscription.head:
No. 370.
(A.R. No. 585 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north wall of the goddess temple in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple in Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Registers an order in the name of the deity (of Vijayamaṅgalam ?) addressed to the śivabrāhmaṇa
priests of all temples in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu asking them to give one fourth share of their income to the
musicians (ukachchar) and enjoy the rest themselves, as per the custom prevailing in Vaḍa-parichāra-
nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(A.R. No. 586 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north wall of the goddess temple in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Gift of an achchu, made by a member of Veḷḷāḷan Paḍaittalaikaḷ clan, for lamp to be lit before the
goddess Tirukkāmakkōṭṭattāḷuḍai-Nāchchiyar.

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(A.R. No. 587 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the goddess temple in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p: Gift of one achchu for a lamp by a temple girl of Vijaiyamangalam.

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(A.R. No. 588 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the goddess temple in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Gift of the village of Āmūr alias Rāhuttarāyanallūr in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu with all its taxes by the
nāṭṭavar of that nāḍu as dēvadāna to the Tirunāgīśvaramuḍaiyār temple in Vijaiyamaṅgalam. The taxes
given are mentioned as kaḍamai, elavai, oṭṭachchu-kāl, kalam, uppāyam, teṇḍavari, taṇṇīrkāṇam,
vēṇḍukōḷ, viniyōgam, taṟiyiṟai, taṭṭārappāṭṭam, and īḻampuñjey.

ttu paṅkuṉi mātaṅ

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(A.R. No. 589 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the goddess temple in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple at
Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Koṅgu, Sundara-Pāṇḍya, Year 27 = c. 14th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of money (140 paṇam) for a perpetual lamp by a member of VeḷḷāḷanVeṇmaṇikaḷ clan of
Vijaiyamangam. The goddess is called Tirukkamakkottatt-Aludaiya-Nachchiyar.

l.ta:
pāṇṭiya tevaṟku tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellā niṉṟa
tirunalliyāṇṭu irupatteḻāvatu taittiṅkaḷ mutalāka kuṟuppuṉāṭṭu
vicaiyamaṅkalattu āḷuṭaiyār tirunā

muṭaiyār tirukkāmakkoṭṭattu

ca

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(A.R. No. 590 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the goddess temple in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple at
Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Registers an agreement of the ūr and ūrāḷikaḷ of Vijaiyamaṅgalam to adopt heaped measures
(perhaps when they measure out their grain dues to the temple).

l.ta: niṟaittu aḷakkak kaṭavom ākavum inta niṟaittaḷavu makkaḷ makkaḷ

edition.p: Another piece along with this inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(A.R. No. 591 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the goddess temple in the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple at
Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Kongu Sundara Pāṇḍya, Year 27 = c. 14th century.

inscription.p:
Gift of money for a lamp by the ūrār of the village. The money was obtained by selling the paddy
belonging to the shrine of the goddess.

l.ta:
pāṇ[ṭiya t]evaṟku tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellāni[ṉṟa tiru]
nalliyāṇṭu irupatteḻāvatu taittiṅkaḷ mutal kuṟuppuṉāṭṭu vicai[yaṉ]

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(A.R. No. 592 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the stone built into the western wall of the first prakāra of the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple at
Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Koṅgu, Chōḻa Rājādhirāja, Year 14+1, Śaka 1022 = 1090 A.D.

inscription.p: Damaged much. Seems to register a gift of land by the king as ēkabhōga-muṟṟūṭṭu.

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(A.R. No. 593 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the slab set up near the first prakāra of the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Koṅgu, Rājādhirāja Vira-Chōḻadeva, Year 5, Śaka 1044 = 1122 A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of land. Mentions a woman member of Veḷḷāḷan Veḷḷaikaḷ clan of Parañjerpaḷḷi in Kāngaya-
nāḍu gifted some land to the temple of Chināpuramuḍaiyār in Vijaiyamaṅgalam for conducting festivals.
The land was situated near the temple of Nangai Nīli at Tennūr, a part of Vijayayamaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(A.R. No. 594 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On a stone built into the west wall of the Sōmēśvara temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. Refers to two temple functionaries called Vīrarājēndira-kālān and
Irājakembīra-piḍāran.

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(A.R. No. 595 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north wall of the Sōmēśvara temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Mutilated and stones out of order. Records the gift of one achchu for a lamp to the temple of
Attāṇīśvaramuḍaiyār by a member of Veḷḷāḷan Padaittalaikaḷ clan. [This reading is from two different
slabs in between which are built two more slabs containing fragmentary inscriptions.]

l.ta: mārkaḻit tiṅkaḷ mutalākak kuṟuppu nāṭṭu vicaiyamaṅkalattu ve[ḷ]-
oṭukkiṉa accu śrīyakki paḻañcalākai yaccoṉṟuṅ kaikkoṇṭomik[ko]-

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(A.R. No. 596 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the east wall of the central shrine in the Chandranātha (Jaina) temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b:
Vijayanagara Vīra-Harihararāya-Uḍaiyar, Śaka 1334 [in Kaṭapayādi system], Nandana, Aippasi
25 = 1412, October 23.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Gift of the village of Cheṅgaṟpaḷḷi in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu to the temple of Aṇiyādaḻagiyār
at Vijaiyamangalam in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu for services and renovation. The gift was made jointly by the
nāṭṭavar of Vaḍa-parichār-nāḍu, Vengarai-nāḍu, Nālūr-paṟṟu, Pērūr-nāḍu, Kuṟuppu-nāḍu, and
Vīrachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu included in Kōṭṭai (kōṭṭaichūḻnda).

perur nāṭu kuṟuppu nāṭum viracoḻavaḷanāṭum āka inta nāṭṭu nāṭṭavarom
kuṟuppu nāṭṭu vicaiya-

maṅkalattu nāyinār aṇiyā[ta]ḻakiyārkkuc cātanam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu

āṟun[āṭṭu] [nā]ṭṭavarom nāyi-

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(A.R. No. 597 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the east face of a pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the Chandranātha (Jaina) temple at
Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p: Says that the stone is the niśīdika of Puḷi Appai, younger sister of Chāmuṇḍarājan.
[Published in Epigraphica Indica Vol. VII p. 108.]

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(A.R. No. 598 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north and west faces of the same pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the Chandranātha (Jaina) temple
at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: Koṅgu, Kulōttuṅga Chōḻa, Year 13+1, Śaka 1085 = 1163 A.D.

inscription.p:
The king made a gift of land for offerings to the Vīrasangātap-perumbaḷḷi at Vijayamaṅgalam The
land with a seed capacity of 3 kalam was in some village (name lost) in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu. The king ordered
his officials (nan-tamar) not to disturb the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(A.R. No. 599 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the door-post of the castern entrance into the maṇḍapa, right side, in front of the Chandranātha
(Jaina) temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.
inscription.p:
Begining of the inscription and lines are built in. Registers an endowment of 2 achchu for the
requirements of certain festivals.

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(A.R. No. 600 of 1905.)
Vijayamaṅgalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the door-post of the castern entrance into the maṇḍapa, left side, in front of the Chandranātha
(Jaina) temple at Vijayamaṅgalam.

p.b: King's name lost, Year 46, Śaka 1179 = 1257 A.D.

inscription.p: Mutilated. Gift of the door post by a member of Veḷḷāḷan Paḍaittalaikaḷ clan.

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(A.R. No. 601 of 1905.)
Kāvattampāḷaiyam near Vijayamangalam, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On a slab set up at the tank in the village, Kāvattampāḷaiyam.
inscription.p:
Latter part damaged. Seems to record king's order restoring the tax-free status of the dēvadāna
village Kūḍūr in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu to the temple of Chittiramēḻi-viṇṇagar in Vijaiyamangalam. It is said that
some time after the earlier gift had been made the village was added to the taxable category of the nāḍu
by the king's officials, hence the order. [Lamps, plough and some other images are sculptured above
and below the inscription.]

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(A.R. No. 602 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Built in the end. Seems to be an undertaking given by the trustees of the temple to two officials
that they would conduct all the stipulated services in the temple using the income from the
tirumaḍaiviḷāgam, etc. as per the old documents.

l.ta:

paṭima •••mai kuṭuttu naṭatti vi

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(A.R. No. 603 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south base of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Records that a woman of Veḷḷāḷan Chāttandaikaḷ clan of Vijayamangalam made some repairs to
the temple of Chandrapura-Uḍaiyār at Tiṅgaḷūr in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu.

l.ta:
virarācentira
teva[r*]kku tiruveḻuttiṭṭu cellāniṉṟa tiruṉālliyāṇṭu irupat[ā]-

vatu taimāta[t*]tu kuṟuppu nāṭṭu tiṅkaḷḷū[r*] uṭai[yār*] cantirapura uṭaiyār

cevittāḷ §

inscription.head:
No. 389.
(A.R. No. 604 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south and east base of central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Built in at the beginning. The king made a gift of a tank with its cultivable land for celebrating a
festival called Kulōttuṅga-chōḻan sandi using one third of the cultivator's share (kuḍimakkaḷvāram) of the
produce of the land.

l.ta:
koṉerinmaikoṇṭān kuṟuppunāṭṭiṟ tiṅkaḷūr āḷuṭaiyār
cantirapuravicuramuṭaiyār koyiliṟ tevarkaṉmikaḷukku nammolai
kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu iṉṉāyaṉāṟku amutupaṭi kolottuṅkacoḻan cantiyāka
amutu ceytaruḷukaikku nāṅkuṭutta kuḷam ikkoyilukku vaṭakkiṟ
tāmaraikkuḷam ikkuḷattiṉki[ṭ]puṉcey viḷaitta kuṭimakkaḷe yikkuḷattāl
nirpāyumiṭam viḷaittuk kuṭiniṅkāttevartāṉamāka uḻutu viḷaitta nilattukku
muṉṟattoṉ[ṟu]vāraṅ kaḷattiley kaṇkāṇittu aḷavukoḷḷa
eṭuttikkuṭuppārkaḷāka

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(A.R. No. 605 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the east and the north bases of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at Tingaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Records king's gift of the village of Tēraiyūr alias Kulōttunga-Chōḻanallūr in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu to
the temple of Chandrapurīśvaramuḍaiyār at Tiṅgaḷūr in the same nāḍu. All the taxes (nanchey punchey
puravu, siddāyam, elavai, ugavai, maṉṟupāḍu, teṇḍakuṟṟam, chīkāriyappēṟu, adikārappēṟu, etc.) from
the village were given to the temple. The officer Chōḻamāṇikka-mūvēndavēḷān signed the grant.

l.gr: svastiśrī [||*] ētada viśvamahipāla maulimālāvi bhūṣaṇam śrīkulō
l.ta:
muṭaiyār tevarkaṉmikaḷukku nammolai
kuṭuttapaṭiyā[vatu] i[t]te[vaṟ]ku amutu[paṭi] • • •

tuppaṭi pūccuppaṭikkum tiruppaṇi ceyvikkai[k*]kum nāṅkuṭutta ūr innāṭṭu
teraiyūrāṉa kulottuṅkacoḻanallūreṉṟu ūreṟṟit

naṅkarumañ colluvār pāṇar puravukaḷum maṟ[ṟu]m eppeṟpaṭṭutūm

utakapūrvamāka iccātaṉam paṇṇik kuṭuttom ippaṭi[k] kallilu cempilum
veṭṭik koḷḷap peṟuvārkaḷāka cantirā-

tittavarai celvatāka nammolai kuṭuttom ivvūr veṭṭi muṭṭaiāḷ
ittevaṟku veṇṭum tiruppaṇi ceyvutāka kuṭuttom itu nantamar uḷḷār
pāṟpaṭuttuk kuṭukka ivai coḻakulamāṇikka muventaveḷāṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 391.
(A.R. No. 606 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at
Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Registers a letter from the officer Akaḷaṅka-nāḍāḻvān to the ūr and ūrāḷikaḷ of Tiṅgaḷūr, asking
them to conduct the Paṅguni festival in the temple of Chandrapurīśvaram-uḍaiyār using some tax on oil
(iṟaiyeṇṇai) and some paddy income from the village.

l.ta: [v]āṉ olai tiṅkaḷurum ūrāḷikaḷuṅ kaṇṭu taṅkaḷūr nāyaṉār ca

ḻiyum aiñcuṭṭi yeṇṇai eḻukuṟuṇi nāṉāḻiyum āka eṇṇaikalaṉe kuṟuṇi
oru

nāḻiyum ivvūril iṟaik[ā]ṇiyil muppatiṉ kalakāṇiyu c[e]lliṭṭukkoṇṭu
tiruṉāḷ
eḻuttu ivai vicai[yakāmav]eḷāṉ eḻuttu itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(A.R. No. 607 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at
Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Gift of an achchu by a member of Veḷḷāḷan Paṉaingāḍaikaḷ clan of Kūḍūr in Kuṟuppu-
nāḍu for a sandhyā lamp in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(A.R. No. 608 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa, left of entrance in front of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara
temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Gift of an achchu for a lamp by a member of Veḷḷāḷan Vaṇṇakkar clan of Tiṅgaḷūr in Kuṟuppu-
nāḍu.

l.ta:
ṇṇakkaril periyakoṟṟaṉ koṟṟaneṉ cantirapuramicu[ra]muṭaiy[ā]rkku
cantiyātipam oṉṟukku i-

ṇaṉ pālattuvāra kottirattil tevaṉ
periyāṉ cittirameḻipaṭṭaṉeṉ

paḻañcalākai accu onṟuṅ kaikkoṇṭu cantirātittavarai tiruviḷakkoṉṟu
[ce]luttuveṉāka ivvaccu [kai]-
kkoṇṭeṉ kuṭaṅkoṭu koyil pukuvāṉ celuttuvāṉāka paṉmāhecu

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(A.R. No. 609 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa, left of entrance in front of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara
temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Gift of an achchu for a lamp by a member of Koḻukattāḷi Vēṭṭuvar clan of Tiṅgaḷūr in
Kuṟuppu-nāḍu.

l.ta:
nāṭṭu tiṅkaḷurilirukkum koḻuka[t]tāḷi veṭṭuvaril mata[va pi]ḷḷai • • kura •••[tu]

paḻañ[ca*]lākai

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(A.R. No. 610 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On a pillar of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.

p.b: Koṅgu, Parakēsarivarman Vikrama-Chōḻa, Year 2, Śaka 1197 = 1175 A.D.

inscription.p:
A portion of the inscription (lines 8 to 13) is written from the bottom upwards. Says that a member
of Taṟaiya-Vēṭṭuvar clan of Tiṅgaḷūr, called Samaiya-mantiri Chiṟuppiḷḷai, made the pedestal for the
deity Vināyaga-piḷḷaiyār set up in the Tirunaḍaimāḷigai of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(A.R. No. 611 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On another pillar of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at
Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p: Records that a merchant of Namaṉi in Nāḍāḻvān-nāḍu set up a balipīṭha.

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(A.R. No. 612 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On a third pillar of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at
Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Records the setting up of the image of Astradēvar by a merchant of Arachanārāyaṇa-perunderu
alias Sundarapāṇḍya-puram in Kalvāyi-nāḍu of Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: vaṟku yāṇṭu muṉṟāvataṟ ketirāvatu kuṟuppu nāṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(A.R. No. 613 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On a fourth pillar of the maṇḍapa, in front of the central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at
Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p: Records the vēṭṭavūrār of Tiṅgaḷūr made a portion of the Tirunaḍaimāḷigai.

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(A.R. No. 614 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the door-post of the kitchen in the Pushpanatha (Jaina) temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.

p.b: Konāṭṭāṉ (Koṅgu,) Vikrama Chōḻa, Year 40, Śaka 967 = A.D. 1045.

inscription.p:
Records the erection of mukamaṇḍapa in the Chandiravasati (Jaina temple) by Kaṇita-
māṇikkachcheṭṭi, alias Aṟattuḻān-devan Poṉṉaṉ.
[Published in EI, XXX, No. 41, p. 243-49.]

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(A.R. No. 615 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Alagiyaraja-Perumal temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Records that the king granted the entire tax revenue of the village of Tāḷūṉṟi in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu to
the temple of Sundara-pāṇḍya-Viṇṇagar Emberumān at Tiṅgaḷūr. A number of taxes (kaḍamai, oṭṭachchu,
etc) is mentioned. The officials Kālingarāyan, Kachchirāyan and Viḻuppādarāyan have signed the
document.

edition.l: śrī [||*] tribhuvaṉaccakrava[ ttik] oṉeriṉmaikoṇ

l.ta: ḷuṅ kuṟṟateṇṭamum elavai yukavai maṟṟum eppeya-
luñ cempilum veṭṭi ivvūr kuṭiyeṟṟik koḷka
ippaṭikku kāliṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku kacciyarāyaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(A.R. No. 616 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Alagiyaraja-Perumāḷ temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.
inscription.p:
Records a sale of land for an amount of 100 paṇam to the temple by a member of
Veḷḷāḷan Vaṇṇakkar clan of the village.

l.ta: ṭait taimmātam mutal tiyati[yi]l nāṉ koṇṭu kaṭava-

paṇam nūṟu ippaṇam nūṟukkum eṉṉuṭaiya nilam kāṟkaṇkūla kaḻaṉikaḷil

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(A.R. No. 617 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Alagiyaraja-Perumāḷ temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.

p.b: Koṅgu, Sundara-Pāṇḍya, Year 23 = c. 14th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of money (22 paṇam) for two lamps by a Chaukshāyaṇan Perumāḷ Nambi of Tiruppattūr in
Kēraḷasiga-vaḷanāḍu, a sub division of Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
koccaṭapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcuntarapāṇṭiya
te[va]ṟku yāṇṭu irupattumuṉṟāvatu kuṟuppunāṭṭut tiṅkaḷur nāyaṉār
aḻa[kapperumā*]ḷuk-

rupputtūr
caukṣ[ā]yaṇaṉ perumāḷ nampikkāka iṉāyaṉāṟku iraṇṭu

ttavarai kuṭaṅko[ṇ*]ṭu koyil
pukuvār erippomāka kalveṭṭikkuṭuttom i

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(A.R. No. 618 of 1905.)
Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Alagiyaraja-Perumāḷ temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.

p.b: Koṅgu, Rājakēsarivarman Vīra Pāṇḍya, Year 18 = 1283 A.D.

inscription.p:
Registers a decision of the ūr and ūrāḷikaḷ of Tāḷūṉṟi alias Vikramachōḻa-puram to contribute
some specified quantity of rice daily to the temple for feeding the śrivaishṇava pilgrims.
[Palaeographically this inscription is interesting in that it has the script for the aydam letter in line 4].

l.ta:
vaṟkut tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellāniṉṟa tirunalliyāṇṭu patineṭṭāvatu
āvaṇimātamutal kuṟuppunāṭṭut tiṅkaḷūrc cuntarapāṇṭiya viṇṇakar nāyanār
aḻakiyapperu-

coḻapurattu ūrum ūrāḷikaḷom
eṅkaḷ nāyaṉār aḻakapperumāḷukku nāṅkaḷ cātaṉam paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu
iṉṉāyanāṟku nāṅkaḷ nirvār-

ttu nāḷo[ṉ*]ṟukku ponṉāṭunāḻiyāl uriarici amutu ceytu ippicātam
eṅkaḷ taṉmamāka tecāntiri
kku uriyarici celuttuvomākavum celuttum iṭattu aḵkam

[Picture] The semi-vowel character ‘ah’ denoted by three dots may be one of its
earliest occurrences.

eṟṟakkuṟai

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(A.R. No. 619 of 1905.)
Veḷḷiyyarasal, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Māndīśvara temple at Veḷḷiyyarasal.
inscription.p:
An endowment of five paḻañjalākai-achchu, made by Kaṉakapālaṉ to the temple
Mandāniśvaram-uḍaiyār in Vaḷḷi-eṟichchal in Kāngeya-nāḍu, for food offerings and lamps.

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 620 of 1905.)
Veḷḷiyyarasal, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On a stone built into the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Māndīśvara temple
at Veḷḷiyyarasal.
inscription.p:
Vatteḻuttu. Fragment. Seems to be an arrangement for gift of cloth and ghee to the temple of
Tiruppāṇḍikoḍumuḍi. The cloth was collected from a tax on shops (kaḍappuravu).

l.ta: m kāviti kaṭappuravu puṭavai muṉṟiṉāṉāluṅ ku • va

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 621 of 1905.)
Veḷḷiyyarasal, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District.
On a second stone built into the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Mandisvara
temple at Veḷḷiyyarasal.
inscription.p:
Vatteḻuttu. Fragment. Seems to relate to some punishments to be imposed on some defaulters. The
record was put up by the official Āmūr-uḍaiyān Nārāyaṇan Chiṟiyān.

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 622 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock just below the boulder known as Nāgarguṇḍu, west of the steps leading to the
Ardhanārīśvara temple on the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
Grantha and Tamil. Records that the nāṭṭavar of Kīḻkarai-Pūnduṟai-nāḍu in Koṅgu, alias
Vīrachōḻa-maṇḍalam decided to assign the village of Nāppaḷḷippaḷḷam in their nāḍu that was lying waste
for many, Year to somebody (name lost) from Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam, asking him to reclaim, cultivate and
enjoy it free of taxes. It seems the village was made into a brāhmaṇa settlement, whose name is lost.

l.ta: ppaḷḷik kuḷamum uṭaintu itil aṇaiyiṉ kiḻkarai
pāḻāy kiṭakkaiyil ivvūr naṉcey puṉcey nā-

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 623 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock west of the boulder known as Nāgarguṇḍu, west of the steps leading to the Ardhanāriśvara
temple on the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold by kāseṭṭānkal for burning a lamp in the temple of
Tiruchchankōṭṭu-Āḻvār by Muri-Amudanār, the wife of Iḷangōnaḍikaḷ. The gift was left in charge of the
body called Panniraṇḍu-nāṭṭu-perumakkaḷ.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII, No. 242.]

l.ta: ṉṟa yāṇṭu pati[ṉā]lāvatu ivvāṇṭu tiruccaṅkoṭṭu paṉṉiraṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 624 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock west of the boulder known as Nāgarguṇḍu, west of the steps leading to the Ardhanāriśvara
temple on the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
This is a damaged incomplete record. It seems to register an endowment of money for feeding (?)
on ēkādasi days in the temple, made by Muri-Amudanār the lady mentioned in the above inscription.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII No. 243.]

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 625 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock west of the boulder known as Nāgarguṇḍu, west of the steps leading to the Ardhanāriśvara
temple on the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
This registers an endowment of 25 kaḻañju of gold by a certain Koṟṟandai Poduvan and Pērangi
Maṇiyan for feeding Brahmanas every, Year on the day of Svāti in Puraṭṭādi month which was the natal
star Koṟṟandai Poduvan Māṟi, respectively the daughter of the first mentioned person and the wife of the
latter. The gift was entrusted to the body Pāṇḍināṭṭu [= panniraṇḍu ?]-Perumakkaḷ of Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII No. 261.]

l.ta: tuvanum ivaḷ maṇavāḷaṉṉāṉa peraṅ ••••• ṉum ivviruvarum •••••

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 626 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock west of the boulder known as Nāgarguṇḍu, west of the steps leading to the Ardhanāriśvara
temple on the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakesarivarman Rajendra I, Year 10 = 1024 A.D.

inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to record some agreement among the uvachchar regarding their drumming
duties during the early morning service in the temple.

l.ta: varkku tiruppaḷḷi eḻucci ivvāṇṭu mutal ko • • •

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 627 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock west of the boulder known as Nāgarguṇḍu, west of the steps leading to the Ardhanāriśvara
temple on the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers an endowment of 20 kaḻañju of gold, by Muri Kāmakkanār, the wife
of Iḷangōnaḍikaḷ, for feeding 20 brāhmaṇas on the day of Ārdra every month, for the merit of one
Ēkavīra-Divākaradēvar. A body called Tiruvāthirai-gaṇapperumakkaḷ accepted this gift.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII No. 244.]

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 628 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock west of the boulder known as Nāgarguṇḍu, west of the steps leading to the Ardhanāriśvara
temple on the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to record a gift of 26 kaḻañju of gold by one Uttaman Gaṇavadi,
which was entrusted to the body know as Chōṇāṭṭu-Perumakkaḷ, for feeding Brahmanas on the hill on
festival days in the month of Masi.
[Published in SII Vol XIX No. 298.]

l.ta: cai koṇṭu ivaṉ piṟanta nāḻ māci tirunā-

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 629 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock west of the boulder known as Nāgarguṇḍu, west of the steps leading to the Ardhanāriśvara
temple on the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
Gift of 20 kaḻañju of gold, through the body called Paṉṉiraṇḍunāṭṭu-perumakkaḷ, by the lady
Kāmakkanār, wife of Iḷangōnaḍikaḷ, for feeding 20 Brahmanas on the day of ēkādasi. The gift was to be
administered by the body called Tiruvēkādasi-gaṇapperumakkaḷ.
[Published in SII Vol. XIII No. 232.]

edition.l: brāhmaṇarai yūṭṭuvatāka [ pontatu] tiruvekā

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 630 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Arubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēśarivarman, Year 28 = c. 10th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of gold for lamp through the body called Paṉṉiraṇḍunāṭṭu-perumakkaḷ. The donor is not
known.

l.ta:
kopparakecari paṉmakku tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellā niṉṟa

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(A.R. No. 631 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p: Damaged. Gift of gold for feeding Brahmanas. The donor seems to be a woman from Pugaḻiyūr.

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(A.R. No. 632 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Chōḻa, Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēśarivarman, Parāntaka I, Year 37 = 944 A.D.

inscription.p: Fragment. Details lost.

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(A.R. No. 633 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Chōḻa, Madiraikoṇḍa Parakēśarivarman. Parāntaka I, Year 20 = 927 A.D.

inscription.p: Incomplete and damaged. Mentions Tiruppiḍavūr.

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(A.R. No. 634 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p: Damaged. Gift of gold. [Pilgrims scrawls in modern characters have defaced this inscription].

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(A.R. No. 635 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p: Completely damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(A.R. No. 636 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of 20 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the Tiruvōṇagaṇapperumakkaḷ
at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu by one Araṭṭanakkar's son on behalf of Tañchamaṇikaṇṭi-Oḍaiyamandāḷ, a lady
residing at Irōḍu (Erode), for feeding 20 Brahmanas (once) every month (probably on the Tiruvōṇam
day).
[Published in SII Vol. XIX No. 219.]

l.ta: ḷukkāka araṭṭa nakkar makaṉ tiruvoṇak kaṇapperumakkaḷ
vacam vaitta poṉ irupatiṉ kaḻañcu poṉṉu-

inscription.head:
No. 422.
(A.R. No. 637 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It seems to record some endowment made by one Raṇamuka-Nāraṇan,
for feeding Brahmanas (in the temple on the hill) on the day of his natal star Rohini in the Masi month.
[Published in SII Vol. XIX No. 299.]

edition.p: Another inscription is engraved along with this

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(A.R. No. 638 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p: Gift of gold for feeding (Brāhmaṇas). The donor's name is not given.

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(A.R. No. 639 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 5 kaḻañju of gold deposited with the Tiruvādirai-gaṇapperumakkaḷ at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu for feeding with the interest thereon five Brahmanas. Kāḍan Pudi-māṇikkan made the
gift for the merit of his daughter Muṇḍaṅkōn.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIX No. 426.]

l.ta: kaiyyil vaitta poṉ aiṅkaḻañcu ippoṉṉiṉ palicai

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(A.R. No. 640 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock east of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
Gift of gold for a lamp by a native of Kiḷinallūr in Kiḻār-kūṟṟam. The money was entrusted to the
panniraṇḍu-nāṭṭu-sabhai of Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, while the people of the ‘eighteen districts’ were to protect
the grant.

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(A.R. No. 641 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the boulder west of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Jatavarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya, Year 10 = c. 13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record a gift of land. Mentions the god Subrahmaṇya-Piḷḷaiyār standing on
the hill.
[Both estampage and transcript are not traceable.]

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(A.R. No. 642 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the boulder west of the steps known as Aṟubadāmpaḍi on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p: Mutilated at the end. Gift of gold. [Both estampage and transcript are not traceable.]

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(A.R. No. 643 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock close to the Papanasatirtha on the top of the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakesari Rajendra I, Year 23 = 1035 A.D.

inscription.p:
Partially damaged. Seems to record a gift of land. [Both estampage and transcript are not
traceable.]

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(A.R. No. 644 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock west of the Gaṇapatitīrtha on the hill of the Ardhanāriśvara temple at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Jatāvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya, Year 7 = c. 13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record a tax-free gift of land to the Piḷḷaiyāṟ (deity) of Tiruchcheṅgōḍu. It is
not clear who made the grant.

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(A.R. No. 645 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Ardhanāriśvarara temple on the hill
at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Records a gift of a village by Pemmarasu-Uḍaiyar, the king's agent (kāriyattukku
kaḍava), to the temples of Ardhanāriśvarara and Subrahmaṇya on the hill at Tiruchehengōḍu in Kīḻkarai-
Pūnduṟai-nāḍu, a district of Koṅgu, alias Virachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The gift village was Chūrappaḷḷi alias
Akaram Maṇṇākki-nattam in the same nāḍu.

l.ta: harāya uṭaiyar kāriyattukkukkaṭava pemmaraca uṭaiyar koṅkāṉa vīra-
coḻamaṇṭalattuk kiḻkaraip pūntuṟai nāṭṭut tirucceṅkoṭṭu tirumalaimel
paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu inta nāṭṭuccūrappaḷiyāṉa akaram [ma]ṇṇākki na-

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(A.R. No. 646 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the west wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Ardhanāriśvarara temple on the hill
at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
Records the building of the nirta-maṇḍapam by Tirumalaiyattappa Nallatambi-Kāngaiyaṉ, a
member of VeḷḷāḷaKaṇṇar clan of Mōrūr in Kīḻkarai-Pūnduṟai-naḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(A.R. No. 647 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Ardhanāriśvarara temple on the hill
at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
Registers the building of different components of the Mahā-maṇḍapam and Vilāsa-maṇḍapam by
Kumārasuvāmi-Kāngaiyaṉ, the younger son of Nallatambi-Kāngaiyan of Mōrūr, mentioned in the above
inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 433.
(A.R. No. 648 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Subrahmanya shrine in the Ardhanāriśvarara temple on
the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
Records the construction of mahā-maṇḍapam of Kumārasuvāmiyar, etc. by Attappa Immuḍi-
nallatambi Kāngaiyan Kavuṇḍan, the elder son of Nallatambi-Kāngaiyan referred to in the above two
inscriptions.

l.ta: ṭa[n u]paiyam kumāra cuvāmiyār ma(k)kā ma-
cai uḷpaṭa mukippittāṉ puṇṇiyattiṉāl aṉṉatāṉa comaṉeṉ yirun

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(A.R. No. 649 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Subrahmanya shrine in the Ardhanāriśvarara temple on
the hill at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu.
inscription.p:
End part much damaged. The king's ancestors mentioned as Visvanāta-NāyakaTirumalai-
Nāyaka-Muttuvirappa-Nayaka. He seems to have gifted a village with all its revenue income to the temple
at Tiruchcheṅgōḍu for various services. The village (name lost) was in Ēḻūr-nāḍu in Durgam sīrmai.

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(A.R. No. 650 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Subrahmanya shrine in the Ardhanāriśvarara temple on
the hill at Tirucheṅgōdu.
inscription.p:
Seems to record gift of a village of for the merit of the king's son (kumāra) Muttu-Tirumalai-
Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(A.R. No. 651 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the base of the maṇḍapa in front of the Subrahmanya shrine in the Ardhanārīśvara temple on the hill
at Tirucheṅgōḍu.

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Krishṇadēvarāya, Kali 4444 (irregular), Chitrabhānu, Vrichchika, Su. 12,
Friday, Revathi = 1522 A.D.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Records the gift of market-tolls in the Paṉṉiraṇḍu-paṟṟu within Kuṉṟattūr-durgam
(Rājya) in Koṅgu, alias Vīrachōḻa-maṇḍalam by Triyambaka-uḍaiyār for celebrating certain festivals in
the temple.

l.ta:
ṉ tulukūtaḷa vipāṭaṉ oṭṭiya taḷavipāṭaṉ
ka

lamum tiṟai koṇṭu
veṉṟaruḷiya rācātirācaṉ rācaparame

lakaraṇamum peṟṟa iṟṟa ṉāḷ koṅkāṉa
vīracoḻama
l.note: Same name is givenin Telugu characters with conch and discusat the end.

edition.l:
nakṣatrattil [ dvajāro] haṇamum repati nakṣatrattil teṟpatirunāḷ
naṭakka[kaṭava]tāka kaṭṭaḷai iṭṭu iṉta tiruṉāḷ vecca(t)tukku
caṉtaiyiluṇṭāṉa cuṅkattarakum ka[ṭa*]mai ēṟucammātam ātāya mutaliṭṭu teṟpat
tirunāḷ naṭatta kaṭṭaḷai iṭṭu attanāriśvaramuṭaiya tampirānār caṉnatiyile
uttaraṉa tuvāteci puṇyakālattile kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭu tāraivāttu kuṭutteṉ yinta
taṉmam cantirātityavarai naṭakka kaṭavatākavum yinta taṉmattai ta[ṉi o] •••
ttukku[varum] rācākkaḷum yinta taṉmattukku vanta [a]ntarāyam irukka
nāṭṭāṇmaikkāṟak kavuṇṭakaḷum tirunāḷai paripālaṉam paṇṇi
naṭattiṉavarkaḷukku nūṟu keṟpa aśvametaiyiyākam paṇṇiṉa palam
kaṅkaikaraile āyiraṅkoṭi kapilai kotāṉam paṇṇiṉa palam uṇṭāka
kaṭavatākavum yinta tirunāḷ naṭakkāmal akitam paṇṇiṉavaṉukku
taṅkalmā[tāpitāvai] ••••• kkaraiyilē koṉṟa pāvattile poka kaṭavatāka •••
•••

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(A.R. No. 652 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
inscription.p: East wall of the nritta-maṇḍapa in the Kailāśanāthaśvāmin temple at Tirucheṅgōdu

Records the building of the maṇḍapa by Vangārammaṉ, wife of Eṟamacheṭṭi-nāyakkar. [A
Telugu version of this inscription is engraved close to it.]

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(A.R. No. 653 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the north wall of the Subrahmanya shrine in the Kailāśanāthaśvāmin temple at Tirucheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Dated in Kalyukti, Year 6 th day of Kārthikai month. = c. 17th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
Records the building of the tank and the Komārar shrine is by a nagarattār member of Andiyūr in
Vaḍakarai-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(A.R. No. 654 of 1905.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Taluk, Salem District.
On the ceiling of the gopura in the Kailāśanāthaśvāmin temple at Tirucheṅgōḍu.

p.b: Madurai Nāyaka Visvanātha Chokkaliṅga-Nāyaka, Śaka 1585, Sōbakiritu = 1663 A.D.

inscription.p:
In Tamil prose and verse. Records the building of the gōpura and of the temple of Kāsi-
Visvēśvara on the hill by Ṟecharuṇai Chēshāttiri.

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(A.R. No. 655 of 1905.)
Veḷḷār, Omalur Taluk, Salem District.
On a slab set up at the foot of the hill at Veḷḷār.
inscription.p:
Badly worn out. Seems to be a decision of the Iḍangai group relating to the village of Veḷārttuṟai
in Magada-nāḍu of Jayangoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. Mentions Iḍaṅgai mahāsēnai of Eḻupattonpatu-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(A.R. No. 656 of 1905.)
Veḷḷār, Omalur Taluk, Salem District.
On two faces of a broken slab at the foot of the hill at Veḷḷār.
inscription.p: Grantha and Tamil. Fragment. The preserved portion contains a few Hoysala birudas.

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(A.R. No. 657 of 1905.)
Veḷḷār, Omalur Taluk, Salem District.
On the top of the hill at Veḷḷār.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Mentions the nāṭṭavar and nagaram of Madaga-nāḍu alias Veḷḷārai-nāḍu and the
temple of Tirukkunṟīśvaramuḍaiyār.

l.ta: utti[ra*]ṭṭātiyum cattamiyum peṟṟa tiṅkaḷ kiḻamaināḷ veḷ-

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(A.R. No. 658 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the north and west walls of the central shrine in the Tirthagirisvara temple on the hill at Tirthamalai

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Vijaya Bhūpatirāya, Śaka 1331, Virōti, Makara, Purva 14, Monday,
Punarpūsam = 1409, January 20.

inscription.p:
Mentions that somebody (name lost) renovated the dilapidated tann in Ēḻūrpāḍi, a
tirunāmattukkāṇi village of the temple of Tīrttamalai and in appreciation of that he was given some land
free of taxes. Tīrttamalai was in Kūḍal-paṟṟu in Puṟamalai-nadu in Nigarili-Chōḻa-maṇḍalam on the south
bank of the river Peṇṇai.

l.ta:
ṭ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattu nāḷ nikarilicoḻamaṇṭalattu peṇṇaikku
teṉkarai aru-

malai cūḻnta puṟamalaināṭṭuk kūṭa[l]paṟṟu nāyinār tirttamalai nāyaṉār
tirunāmattukkāṇiyā[ṉa] ēḻūrpāṭiyil eri-

nerāka yi[ṭi]ntuk kiṭakkaiyil muṉṉāḷ •••vāṅkapperum •••ta •••yum •••[yum] •••yilu
tāmu ma[ṇ]
ppaḷḷattukku mel karaiyiliṭai vūr kiḻ karai yinta nilattil

māṟiṉār uṇṭāṉāl keṅkaik karai • • •

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(A.R. No. 659 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Tirthagirisvara temple on the hill at Tirthamalai.

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Vijayarāya Uḍaiyar, Śaka 1333, Khara, Year, Avani month, su. 11, Uttara,
Friday = 1411, August 29.

inscription.p: Incomplete. Seems to mention some gift made by a Kaikkōḷa-mudali.

l.ta:
rāya vipāṭaṉ pāṣaikku
tappuvarāya kaṇṭaṉ muvarāyakaṇṭaṉ pū

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(A.R. No. 660 of 1905.)
Tirttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tīrthagirisvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p:
Records that Aṟputamaḻagiyār alias Ayyanāyaṉār, the wife of Muḍikoṇḍachōḻa-Kaṟkaṭa-
mārāyan Chokkapperumāḷ, the chief of Kūḍal in Puṟamalai-nāḍu set up the image of Kuṉṟameṟinda-
perumāḷ and donated a number of ornaments to the shrine.

l.ta: ḻvār peṟṟa piḷḷai aṟputamaḻakiyarāna aiyyanāyaṉār marit-
tuk kuṉṟameṟinta perumāḷai eḻuntaruḷuvittu iṭṭa tiruvā-
paraṇam tiruttoṭu iraṇṭiṉāliṭai accu tūkkam urai eṭ-
urai eḻum [vā*]ḷi muttiraṇṭum pavaḻam onṟum poṉṉumakappaṭa ā-
ṟu paṇattūkkam urai eḻarai poṟpūṇnūliṭai onpatekā-
kāciṭai araikkaḻañcu muttum pavaḻamum vaṭamuṉṟu muttu to-
ṇṇūṟṟāṟu pavaḻam aṟupatteḻum iṭṭa pākalam iṭai muppa-
ṭai kai[cu]kuṟaiyu aṟupaṇam tiruvaṭṭamaṇi iṭai añcupaṇa-
ttūkkam urai eṭṭemukkāl maṇi patiṉāṟum tiruvamutupaṭikku-

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(A.R. No. 661 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tīrthagirisvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p:
Partly built in. Gift of cows and a lampstand for a lamp to the temple at Tīttamalai by Nāyaṉ
Paḍalan alias Chittiramēḻi Amarapuyangara-tāyal-nāḍāḻvān of Miniyūr in Tāyal-nāḍu.

l.ta:
tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ cirikulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu muppattu
nāl[ā]vatu puṟamalai nāṭṭu uṭaiyār tittamalai āḻvuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkut
tāyalnāṭṭu miṉiyūruṭaiya nāyaṉpaṭalanāṉa cittiramoḻi amarapuyaṅkarat

kkumari iṭaikkuṟāṟ pacukkonṟān piramakattip paṭuvāṉ
itupanmāyakeccu

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(A.R. No. 662 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tīrthagirisvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai.

p.b: W. Gaṅga (?), Mallidēva Mahārajā = c. 13th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of the village of Ālambāḍi in Āḍaiyūr-nāḍu by Mallidēva-mahārājar. [The inscription is
preceded by the words Svastiśrī Kuvalāpuraparamēśvara gaṅgakulōttma in characters which appear to be
somewhat older.]

l.ta:
nāyaṉār
tittamalaināyaṉāṟku tiruṉāmattu kāṇiyāṉa tevatāṉamāka malli

vāttuk kuṭutta āṭaiyūr nāṭu melpaṟṟu ālampāṭi ••••• ṭaiyaṉāyaṉāṟku
vūrkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(A.R. No. 663 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tīrthagirisvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p: Gift of 16 cows and a bull for a lamp by Tittan Paḍalan of Karimalaippākkam in Puṟamalai-nāḍu.

l.ta:
pākkamuṭaiyāṉ tittanpaṭalanen tittamalai uṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku viṭṭa viḷakku
cantirātitta[varai c]elvatāka • • •
[ṭu]araikku viṭṭamāṭu* patunāṟu iḻacapam onṟu itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(A.R. No. 663A of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tīrthagirisvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai [On a stone slab built along the previous inscription in the same wall.]
inscription.p: Fragment. Mentions a lady called Tīttāḻvār daughter of Chōḻagangap-pallavariayar.

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(A.R. No. 664 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tīrthagirisvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai.

p.b: Vijayanagara, Bukkaṇṇa-Uḍaiyar, Śaka 1322, Pramāti, Masi, su. 14, Magam = 1400, February 9.

inscription.p:
Gift of land. Mentions gift of land with well in their kāṇi-āṭchi villages, Bukkarāyanpēṭṭai and
Avanivēndarāmanallur, by the ūravarkaḷ of Tāyyil-nāḍu.

l.ta:
ttunāḷ nāyiṉār tirttamalai uṭaiya nāyinārkku
tirunāmattukkāṇi āka [tā]yyi[l*] nāṭṭil ūrava[r*]kaḷ kāṇiāṭci āṉa
ūrkaḷil nañcai nilattil viṭṭa nilamāvatu iṉṉāṭṭil pukkarāyaṉ

iruṅkoḷaṉ tāḻaiyental k[ā]liṭṭa kiṇaṟum cūlakkallu nāṭṭittanta kuḻi

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(A.R. No. 665 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tīrthagirisvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai.

p.b: Vijayanagara Śaka 1325, Subhanu, Kaṭaka, Pūrvapaksha, 12, Monday = 1403, July 16.

inscription.p: Built in. Incomplete. Refers to the names of some temple girls, who had kāṇiyāṭchi in the temple.

l.ta:
kkut tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ
mūvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ pū

[tu] nāḷ tittamalai nāyināṟkkuk kāṇiyāṭcciyāṉa tevaraṭiyār kāttāḷ
kūttaṉ tevakaḷnācciyār puḻukuneypperumāḷ peṟṟārvalliyār
cipatinācciyār tiruvinnā-

[c]ciyār ciraṅkar āṭkoṇṭa nācciyār ceṉaṉ mallūceṭṭiyār
viṭṭatīttāḷ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(A.R. No. 666 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tīrthagirīvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai.

p.b:
Vijayanagara Vira-Dēvarāya-Uḍaiyar. Plavaṅga, Kanni, su. 10. Monday, Sothi (irregular) = 1427
A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Mentions that when the Mahāpradhāni Lakkaṇṇa-Uḍaiyar was the governor of the
area, one Keyidēva-irākuttar of Muḷvāyil gave the ubayamāṟgam (tax) in Kūḍalūr in Puṟamalai-nāḍu for
a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tīrttamalai-nāyanār.

l.ta:
cellāniṉṟa palavaṅka varuṣattuk kaṉṉināyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu tecamiyum
tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa cotināḷ muḷvāyil irāku • • •

i[k]kavuḷiya ammaṉ teva irāku makaṉār keyiteva irākuttar puṟamalaināṭṭu
kūṭalūril upayamāṟkattukku cellum •••[kku matta rākutta]

••••• keṅkaikkaraiyiṟ kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa pāvattil pokak
kaṭavārkaḷākavum

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(A.R. No. 667 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the south wall of the Silai-Nāyaḍu maṇḍapa in the Tīrthagirīvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p:
In Telugu. Refers to the consecration of the shrine of Kāsi-Viśvanatha by Vāchamma, wife of Mutyāla-
nāyana.

l.tetr: avun#*i bahudhānya nāma samvatśaram jyēshṭa bahuḷa 5lu bagutula bili nāyanikū-
māruḍu mutyāla nāyanivāru pemḍlāmu vāchammagāru kaṭṭimchina kāsiviśvanātha

inscription.head:
No. 454.
(A.R. No. 668 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On a slab with Durga figure placed near the central shrine of the Tīrthagirīśvara temple on the hill at
Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p: In Vatteḻuttu characters. Damaged. Refers to the deity Śrī Pakavati Kuṟuchchūli.

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. 669 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock to the west of the central shrine of the Tīrthagirīśvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p:
Mentions that Rājēntrachōḻa-viriyūr-nāḍuḍaiyāṉ alias Uttamachōḻa-puṟamalai-nāḍāḻvāṉ, a
subordinate (?) of Rājentrakariyachōḻa-pēraiyan Kaṟkaḍai-Mahārāyar, made the śrīpīḍam and
chēkaṇḍikai.

l.ta: hārāyar iṭaip peṟṟuṭaiya tāyam pat[tu] iruvū[r]-

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. 670 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock to the west of the central shrine of the Tīrthagirīśvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p:
Gift of three villages by Rājēntra-Chōḻa-Viriyūr-nāḍuḍaiyān alias Uttama-Chōḻa-
Puṟamalaināḍāḷvān. The donor repaired the temple and constructed the muka-maṇḍapa. The temple is
called Tīrttamalai-Uḍaiya-Mahādēvar in Puṟamalai-nāḍu a sub-division of Tagaḍūr-nāḍu, in Nigarili-
chōḻa-maṇḍalam. The gifted villages were Kuṟumatali, Avvaḍi-mangalam, and Alaiyūr.

l.ta:
coḻateva[r]kku yāṇṭu i[ru]pattonpatāvatu ṉikarili coḻamaṇṭalattut
takaṭūrnāṭṭu-
mum meṭuppitten kaṟkaṭaivaiyriyanāna viyaḷaṉ maṟapaṭai kaṇṭanā-
tirumaṭaippaḷḷip puṟam[āka ku]ṭuttavūr kuṟumataliyum avvaṭimaṅkalamum alaimalai

nāṭṭil alaiyūrum kuṭutteṉ uttamacoḻappuṟamalai nāṭāḻvāneṉ itu
aḻi[pāṉ]

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. 671 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock to the west of the central shrine of the Tīirthagirīśvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p:
Records the building of the shrines of Kshētrapāla and Chaṇḍēśvara by Mummuḍichōḻanāti, son
of Vayiśraṇabaktoṉparam and a subordinate of Uttamachōḻa-puṟamalai-nāḍāḻvāṉ. [This inscription is
engraved below the above inscription and therefore this could belong to 29th, Year].

l.ta: ••••• uttama co[ḻa*]p pu[ṟa]malai nāṭāḻvāṉ aṭiyeṉ vayi
ṉ makaṉeṉ mum[mu*]ṭiccoḻa nātiyeṉ tirttamalai mā

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. 672 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock to the west of the central shrine of the Tīirthagirīśvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman, Year 4 = c. 11th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It records a gift of gold for burning a perpetual lamp in the Kudal-Āḻvār
shrine by the queen of Mummuḍichōḻa-Perumāḷ, whose name cannot be properly made out.
[Published in SII Vol. XIX No. 85.]

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. 673 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock to the west of the central shrine of the Tīirthagirīśvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarman, Year 4 = c. 11th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
This records a similar gift of gold for burning lamp in the Kūḍal Āḻvār temple by a
Aiyyan Vīramādēviyār of Punguṇḍṟam, a queen of Mummuḍichōḻa Perumāl.
[Published in SII, Vol. XIX, No. 86.]

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. 674 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock to the west of the central shrine of the Tīirthagirīśvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.

p.b: Chōḻa Kulōttuṅga, Year 17 = c. 11th-12th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of 32 cows for a lamp, by a merchant (vāṇiyaṉ) called Perungoḻappaṉaiyaṉ Chempaṉ alias
Aṇukkanambi of Kūḍal.

l.ta: m itil maṉ[ṟā]ṭi kaḻuttaṟu[t*]tāḷ umiyaṉ virapattirakkoṉ

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. 675 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock to the west of the central shrine of the Tīirthagirīśvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.

p.b: Chōḻa, Kulōttuṅga, Year 12 = c. 11th-12th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that on the instructions of Kaṟkaḍamārāya Pūmināyakar, one Naṟṟa-koṟṟantai
Pērāyiramuḍaiyān Ponnan of Tirumunaippāḍi made a tank in the village Kuṟichilipāri in Puṟamalai-nāḍu
and gave it as tirumaḍaippaḷḷi-puṟam of Tīrttamalai-uḍaiyār temple.

l.ta: koṟṟantai ponnan kuṭiniṅkāt te[va*] tānamāka uḻu-

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. 676 of 1905.)
Tīrttamalai, Harur Taluk, Salem District.
On the rock to the west of the central shrine of the Tīirthagirīśvara temple on the hill at Tīrttamalai.
inscription.p:
Records the appointment of one Patanjali-pāḍiyūran alias Sankara-baṭtan as the priest (sthānam)
with hereditary rights in the temple of Tīrttamalai, by the nāṭṭār of Puṟamalai-nāḍu in Tagaḍūr-nāḍu and
the chief Maṟupaḍai-kaṇḍan Jayangoṇḍchōḻan. Tagaḍūr-nāḍu is mentioned as a district of Ganga-nāḍu
in Nigarilichōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: coḻaṉum iru eṅkaḷ ṉāṭṭu tirttamalai uṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(A.R. No. 677 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkōṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.

p.b:
Vijayanagara, Krishnadēvarāya, Śaka 1447, Vyaya, Dakshiṇāyanam, Varsha ritu, Pūrva,
Guruvāra, Pūra = 1526 A.D.

inscription.p:
Built in at the end. Gift of a village (name lost) in Teṉkallaka-nāḍu by pachchai-Perumal
Pachchai Kaṇḍiyadēvar with the approval of Chinnappa-Nayaka, to the temple of Tiruvēḍakam as
kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadāna. The fixed tax rates to be paid to the temple by the donor are mentioned = 5 paṇam
on every mā of land cutivating banana and sugarcane, and three-fourths of this rate for the kōḍai-kuṟuvai
crops.

inscription.head:
No. 464.
(A.R. No. 678 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkōṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Māravarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya I, Year 4+1+1 = 1221 A.D.

inscription.p:
Built in at the beginning. The king gave order fixing the annual remuneration (śrīkāṟiyappēṟu),
as 30 kalam of paddy and one achchu, to be given from the temple to śrīkāriyam officer, upon the
request made by Chembiyaṉ Viḻupparaiyaiṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. 679 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkōṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Māravarman Sundara I, Year 7 = 1222 A.D.

inscription.p:
Built in the middle. This is an order of the king reducing the tax rates, in king as well as money
on different crops that were being paid by the chaṭṭar (brāhmaṇas) of Chōḻakulāntaka-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Pāgaṉūr-kūṟṟam as they were feeling the existing rates as heavy (ataippu). The
king took this action at the request of his elder sister (akkaṉ). The new rates are specified for various
crops (paddy, tiṉai, varagu, eḷ, pul, iṟuṅgu) and cropping seasons (like perum-pū, aiṟpachi-
kuṟuvai). The rate for pāḍikāval (watchmanship) is also given.

inscription.head:
No. 466.
(A.R. No. 680 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkōṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine, left of entrance, in the Patrikāparamēśvara
temple at Tiruvēḍagam.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Jadāvarman Kulasēkharadēva I, Year 2 = 1192 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. This records the royal grant of some land as tax-free dēvadāna to the deity of a minor shrine in
the temple of Tiruvēḍagam at the request of a senior officer.

l.ta:
pūviṉ kiḻatti meviviṟṟiruppa metiṉimātunitiyiṟ puṇara vayappor
maṭantai j[eya]-
ppuyattiruppa mākkalai maṭantai vākkiṉil viḷaṅkat ti[caiyiru] nāṉku [micai]

iṟaiyili •••••

inscription.head:
No. 467.
(A.R. No. 681 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkōṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On the east wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine, right of entrance, in the
Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Tax-free gift of land by king to the maṭha called Tiruñānasambandan-maḍam
managed by one Aṇukka-vaṉṟoṇḍar for feeding twenty visiting asceties (tapasyar) there.

l.ta: •••kuṅkolāl araiye iraṇṭumā nilam kaṭamaiyum antarāyamum viṉi-
••••• tiruveṭakattil vaṭakālilp perumpūvum kuṟuvaiyum viḷaiyum nila

inscription.head:
No. 468.
(A.R. No. 682 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkōṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On the east wall of the first prakara, right of entrance, of the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.
inscription.p:
Partly damaged. King's gift of the payment towards the tax mākkalam-pāḍikāval on certain land
in Tiruvēḍagam in possession of the sabhā of the village, for food offerings, etc in the temple.

l.ta:
vatu tirumukappaṭikalveṭṭu pākaṉūrkūṟṟattut
tevatāṉa[m] tiruveṭakattu uṭaiyār tiruveṭakaṭattu •••śrīkāriyañ ceyvāṉu-

kkum tevakaṉmikkum innāyaṉāṟku tiruppaṭimāṟṟu uḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum nitta
nimantaṅkaḷukku ivvūr

yār paṟṟu ••••• ittēvar tevatāṉam pāṭakappaṭi
ni-

lam onṟaraiye yiraṇṭu māvukku perumpūvukkum kuṟuvaikkum namakku iṟuttu
varukiṟa mākkalam pāṭikkāvalāl vanta nellu ivvāṇṭu patiṉālāvatu mutal
kuṭuttom iv-

vāṇṭu mutal kaikkoṇṭu tiruppaṭimāṟṟu uḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum nimantaṅkaḷ
cellap paṇṇi ippaṭi cantirā

tuk koḷka ippaṭikku ivaimiḻalaikkūṟṟattu naṭuvil kūṟṟatut
tuñcalūruṭaiyāṉ araican civatāṇṭāṉ ••••• eḻuttu •••

inscription.head:
No. 469.
(A.R. No. 683 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkōṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On the east wall of the first prakara, right of entrance, of the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Maravarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya I, Year 9 = 1225 A.D.

inscription.p:
Registers a copy of an old inscription belonging to the third regnal Year of Jaṭāvarman
Śrīvallabha (= 1124 A.D.) reinscribed now as the old structure had to be rebuilt. The old inscription
refers to a royal grant of the village called Aḻichiṟai Śrīkaraṇamaṅgalam in Ten-kallaka-nāḍu as tax-free
dēvadāna for the deity Umāsahitar consecrated by the queen Puvaṉimuḻuduḍaiyāḷ in the temple. That
grant is signed by a number of puravuvari-tiṇaikkaḷam officers.

l.ta:
śrīkomāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrīcoṇāṭu
vaḻaṅkiyaruḷiya vira

ttu [i]raṅka nācciyār cilaiyāle
ceyvittaruḷat tirumalaiyil kalveṭṭuppaṭi eṭuppu [|] tirumaṭantaiyuñ

ceyamaṭantaiyun tiruppuyaṅkaḷiliṉitiruppa irunilamum perumaiyeyti
yeṇṭicaiyuṅ ko[ṭ]

varumaṇi muṭicūṭit teṉkumariyum vaṭakaṅkaiyun tiraikkaṭalellaiyākap
pārmuḻutuṅ kayalāṇai parantu ceṅkoluṭan varaimaṉṉiya virasi

ulakamuḻutuṭaiyāroṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya māmutalmatikkulam viḷakkiya

nivantaṅkaḷukkut teṉkallakanāṭṭu aḻiciṟai [śrīka]raṇamaṅkalam paṇṭuṭ

iṭṭa aḻi[ci]ṟaiyāṉa śrīkara[ṇa]maṅkalattukku ellai kiḻellai uṭaiyā-

yār tevatāṉam poṉṉamaṅkalattu melellaikku meṟum
tiṟalār melellaikku [te]ṟkum uṭaiyār tiruppūvaṇa muṭaiya nā-
