kiḻellaikku kiḻakkum vaṭavellai uṭaiyār tirumuṭaṅkai nāyaṉār tevatāṉam
karu-

māttūr teṉellaikkut teṟkum vaṭavellai uṭaiyār tiruvālavāyuṭaiya
nāyaṉār tevatāṉam vaḷḷaikkuḷattu teṉellaikku teṟkum iṉṉāṉkellaikku

kārāṇmai mitāṭciyum antarāyamu[ṭ]paṭa iṟaiyili tevatāṉamāka yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 470.
(A.R. No. 684 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkottai Taluk, Madura District.
On the east wall of the first prakara, left of entrance, of the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Māṟavarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya I, Year 8 = 1224 A.D.

inscription.p:
At the request of the officer Maḻavarāyaṉ the king made the village Tōḍaṉēri as tax-free
dēvadāna to the temple of Tiruvēḍagam, excluding the lands given earlier as udirappaṭṭi,
paḷḷichchandam and dēvadāna. The granted village is said to be a chēvaga-jīvita-muṟṟūṭṭu village in
Pāgaṉūr-kūṟṟam. Many officers have signed the document.

mā[ṅ]-

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. 685 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkoṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On a stone built into the east wall of the second prakara of the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Maravarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya I (?), Year 2 = 1218 A.D.

inscription.p:
Fragment. Seems to be some decision of the temple trustees granting something to one
Āṭkoṇḍadēvaṉ Chīrāḷadēvaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(A.R. No. 686 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkoṭṭai Taluk, Madura District.
On a stone built into the south wall of the second prakara of the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at
Tiruvēḍagam.
inscription.p: Fragment. Some decision of the temple authorities.

inscription.head:
No. 473.
(A.R. No. 687 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkottai Taluk, Madura District.
On a stone built into the north wall of the second prakara of the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at
Tiruvēḍagam.
inscription.p:
Fragment. Appears to refer to a Vishṇu temple and the uncle (māmaḍi) of the king, whose name
is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 474.
(A.R. No. 688 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkottai Taluk, Madura District.
On a pillar near the second prakara of the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.
inscription.p:
Partially damaged. Mentions that the pillars, staircase, etc were made by a Mūvēndavēḷān,
the accountant of the village sabhā.

l.note: The right ends of linesare lost. The letters inbrackets at the end are restored from the context.

inscription.head:
No. 475.
(A.R. No. 688A of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkottai Taluk, Madura District.
On several stones built into the north wall of the second prakara of the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at
Tiruvēḍagam.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Kulasekara-Pāṇḍya, Year 13 = c. 13th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Mutilated. Seems to record a royal order, communicated by the officer Maḻavarāyaṉ to the
sabhā of Chōḻakulāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, relating to some tax concession.

inscription.head:
No. 476.
(A.R. No. 689 of 1905.)
Tiruvēḍagam, Nilakkottai Taluk, Madura District.
On a slab used as a seat by garland makers in the Patrikāparamēśvara temple at Tiruvēḍagam.
inscription.p:
Appears to record the gift of the slab used for garland-making (pūmālaikkal) by Koṉṉapūvaṉ alias
Teṉṉa-pallavaraiyaṉ alias Aṇukka-nambi.

inscription.head:
No. 477.
(A.R. No. 690 of 1905.)
Ramanathapuram Dindigal Taluk, Madura District.
On the rock at Ramanathapuram near Dindigul.
inscription.p:
Registers the building of a tank by Parāntakapaḷḷi-Vēḷān alias Nakkam Puḷḷaṉ, who
accompanied the king in his expedition (yāttirai) against Iḍavai in the Chōḻa country. This Puḷḷaṉ had
begun the excavation of an irrigation tank in his name, called Puḷḷaṉēri, strengthened the banks with
stone revetment and fixed the sluice therefor; but he died before the project could be completed. The
work was then completed by Puḷḷaṉ Nakkaṉ, evidently his son, with the help of the mason named
Vaḍugan-Kūṟṟaṉ and his son. Puḷḷa Nakkaṉ gifted to the masons some wet land in both the divisions of
Paḷḷi-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 26.]

l.ta: kkaṉ [|*] ṉatu ceyta taccaṉ vaṭukaṅkūṟṟaṉ [|*] a-
vaṉ makaṉ kuṟaippaṇi muṟṟu vikka puḷḷana-
kkaṉ avaṉukkuk kāṇiyāka aṭṭiṉa pūmip paḷ-

inscription.head:
No. 478.
(A.R. No. 691 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below the second image cut out at Aivarmalai.
In Vatteluttu character. Mentions Arampicci Varadhavani of Vadapalli did this image.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 107.]

inscription.head:
No. 479.
(A.R. No. 692 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below the fourth image cut out at Aivarmalai.
In Vatteluttu characters. Mentions Accanandhi made this image.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 108.]

inscription.head:
No. 480.
(A.R. No. 693 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below the sixth image cut out at Aivarmalai.
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. The senior pupil Chētiyar did this image.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 109.]

inscription.head:
No. 481.
(A.R. No. 694 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below the seventh image cut out at Aivarmalai.
In Vatteluttu characters. Mentions Indrasena did this image.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 110.]

inscription.head:
No. 482.
(A.R. No. 695 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below the eighth image cut out at Aivarmalai.

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Mentions that one Kuḍiyan Vēntaṉ of Nālkūr in Veṇpu-nāḍu did this
image.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 111.]

inscription.head:
No. 483.
(A.R. No. 696 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below the ninth image cut out at Aivarmalai.
In Tamil characters. Mentions that one Pathamulattal did this image.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 112.]

inscription.head:
No. 484.
(A.R. No. 697 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below the tenth image cut out at Aivarmalai.

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Mentions that one Perumaḍai Mallisena-periyār of Vīra-saṅgam did this
image.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 113.]

inscription.head:
No. 485.
(A.R. No. 698 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below the eleventh image cut out at Aivarmalai.
In Vatteluttu characters. Mentions that one Venmathi did this image.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 114.]

inscription.head:
No. 486.
(A.R. No. 699 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Above the natural cave at Aivarmalai.
In early Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Mentions that one Kuṇmēkaṉ Kaṭṭitāmaṉ.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 115.]

inscription.head:
No. 487.
(A.R. No. 700 of 1905.)
Ayyampalayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Above the natural cave at Aivarmalai.

In early Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Fragment. Mentions a gift of gold to some deity (called . . śva-
baṭārar) by somebody from Koḻumam.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 116.]

inscription.head:
No. 488.
(A.R. No. 701 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Above the natural cave at Aivarmalai.

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Records that Avvaṇanti-Kurattiyār, a disciple of Paṭṭinik-Kurattiyār
from Perumpattiyūr made the deity (dēvar).
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 117.]

inscription.head:
No. 489.
(A.R. No. 702 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Above the natural cave at Aivarmalai.

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Records an endownment of two kalañju of gold by some goldsmiths to
provide offerings (as avipuṟam) to the deity (dēvar) at Ayiraimalai.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 118.]

inscription.head:
No. 490.
(A.R. No. 703 of 1905.)
Ayyampalayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Above the natural cave at Aivarmalai.

In Tamil characters. Fragment. Records endowment of two measures of paddy (?) by a lady for
some offerings. [Published in SII, XIV, No. 119.]

inscription.head:
No. 491.
(A.R. No. 704 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Above the natural cave at Aivarmalai.

In late Tamil characters. Refers to the engraving of the conch and wheel by an individual of
Pāppaṇampaṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 492.
(A.R. No. 705 of 1905.)
Ayyampāḷayam, Palani Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Above the natural cave at Aivarmalai.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Varaguṇa, Year 8 and Śaka, Year 792 = 870 A.D.

inscription.p:
Vaṭṭeḻuttu. Records that Santivīrakuravar, pupil of Kunavīrakkuravaḍigaḷ, renovated the images
of Pāriśvapaḍārar and yakshis at Tiruvayirai and gave an endowment of 505 kāṇam for two continuous
offerings (muṭṭāavi) and for feeding an ascetic.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 22.]

inscription.head:
No. 493.
(A.R. No. 706 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Vīranāraṇa Vīrakēraḷa, Year 11 = c. 11th century A.D.

inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Incomplete. Mentions one Chembiyaṉ Ciritoṅga-pallavaraiyaṉ was asked
by Tikai-āyirattiaññūṟṟuvar (guild) to make some gift in the temple at Tiruvirālūr in expiation of his
causing the death of a Vaḷañjiyaṉ at Iravi-maṅgalam.

nicati iruvar

No. 494.
(A.R. No. 707 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Vīranāraṇa Vīrakēraḷa, Year 2+6 = c. 11th century A.D.

inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Fragment, continuation of lines 1-5 are lost. Mentions Kōdaimangalam
and Amarabhujamga.

On the north wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Vīrakēraḷa Amarabhujamgavarman, Year 23 = c. 11th century A.D.

inscription.p: In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Mutilated. Mentions Naraiyanurnadu.

inscription.head:
No. 497.
(A.R. No. 710 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Koṅgu Adhirāja Rājarāja, Year 4 = c. 11th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Built in the middle. Records a gift by Kaṇṇaṉ Uḍainaṅgai alias Villavaṉ-kiḻāṉaḍi, the queen of
Atijayachōḻa alias Adhirājarājadeva.

inscription.head:
No. 498.
(A.R. No. 711 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Vīrakēraḷa Adhirāja Rājarājadeva, Year 40 = c. 11th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Mutilated. Refers to the sale of land by the ūrār of Chatturukāla-nallūr in Vaiyāvi-nāḍu to a
Veḷḷāḷa called Vīranārāyanan alias Mūlabalattu-paḍai-vaḷantāṉ alias Chembiyaṉ Kāmiṇḍaṉ, a resident of
Kōtaimaṅgalam alias Maḍigai-taḷam. The land is meant for conducting the Māsi-magam festival. The
latter is also said to have built the verandah hall in the temple of Tiruvarāl-paramēśvarar.

inscription.head:
No. 499.
(A.R. No. 712 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the north wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Pāṇḍya, Jaṭāvarman Śrivallabha = c. 14th century A.D.

inscription.p:
Mutilated. Seems to be a king's order to the grāma and ūr in Vaikāvūr-nāḍu regarding some tax
remission (puravilekaḻittu).

inscription.head:
No. 500.
(A.R. No. 713 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Kongu, Vīrakēraḷa Amarabhujamgavarman, Year 28 = c.11th century A.D.

inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttucharacters. Mutilated at the end. Records a sale of land by the sabhā of some
Chaturvēdimaṅgalam to Sundarachōḻa-māyilaṭṭi, a native of Kāṭṭagam in Puṟangarambai-nāḍu. The latter
seems to have gifted the land to the temple of Tiruvarāl-ūr. The gift was to be protected by the army
people mūlabalattār and agrattoṭi-paḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 501.
(A.R. No. 714 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Kongu, Viranāraṇa Vīrakēraḷa, Year 29 = 11th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttucharacters. Gift of a gold ornament for the merit of a soldier (chēvakaṉ) called
Tēvātirāchaṉ by the army people called Mūlabala Eḻunūṟu-koṟṟavāḷār.

inscription.head:
No. 502.
(A.R. No. 715 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Kongu, Vīranāraṇa Vīrakēraḷa, Year 6 = 11th Century A.D.

inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Mutilated. Mentions Aḍivāḷa-nāḍu in Vēsālippāḍi on the north bank and the
temple of Mahādēva in Tiruvarālūr.

No. 503.
(A.R. No. 716 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Kongu, Vīranāraṇa Amarabhujanga = c.11th Century A.D.

inscription.p: In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Much damaged. Some land gift to the temple at Chēlūr in Aṇḍa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 504.
(A.R. No. 717 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.

p.b: Koṅgu, Parakēsarivarman Perumāḷ Virachōḻadeva = c. 11th century A.D.

inscription.p:
In Tamil characters. Mutilated. The king is said to have reigned over the two Kongustogether.
Seems to be a gift by the king to the temple of Paramēchuramuḍaiyār at Iravār in Vaigāvi-nāḍu.

No. 505.
(A.R. No. 718 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.
inscription.p: In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Mutilated. A sabhā and some land boundaries are mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 506.
(A.R. No. 719 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.
inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Much damaged. The god of Tiruvarālūr and Kōdaimaṅgalam are
mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 507.
(A.R. No. 720 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the south wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.
inscription.p: In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Much damaged. Relates to some tax remission.

inscription.head:
No. 508.
(A.R. No. 721 of 1905.)
Paḻani, Paḻani Taluk, Madurai District.
On the west wall of the Periyāvuḍaiyār temple near Paḻani.
inscription.p:
Gift of land. This is the king's order to the sabhā and ūrār of Kōdaimangalam alias Ellāchōḻa-
chaturvēdimangalam in Vaigāvūr-nāḍu regarding his gift of a kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadāna to the temple of
Iruvirāl-Paramēśvaram-Uḍaiya-Nāyanār. Refers to the deities Subramaṇya-piḷḷaiyār and his consort set
up respectively by Nallammai and Vikrama-Pāṇḍya-Ganganārāyaṇa-Chakravartikaḷ.
[Published in S. Raju, Paḻani Varalāṟṟu Āvaṇaṅgaḷ, 2015, p. 39.]

inscription.head:
No. 509.
(A.R. No. 722 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Above the first three images, first row, on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam,

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Much damaged and refers to some Tīrttappaḷḷi, the Jaina temple, and
Koṟkai in Kuḍa-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 69.]

edition.p:
[AR. 723 to 731 of 1905 Below the same images. Vaṭṭeḻuttu. Mention Venbaikudi-nadu (No. 723),
Arattanemipperiyar, pupil of Attopavasigal (No. 725). Sengudi-nadu (Nos. 728 and 731) and Ajjanandi (No.
729).]

[The subjoined Vaṭṭeḻuttu inscriptions No. 510 to 518 are engraved in characters attributable to about the
end of 9th centuryand to the beginning of 10th century A.D. below the images of the Jaina deities sculptured in low
relief on the face of the rock. Some of them are very much worn out while three of them are legible.].

inscription.head:
No. 510.
(A.R. No. 723 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam.

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Mentions some individual of Iḷaveṇbai in Veṇbaikkuḍi-nāḍu did
something.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 120.]

inscription.head:
No. 511.
(A.R. No. 724 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam, .

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Mentions the disciple (māṇākkar) of Chandraprapaṉ, of Tirumaḍam-
paḷḷi in Kuṟaṇḍi in Veṇbu-nāḍu.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 121.]

inscription.head:
No. 512.
(A.R. No. 725 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam, .
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. States that Araṭṭaṉēmip-periyār, the disciple of Aṭṭōpavaṇikaḷ made it.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 123.]

inscription.head:
No. 513.
(A.R. No. 726 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam, .
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Damaged. Veṇbu-nāḍu seems to be mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 514.
(A.R. No. 727 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam, .
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Damaged. The place Cheṅguḍi is legible.

inscription.head:
No. 515.
(A.R. No. 728 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam, .
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Damaged. Only Cheṅguḍi-nāḍu is legible.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 125.]

inscription.head:
No. 516.
(A.R. No. 729 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam, .
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Says that it is the gift of Achchaṇandi.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 126.]

inscription.head:
No. 517.
(A.R. No. 730 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam. .
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. The place name Vāḻaipaṭṭi is legible.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 127.]

inscription.head:
No. 518.
(A.R. No. 731 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
inscription.p: Below one of the images on the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam, .
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Much damaged. Seems to mention the making of the image.

inscription.head:
No. 519.
(A.R. No. 732 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
On a boulder near the Karuppannasami rock at Uttamapāḷaiyam, .
inscription.p:
In Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. Damaged. Records a gift of money by Anantavīra-aḍigaḷ for a lamp to
(the Jaina shrine of) Tirukkuṇagiridēva. The trustees of the Paḷḷi were asked to protect the gift.
[Published in SII, XIV, No. 128.]

l.ta: ta vīra aṭikaḷ aṭṭiṉa kācu patino-
ṉṟu ikkāciṉ policai koṇṭu tevarkku muṭ-

inscription.head:
No. 520.
(A.R. No. 733 of 1905.)
Uttamapāḷaiyam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District.
On a slab set up at the main entrance into the Kāḷahastīśvara temple in Uttamapāḷaiyam,

p.b: Madurai Nāyaka. Rāṇi Maṅgammāḷ, Kaliyuga 4794, Śaka 1615, Srimukha = 1693 A.D.

inscription.p:
Gift of land by a number of people including Vaḍamalaiyappa-piḷḷai, the executive agent of
Rāma-nāyakkarayyan.

inscription.head:
No. 521.
(A.R. No. 734 of 1905.)
Chāvakkāḍ, Kerala state.
On a slab placed under a tree in the Deputy Tahasildar's office at Chāvakkāḍ.
inscription.p: c. 11th-12th century.

In Vaṭṭeḻuttu Characters. Damaged in the middle. Mentions Vaḷḷuva-nāḍu in line 5 and Palaiyur
in line 14.
Text is much illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 522.
(A.R. No. 735 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p: Chōḻa, Parāntaka I, Year 36, Kaliyuga 4044, = 943 A.D.

In Grantha and Tamil characters. Records that Veḷḷaṅkumaran, the Kerala general (perum-
paḍai-nāyagar) of Prince Rājāditya built in stone the Śiva temple at Tiru-muḍiyūr (Mauligrāma) on the
banks of the Peṇṇai river. Rājāditya is said to be the son of Parāntaka alias Vīrachōḻa-perumānaḍigaḷ.
Veḷḷaṅkumaran hailed from Nandikkaraip-puttūr in Malai-nāḍu.
[Published in Annual Report of Archaeological Survey of India, 1905-06, p. 182-84.]

inscription.head:
No. 523.
(A.R. No. 736 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the north wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.

p.b: Chōḻa, Āditya II, Year 4 = c. 968-9 A.D.

inscription.p:
The king is mentioned as Parakesarivarman, ‘who took the head of Vīra-Pāṇḍya’. Gift of a
lamp to the temple of Aṟṟuttaḷi-Mahādēvar at Tirumuḍiyūr in Tirumunaippāḍi. The donor was
Chivageṅga-palavaraiyan of Chemaṇaṅgūr on the south bank.

inscription.head:
No. 524.
(A.R. No. 737 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Sivalōkanātha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Sale of land by the sabhā of Tirumuḍiyūr in Muḍiyūr-nāḍuto the temple for fifty
kaḻañju.

inscription.head:
No. 525.
(A.R. No. 738 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p: Gift of sheep for a lamp by Muṉaiya-chūḷāmaṇip-pēraraiyaṉ of Chemaṇakkūr on the south bank.

inscription.head:
No. 526.
(A.R. No. 739 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p:
Gift of sheep for a lamp by Veḷḷaṅkumaran from Nandikkaraip-puttūr in Malai-nāḍu, who was
the army captain of Prince Rājāditya.

inscription.head:
No. 527.
(A.R. No. 740 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the west wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p: Gift of gold for a lamp by a native of Kōṭṭāṟu.

inscription.head:
No. 528.
(A.R. No. 741 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Some gift for the merit of Chōma (?), daughter of Tiruvaraṅgan, a kuḍippaḷḷi of
Muḍiyūr (alias) Śrīparāntaka-chaturvēdimangalam in Tirumunaippāḍi in Rājēntrachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 529.
(A.R. No. 742 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Gift of two trumpets (kāḷam) by Pakayan Māṉi of Pulikkā for the merit of his
grandfather.

inscription.head:
No. 530.
(A.R. No. 743 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of fifty silver plates (veḷḷittaḷikai) to the temple of Āṟṟuttaḷi-perumāṉ at
Tirumuḍiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi by Vaidumba Mahārājan Tiruvaiyan Śrīkaṇṭar.

inscription.head:
No. 531.
(A.R. No. 744 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p: Gift of 90 sheep for a lamp by the nāṭṭār of Tirumuṉaippāḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 532.
(A.R. No. 745 of 1905.)
Grāmam, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
On the south wall of the central shrine in the Sivalokanatha temple at Grāmam.
inscription.p:
Gift of 10 kaḷañju of gold for a lamp by certain servants (kōyiṟ-piḷḷaikaḷ) of prince Rājāditya to
the temple of Āṟṟuttaḷi-Mahādēvar. The gold was received by the ūrār of Kuchappāḍi in Muḍiyūr-nāḍu
for maintaining the lamp.
